chapters.title: Chapter 1541 Defeating The God Of Creation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1541 Defeating The God Of Creation

Chapter 1541 Defeating the God of Creation

1541 Defeating the God of Creation

“Y-you’re done with the ceremony…? That’s impossible! It’s only been several days since we started!” God of Creation didn’t even have to look at Tian Qiyuan’s workstation to know that he was farting.

Tian Qiyuan’s expression remained calm as he spoke, “You really don’t learn, do you? If you don’t believe me, take a look for yourself.”

He gestured at his completed treasures on display.

The God of Creation didn’t want to entertain Tian Qiyuan and his nonsense, but he had to take a look.

When he turned his head to Tian Qiyuan’s workstation, he could not see any materials. On Tian Qiyuan’s display were 100 Celestial-grade treasures, all of them following their own specifications.

Clang.

The God of Creation dropped his hammer from shock after seeing this.

He turned to look at the audience. They all wore expressions of defeat, a sorrowful aura hanging around them as if they were mourning a profound loss—his imminent death.

While the God of Creation lamented in silence, Tian Qiyuan spoke, “Even though it’s your loss, I won’t personally kill you, nor do I really care if you actually honor your own words. You can continue to live after this, but you will forever be known as a coward and slanderer for the rest of your life.”

Tian Qiyuan no longer paid attention to the God of Creation and turned to look at the Yao Tao, who was seated with several elders of the Ancient Anvil Association and said out loud, “Are you going to claim that I had cheated this competition, too? Are you going to slander my name and call me a fraud to cover up what you’ve done in the past?”

Yao Tao and the elders lowered their heads in shame, not daring to talk back. With their credibility lost and their slander now exposed to so many people, the Ancient Anvil Association was essentially finished.

Moreover, after Tian Qiyuan’s performance today, nobody would dare to offend him, who could churn out Celestial- grade treasures like candy.

The Celestial Emperor approached Tian Qiyuan once it became clear that the God of Creation had given up—not that it would have made a difference even if he continued.

“Congratulations on becoming the next God of Creation, Exalted Blacksmith.”

However, Tian Qiyuan shook his head and said, “I have no intention of accepting that position.”

Seeing the confused look on the Celestial Emperor’s face, he explained, “I came back as the Exalted Blacksmith to clear my name, that’s all. I have no intention of working as a blacksmith again.”

“No way! With your talents… it’d be an immense waste of talents!” Heaven and Earth Blacksmith suddenly stood up and exclaimed.

“Why did you become a blacksmith in the first place?” The Celestial Emperor asked.

Tian Qiyuan showed his sword and said, “I started my journey as a blacksmith to craft the perfect weapon, and I have achieved my ambition.”

The Celestial Emperor swallowed nervously. With the sword so close to him, he had a strong urge to grab it from Tian Qiyuan.

“What do you plan on doing now that you’ve retired? I could offer you a position within the Celestial Palace.” The Celestial Emperor said.

“I’m going to relax at home for a bit, hopefully with some company. Then, I’ll begin my next project, whatever that may be.”

Tian Qiyuan proceeded to collect all of the treasures he’d crafted. Then, without giving the audience any more of his attention, he disappeared into the distance.

The audience remained silent even after Tian Qiyuan left. As for the God of Creation, he continued to sit before his anvil in silence, his thoughts unknown.

After leaving the Astral Holy Grounds, Tian Qiyuan returned to his own world.

Over the next several years, the world would not stop talking about Tian Qiyuan’s match against the God of Creation, who disappeared after their competition and vanished from the world, never to be seen or heard from again.

Nobody knew what happened to him. Some speculated that he’d killed himself in isolation, while others speculated that he chickened out and couldn’t kill himself and changed his identity.

As for the Ancient Anvil Association, they were disbanded when everyone lost faith in them. Many blacksmiths blamed the association for Tian Qiyuan’s early retirement and refused to collaborate with them. Countless blacksmiths tore up their contracts and discarded them outside the association’s headquarters.

The Ancient Anvil Association, one of the oldest establishments for blacksmiths, ended because they slandered a single man, and nobody felt sorry for them.

With the God of Creation gone and Tian Qiyuan refusing the title, the position became awkwardly vacant. Despite being qualified, neither the Star Forging Blacksmith nor the Relentless Blacksmith stepped forward to claim it.

When asked about it, they simply said they were not worthy of it, especially when Tian Qiyuan still existed.

In the end, people gave Tian Qiyuan a new title as the God of Refinement due to his ridiculous material refining speed.

As for Zi Xuan, it became clear to everyone that she had been right all along—Tian Qiyuan was indeed innocent. Although her methods had been extreme, few blamed her for her actions. The Unrivaled Nine Swords Sect publicly announced their willingness to welcome her back into the sect, but they never heard from Zi Xuan again.

Nine years after Tian Qiyuan defeated the God of Creation and cleared his name, he sensed a presence appearing inside his world.

He greeted the presence with a gentle smile, “It’s been a while, Zi Xuan.”

“I’m sorry to show up in this pathetic state…” she sighed, still in her soul form.

“It’s all good. I’m glad you’re okay. I was worried that I might never see you again.”

“Really? You don’t hate me? Even though I’ve done such horrible things?”

“What’s so horrible about trying to clear my name? So what if you’ve killed some people? They deserved it for slandering me.” Tian Qiyuan shrugged.

“Qiyuan… thank you…” Zi Xuan said in a trembling voice.f𝗿e𝗲𝘄𝗲bn𝐨𝚟𝚎l.com

chapters.title: Chapter 1542 Empyrean Overlord’S True Name
chapters.body:
Chapter 1542 Empyrean Overlord’S True Name

Chapter 1542 Empyrean Overlord’s True Name

1542 Empyrean Overlord’s True Name

“What’s your plan now?” Tian Qiyuan asked Zi Xuan sometime later.

“I don’t know yet, but I’ll let you know once I think of something. In the meantime, let me see your sword. I’ve been dying to take a look at it ever since I heard about it.” Zi Xuan said, her voice filled with enthusiasm.

“Of course.”

Tian Qiyuan retrieved his sword and held it before Zi Xuan.

“Oh…” freew𝚎bn𝐨𝘃𝐞𝗹.𝗰𝚘𝗺

Even without a body, Zi Xuan could feel her entire existence trembling from excitement.

Once her excitement calmed down a little, she noticed the sword emitting a strange aura, almost as though it was trying to charm her soul.

However, after examining it for a little longer, she felt that something was off about it, and she couldn’t pinpoint why she was feeling this way.

“It’s definitely an otherwordly sword without equal, and don’t misunderstand me for saying this,” Zi Xuan muttered after a moment, “but it feels like there’s something missing. It just doesn’t feel complete, somehow.”

Zi Xuan braced herself for Tian Qiyuan’s reprimand, but to her surprise, he simply smiled at her.

“If anyone could tell, I knew it would be you, Zi Xuan.” Tian Qiyuan chuckled.

Despite the multitude of admirers and expert blacksmiths who had seen the sword, none had noticed the subtle flaw that Zi Xuan pointed out.

“Huh? I was right?” Zi Xuan muttered in a dazed voice.

Tian Qiyuan nodded and said, “Indeed, you’re right. As you pointed out, while the sword is finished, it’s not truly complete. It’s missing one key element, but this element isn’t something that can be forged or crafted.”

“What do you think this element is? Take a guess.”

Zi Xuan stared at the sword in silence. However, no matter how much she thought about it, the answer eluded her.

“I can’t think of anything.”

“The answer is a soul.” Tian Qiyuan answered a moment later.

“A… soul? Like a human’s soul?”

“Although I said soul, it’s not the type you’re thinking about. It’s an extremely rare phenomenon, but did you know that given enough time and spiritual energy, natural plants and herbs can develop into sentient spirits with their own consciousness?” Tian Qiyuan asked.

“Yes, I am aware of that.”

“Treasures are the same. They, too, have the capability to form their own consciousness. When they do, they become Soul Weapons—a weapon with its own soul. However, not all treasures can become Soul Weapons. As for those that have the capability, one can only wait for them to turn into a Soul Weapon naturally.”

Tian Qiyuan then continued, “This sword… it’s an artificial Soul Weapon—a soulless one. While it doesn’t have a soul right now, it has the capability to house a soul and grow together with it.”

“An artificial Soul Weapon…? That’s crazy… but what advantage does it providethat a natural Soul Weapon cannot?”

“Well, for starters, once a natural Soul Weapon’s spirit dies, the weapon loses its powers alongside the spirit. As for my sword, it won’t lose its powers or be destroyed even without a spirit because it came into existence without one.”

“And since the sword won’t be destroyed without a spirit, we can always change out the spirit for whatever reason.”

Zi Xuan listened to Tian Qiyuan’s words as she stared at the beautiful sword.

“Would a human soul be compatible with it?” she suddenly asked.

“A human soul, huh? Since a spirit’s soul is almost indistinguishable from a human’s soul, I think it would work.” Tian Qiyuan said.

“I have decided what I want to do. Qiyuan, let me become this sword’s spirit,” she spoke in a serious tone.

“What?” Tian Qiyuan stared at her with wide eyes.

“The sword is incomplete right now because it doesn’t have a soul, right? Then, allow me to complete it. I have always wanted to become one with your swords, so this is exactly what I’ve been waiting for!”

“But you’re a human who spent most of your life living in this vast world… Are you really fine with throwing away all of that just to spend your life inside a sword as a spirit?” Tian Qiyuan hesitated to make Zi Xuan the spirit of his sword.

“I am. I can continue growing stronger with the sword, right? And it’s not like I have to spend the rest of my life inside the sword since it can continue to operate without a spirit.”

Tian Qiyuan sighed, “If that’s what you really want, I won’t stop you. And it’s true. If you ever change your mind, you can come out of the sword and return to living as a human.”

“Then it’s decided. I want to be together with you as your sword. This will fulfill both of my dreams at once, just like killing two birds with one stone.”

Zi Xuan always wanted to remain by Tian Qiyuan’s side, but her position within the Unrivaled Nine Swords Sect made that difficult. Now that she’ll be living inside his sword, she’ll be able to follow him wherever he goes.

“However, promise me something.” Zi Xuan suddenly said.

“What is it?”

“That you’ll never let another soul dwell inside this sword for as long as I exist.”

“As long as you wish to remain inside the sword, I promise that I won’t let anyone else take your spot.” Tian Qiyuan nodded.

“By the way, what’s this sword’s name?”

Tian Qiyuan smiled and said, “I thought you’d never ask.”

“Since it’s a treasure that stands above all treasures under heaven, I named it Number One Under Heaven.”

“Number One Under Heaven… What an arrogant yet fitting name. It’s perfect.”

Sometime later, Zi Xuan entered Number One Under Heaven, completing Tian Qiyuan’s masterpiece.

“…”

“…”

“…”

“I remember everything now, Zi Xuan!” Yuan exclaimed as the sword spirit’s aura reached its peak.

Zi Xuan’s body trembled after hearing her name, and her aura began to simmer down.

Yuan raised the Empyrean Overlord and mumbled to it, “I apologize for not remembering you sooner, Number One Under Heaven.”

Ding!

<You have learned Empyrean Overlord’s true name!>

<Empyrean Overlord has begun its true awakening>

<Progress: 10%>

chapters.title: Chapter 1543 Number One Under Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 1543 Number One Under Heaven

Chapter 1543 Number One Under Heaven

1543 Number One Under Heaven

“So you’ve remembered everything, huh?” Zi Xuan pointed her finger at him in a threatening manner.

“Not literally everything as Tian Qiyuan, but I remember how we met, about the God of Creation Ceremony, about being slandered and clearing my name, and how you became the spirit of Number One Under Heaven.”

“Then you remember our promise, right? That you’d never let another soul dwell within my sword for as long as I exist!” she growled, her anger palpable.

“Yes… I remember… and I’m sorry for breaking that promise. I have no excuse, even though I only recovered Tian Qiyuan’s memories just now. However, can you hear me out for a moment?”

“I’ll give you one minute to explain yourself.” Zi Xuan replied after a moment of silence.

“Thank you.” Yuan immediately explained the situation to her.

Sometime later, Zi Xuan spoke after grasping the situation, “I understand the situation now, but that doesn’t mean I have to accept it. You tossed them into your Soul Weapons so you could bring them out of the Shadow Realm, but now that they’re out of the Shadow Realm, they no longer have any reason to stay inside.”

“Now give me back my home before I go in there and remove you myself! I can’t promise you’ll be in one piece by the time I am finished!”

Knowing Zi Xuan’s character and how much Number One Under Heaven meant to her, he had no doubt that she would actually carry out her own threat.

Yuan quickly pondered for a solution. Since the Soul Weapon could only house one soul at a time and he didn’t have another unoccupied Soul Weapon, there was only one place for Yu Ning to stay.

“It’s okay, Young Master. I will come out. Since we’re outside the Shadow Realm, it’s true that I no longer need to stay inside, and this is her home, after all.” Yu Ning’s voice suddenly said.

And without waiting for Yuan to respond, Yu Ning emerged from Number One Under Heaven.

Unlike Zi Xuan, who was in perfect shape and looked like an actual human, Yu Ning’s soul resembled a small, ethereal cloud.

“Hmph!”

Zi Xuan immediately entered Number One Under Heaven.

Ding!

<Sword Goddess Zi Xuan has entered Empyrean Overlord>

<Sword Goddess Zi Xuan has reconnected her bond with Empyrean Overlord>

<After reuniting with its spirit, Sword Goddess Zi Xuan, Empyrean Overlord’s awakening has accelerated significantly>

<Progress: 100%>

Ding!

<Empyrean Overlord has fully awakened its true form!>

<Empyrean Overlord has changed name, becoming Number One Under Heaven>

Ding!

<Due to your insufficient cultivation, you will not be able to utilize Number One Under Heaven at its full capacity>

<For the safety of the user, Zi Xuan had placed restrictions upon Number One Under Heaven, reducing its power significantly>

[Number One Under Heaven]

[Level: Max]

[Rank: Soul Weapon]

[Growth Rate: ???]

[Requirement: Can only be wielded by Player Yuan]

[Description: The God of Refinement’s masterpiece. It is the pinnacle of swords and treasures with unfathomable prowess.]

[Unique Ability(weakened): When wielding Number One Under Heaven, all sword techniques will have their power amplified by 100,000%.]

[Unique Ability(2): Constantly devours the Qi of your targets after a successful hit, replenishing your own Qi.]

[Unique Ability(3): Number One Under Heaven’s size and weight can be altered according to the user’s desire. Qi consumption increases based on the size and weight gained.]

[Unique Ability(4): When Number One Under Heaven is out, all swords in the vicinity will have their powers sealed.]

[Unique Ability(5): Sealed]

“…”

“…”

[Unique Ability(9): Sealed]

Five out of nine of Number One Under Heaven’s unique abilities were inaccessible due to the restriction.

“I’ve been meaning to say this for a while now, but what’s with your pathetic cultivation base? You won’t be able to use me at my full potential at your current level. It’s a miracle you managed to come this far inside the Vanishing Valley.” Zi Xuan’s voice resounded from the sword.

“I know. I’m working on it.”

“Hmph. If I hadn’t restricted most of Number One Under Heaven’s power before it fully awakened, your body would’ve exploded just from touching it. Anyway, do you remember how to use me?”

“I do, but right now…”

Yuan turned to look at Yu Ning and said, “You can stay inside my Dantian.”

“What? Are you insane?” Zi Xuan responded before Yu Ning could. “How can you let a stranger enter your Dantian so easily? If she wants to destroy your Dantian, there’s nothing you can do to stop her.”

Due to a Dantian’s unique properties, it was possible to let living beings reside within them. Xiao Hua and Yuan’s other servants were perfect examples of this phenomenon, as they would stay inside his Dantian whenever they entered his body.

However, unlike Xiao Hua and the others who couldn’t harm Yuan due to their master and servant status, Yu Ning, who was not bound by any contract, posed a different story. This made her presence within the Dantian potentially dangerous, as she could destroy his Dantian while inside.

“Even though I haven’t known Yu Ning for long, I trust her enough to let her stay within my Dantian,” Yuan said with a calm smile.

“Young Master… to go through such lengths for me… I am eternally grateful,” Yu Ning spoke, her voice trembling with emotion.

And she continued, “With heaven as my witness, I swear upon my soul that I will never harm you for as long as I live, or may the heavens shatter my soul!”

A profound aura enveloped Yu Ning’s soul as she finished speaking, signifying that her oath had been heard and was now in effect. If Yu Ning were to harm Yuan from this moment forth, her soul would be shattered.

“You didn’t need to do that, but thank you for doing so,” Yuan said.

“Once I regain my body, I will definitely serve you properly, Young Master.” Yu Ning said as she entered his Dantian.

chapters.title: Chapter 1544 Zi Xuan’S Stash
chapters.body:
Chapter 1544 Zi Xuan’S Stash

Chapter 1544 Zi Xuan’s Stash

1544 Zi Xuan’s Stash

“Now then… Zi Xuan, let’s talk about your situation. Why are you staying in the Vanishing Valley, possessing that Giant’s Sword?” Yuan turned his focus to Zi Xuan now that Yu Ning’s issue was resolved.

“Good question. Unfortunately, I do not know. Or, more precisely, I don’t remember. When I woke up, I found myself already inside the Vanishing Valley, and not wanting my soul to degrade, I had to possess a treasure, and this Giant’s Sword happened to be nearby.”

“You don’t remember? Then do you remember what you did before losing consciousness?”

“I have a faint recollection of engaging in a battle, but the specifics elude me. I can’t recall the circumstances that led to the fight or who or what I was up against,” she confessed.

“Is that so…” Yuan mumbled with a pondering face.

“Oh, but I do remember you telling me something right before I lost consciousness. ‘Wait for me’, you said, and so I did… for countless years.”

“Sorry for making you wait so long…”

“You can make it up to me with some intense sex later,” Zi Xuan casually said.

“…”

Xiao Hua lifted an eyebrow at Zi Xuan’s words.

Yu Ning wondered to herself, ‘A sword spirit having intercourse with a human? Is that even possible?’

Unbeknownst to Yu Ning, Zi Xuan was originally human. However, after spending countless years as a sword spirit, her soul experienced a profound transformation that turned her into a hybrid of sorts, the first of its kind.

“Then did the previous ‘me’ tell you anything about what I should do?” Yuan then asked.

“They always say the same time—just let fate take its course.”

“Of course… why did I even ask?” Yuan shook his head at his own foolishness.

“What about you, Zi Xuan? Do you have any goals or purpose at the moment?”

“I only have one purpose—to protect and serve you as your sword. Unfortunately, due to your insufficient cultivation base, I have to suppress my cultivation base and restrict Number One Under Heaven’s true potential, which will make protecting you a lot more difficult. However, unless you run into an immortal or something, there won’t be a problem.”

Yuan nodded, “Now that I’ve done everything I need to in here, let’s get out of this ominous place.”

“Wait. Before you leave, you should take the monster cores I have collected over the years from killing magical beasts that tried to challenge me. I put them right underneath the Giant’s Sword. They will benefit your cultivation greatly if you consume them.” Zi Xuan suddenly said.

“Okay.”

Yuan flew toward the Giant’s Sword.

“Did I hear that right? You can consume monster cores?” Yu Ning doubted her ears.

“Yes, I have a unique physique that allows me to do so.”

“Just what kind of existence are you, Young Master?” Yu Ning muttered in a dazed voice.

Sometime later, Yuan arrived at the base of the Giant’s Sword, where a mountain of monster cores was stashed out in the open. The sight of so many cores piled together was both awe-inspiring and intimidating.

Furthermore, more than half of these monster cores belonged to Immortal-level magical beasts.

“I won’t have to worry about raising my cultivation base for a while…” Yuan swallowed nervously.

Then he remembered that he was cursed by the Celestial Emperor, so these monster cores would lose 99 percent of their effects.

‘If it weren’t for the curse, my cultivation could probably shoot straight to True Immortal with this many monster cores and complete one of the requirements to unlock Heaven’s Supremacy…’ he sighed inwardly.

‘Speaking of Heaven’s Supremacy… Now that I have Number One Under Heaven, maybe I can defeat a Divine Emperor-level magical beast and get that task finished. Moreover, I have Immortal Monarch’s Dominion, which will surely make things easier.’

After putting away the monster cores into his spatial ring, Yuan immediately went around looking for a magical beast with a cultivation of Divine Emperor, which was exactly nine realms above Spirit King.

Zi Xuan continued chatting with him as he traveled.

“Hey, what’s your current life like? And what’s our situation looking like?”

Yuan proceeded to explain about his new life on Earth, the existence of Cultivation Online, and his current situation.

“Cultivation Online, huh? What an interesting concept the Celestial Emperor had crafted. I’m also interested in your world. Let me see it the next time you return.”

Yu Ning also listened with great interest. As for Xiao Hua, she couldn’t comprehend Yuan’s words, nor did she find anything weird about it. To her, it was akin to trying to comprehend an unknown scripture even though she understood the words themselves.

A few days later, Yuan encountered a magical beast at the first level of Divine Sovereign, which was stronger than his initial target. However, he figured that he’d try to defeat it.

He approached the magical beast in a calm manner and used Immortal Monarch’s Dominion on it.

“You are forbidden from moving until I say so.”

The magical beast’s movements immediately stopped. However, it quickly started moving again after a few seconds, albeit extremely slow and stiff.

‘Is my cultivation not strong enough to command them perfectly?’ Yuan wondered to himself.

He used Immortal Monarch’s Dominion on it again, resetting the effects.

Then, with Number One Under Heaven in his grasp, Yuan activated God of War’s Astral Arts before using Nine Supreme Swords.

With Number One Under Heaven’s Unique Ability, Nine Supreme Sword’s power increased by 100,000 percent.

With nine strikes in total, the first strike was powerful enough to kill a Divine Warrior instantly. The second strike could annihilate a Divine Master in one blow. The third strike could defeat a Divine Grandmaster, while the fourth strike was potent enough to kill a Divine Lord.

The Nine Supreme Sword’s fifth strike was powerful enough to obliterate a Divine King. However, it took the seventh strike to defeat a Divine Emperor.

As for a Divine Sovereign magical beast, the eighth strike heavily injured it, with the ninth and final strike killing it.

With a single technique that delivered nine consecutive attacks, each more powerful than the last, Yuan managed to defeat a magical beast ten realms above his own cultivation with relative ease.

chapters.title: Chapter 1545 Leaving Vanishing Valley
chapters.body:
Chapter 1545 Leaving Vanishing Valley

Chapter 1545 Leaving Vanishing Valley

1545 Leaving Vanishing Valley

Ding!

<You have achieved an impossible feat by defeating a Magical Beast that was at least nine realms above you!>

<You have acquired the title ‘Defying Logic With Overwhelming Strength’>

[Defying Logic With overwhelming Strength: All damage dealt to Magical Beasts will be increased by 1,000%]

Yuan looked at Number One Under Heaven after defeating the magical beast with ease.

“The difference between its unawakened form and awakened form is night and day. Their strength is simply incomparable. I haven’t felt this powerful since Heaven’s Supremacy…” he muttered to himself.

“You think it is powerful right now? Just wait until you can use its full potential. This isn’t even 10 percent of its true power.” Zi Xuan said.

“Not even ten percent?” Yu Ning swallowed nervously.

This was her first time witnessing anyone fight and defeat a magical beast ten realms above their own cultivation. Yet, Yuan managed to do it with relative ease, leaving her in awe of his godlike prowess.

“Since I am already here, I might as well test out Number One Under Heaven a little more,” Yuan said before going to find his next target.

Sometime later, he encountered a Divine Grandmaster-level magical beast with a body that was several times larger than an adult elephant.

After halting its movements with Immortal Monarch’s Dominion, Yuan activated Number One Under Heaven’s third Unique Ability.

Then, as though it was a balloon, Number One Under Heaven began growing in size until it was 150 meters long.

Since Number One Under Heaven was too large to hold, Yuan used Qi Manifestation to wield it, levitating it in the air.

However, despite its increased size, its weight remained the same, but that didn’t remain the same for long, as Yuan increased its weight until it was as heavy as a mountain of steel.

Then, he swung it at the magical beast, cleaving its large body cleanly in half.

After killing the magical beast, Yuan reverted the sword’s size but kept its weight the same. However, it still felt as light as a feather in his hand, as if the sword’s increased weight didn’t affect him.

Sometime later, Yuan found a magical beast only at the level of a Spirit Sovereign.

Without using Immortal Monarch’s Dominion on it, he struck its body with Number One Under Heaven without killing it and only injuring it with a superficial wound.

After inflicting the injury, Yuan could feel Number One Under Heaven absorbing the magical beast’s spiritual energy through the wound before replenishing his cultivation with the spiritual energy it absorbed.

‘It’s absorbing much more than I’d anticipated…’

Number One Under Heaven was absorbing enough spiritual energy to completely deplete a Spirit King’s cultivation base within seconds, causing them to suffer from cultivation deprivation.

“Just how many unique abilities does your sword have?” Yu Ning couldn’t help but ask.

A typical treasure would only have one unique ability, with the higher-quality ones having two. Even the most powerful Celestial-grade treasures in the world would not have more than three unique abilities.

“Number One Under Heaven has nine unique abilities.” Zi Xuan responded in a prideful tone.

“Nine?!” Yu Ning was shocked to learn this information.

“And it will learn more as it continues to evolve. Number One Under Heaven is a unique treasure that does not have a limit—

it’s truly a weapon with unlimited potential.”

“Is it a natural weapon, or was it crafted by someone?” Yu Ning asked out of curiosity.

“It was crafted by none other than the person you’re dwelling within, who was once known as the God of Refinement. Both his reputation and skill surpasses even the God of Creation.”

“That was in the past—in one of my past lives.” Yuan calmly said.

“Huh? How many past lives do you have?” Yu Ning was baffled to learn that he had more than one incredible incarnation.

“I don’t know the exact number. Do you, Zi Xuan?” Yuan turned to her.

“You rarely talked about your past, so no. However, while I don’t know much about the lives before Tian Qiyuan, I know he had reincarnated three more times afterward—not including your current one. They are Tian Xin, Tian Kai, and Tian Yingzhe.”

‘Tian Xin… That’s the Sword Emperor and the ‘Lord’. Tian Kai is the one who taught the Zither Goddess… Who is Tian Yingzhe?’

Yuan only recognized two of the three names provided by Zi Xuan.

Tian Xin was the identity of the Nameless Emperor, also known as the Sword Emperor. Tian Kai excelled in music and also taught the Zither Goddess. However, he didn’t recognize Tian Yingzhe at all.

‘He could be the Celestial Overlord’s founder or a brand new identity that I have not discovered yet…’ f𝗿e𝗲𝘄𝗲bn𝐨𝚟𝚎l.com

“What do you know about Tian Yingzhe?”

“Sorry, but I don’t remember anything besides his name. I have more memories missing besides the moment before I lost consciousness and appeared in the Vanishing Valley. However, I think I will remember everything soon since I’ve been regaining bits and pieces of my memories about two decades ago.” Zi Xuan said.

“It’s fine. We can learn a lot with just a name.”

Once he was satisfied with his testing, Yuan flew without stopping until he left the Vanishing Valley with Zi Xuan’s help, as his divine sense was not strong enough to see through the natural illusion that prevented people from leaving the place.

“It feels great to leave that place finally. I was beginning to wonder if I would ever leave.” Zi Xuan sighed once they were outside Vanishing Valley.

“I’m just glad I don’t have to deal with the Black Death anymore. That thing was a menace.”

“That thing? You can damage it with Supreme Sword Aura,” Zi Xuan suddenly revealed.

“I just learned Enhanced Sword Aura. It’ll be a while before I learn Supreme Sword Aura.”

“You can also damage it with Number One Under Heaven—once you unlock its Unique Ability that allows you to cut through anything, including Black Death. However, you’ll have to reach Spirit Enlightenment to unlock it.”

“Spirit Enlightenment, huh? Good to know.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1546 Returning To The Third Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 1546 Returning To The Third Heaven

Chapter 1546 Returning to the Third Heaven

1546 Returning to the Third Heaven

After leaving Vanishing Valley, Zi Xuan asked him, “What do you plan on doing now?”

“I’m going to descend to the Third Heaven for a while.”

“What’s happening down there?”

“Nothing. I’m just meeting up with a friend.”

“Is that so? Anyway, I’m going to slumber for a bit, as I haven’t been able to relax at home for countless years now. Don’t bring me out unless you’re in danger.”

“Alright. Get some good rest, Zi Xuan.” Yuan said before putting her away.

Even in its suppressed form, Number One Under Heaven was incredibly overpowered and definitely not necessary for the Fourth Heaven, let alone the Third Heaven.

“Welcome back, my liege.” Dong Ye’s voice suddenly resounded.

“It appears that you’ve reunited with Lady Zi and managed to awaken your sword’s true appearance.”

“Did you know Zi Xuan was in the Vanishing Valley this whole time?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“No, my liege. I was only aware that a powerful sword spirit existed within the Vanishing Valley. Although I had my suspicions, I could never confirm it.”

Yuan nodded, “Anyway, I’m going to make a quick trip to the Third Heaven. Watch over my friends while I am gone.”

“Naturally.”

Yuan made his way back to the Black Fog City for their teleportation device.

From there, he teleported to the city closest to the Stairway to Heaven.

Sometime later, Yuan arrived at the Stairway to Heaven.

Instead of immediately entering the Stairway to Heaven, Yuan admired it from outside.

“Stairway to Heaven…”

He vaguely recalled crafting the Stairway to Heaven as Tian Qiyuan to make traveling between the Nine Heavens more convenient.

Before the Stairway to Heaven came into existence, cultivators had to traverse the vast starry sky to travel between realms, rendering the notion of journeying from one heaven to another much less accessible.

Each journey, depending on one’s starting point and destination, could take years, if not decades. The arduous and perilous voyages deterred many, making travel between Heavens a rare and significant endeavor reserved for the powerful and privileged.

Realizing this, Tian Qiyuan crafted the Stairway to Heaven, allowing everyone the privilege of traversing the Nine Heavens.

In the beginning, the Stairway to Heaven was only used as a means of travel. However, nowadays, it has become a trial of sorts for those in the lower heavens who wish for a better and more challenging life in the upper heavens. Those who successfully ascend are often regarded with great respect and seen as worthy of the higher realms’ opportunities and challenges.

As for who decided to turn the Stairway to Heaven into a trial, it was the Celestial Emperor, who wanted to regulate travel within the Nine Heavens so the upper heavens were not overpopulated, allowing the natural resources there to remain abundant.

As for the lower heavens, while they may have fewer resources, they don’t have to worry about any Immortals wreaking havoc.

Tian Qiyuan had no problem letting the Celestial Emperor control the Stairway to Heaven, either. It was only natural to let the ruler of Nine Heavens have authority over such issues, and not doing so might spark another war.

Even if he handed control over to the Celestial Emperor, he was still the ultimate decision-maker as its creator, and he could regain full control whenever he wanted. Furthermore, he saw this as an opportunity to turn the Stairway to Heaven into a training ground for his future reincarnations.

Yuan entered the Stairway to Heaven after admiring his own creation for a minute.

“Welcome back, Master.” Tian’er greeted him inside.

“Are you already ascending to the Fifth Heaven?”

“No, I am going to visit the Third Heaven.”

“I understand. Please have a safe journey.” Tian’er didn’t ask any questions and created a staircase that went down instead of going up like usual.

“Thank you.”

Yuan didn’t say anything else and began his descent.

Typically, after receiving approval, one must pay the Lord of their respective Heaven a significant amount of resources before they can descend. The only exception is for those traveling under the orders of the Celestial Emperor, such as the envoys sent to the lower heavens to find Yuan.

Moreover, one would have their cultivation restricted to the limits of their destination to control the damage that could be done while there.

Fortunately, Yuan’s cultivation was still well within the limits of Third Heaven, so he didn’t need to worry about his cultivation being a problem.

An hour later, Yuan emerged from the Stairway to Heaven, arriving at the Third Heaven.

“I’ll be right back.” Yuan logged off shortly after to contact Bai Lianhua.

Since Yu Ning dwelled within Yuan’s Dantian, she was unable to travel to Earth and was forced to enter a slumbering state after he logged off. The same went for Xiao Hua.

“Yes, I’ve arrived in the Third Heaven. Meet me at the nearest Myriad of Techniques.”

“Okay, I’ll see you in a bit.”

Yuan returned to Cultivation Online the next moment.

“Looks like I’m unable to go to Earth with you while I am in your Dantian…”

“I guess it makes sense since I technically have two bodies, and you’re in my other body’s Dantian. Meanwhile, Soul Weapons are connected to one’s soul, so I can bring it out no matter which body I am using.”

“The Spirit Avatar Array and the Soul Transference Array, huh? They were classified as level 8 formations during my time, but even I cannot perform them despite being a level 8 Array Master, so they’re definitely level 9 formations. I cannot imagine how powerful the Array Master who performed these two formations on such a massive scale may be, but they’re definitely powerful enough to be the God of Formations.”

“The God of Formations, huh? What does it take to become one?”

“Well, while the position of God of Formation existed during my time, nobody was actually good enough to claim that title. To even have the chance to be the God of Formation, you need to be able to create a level 9 formation that was considered the most complex and difficult formation in existence—the Chaotic Heaven and Earth Formation. It’s an unfathomably powerful formation that has flawless offensive and defensive capabilities.”

“The Chaotic Heaven and Earth Formation… I have heard of it. It was used by the first Celestial Emperor when I fought him as the Evil God, and it was very annoying to deal with.” Yuan casually muttered.

“Huh? Excuse me? What did you just say?”

“That I fought the first Celestial Emperor as the Evil God? Oh, right. I didn’t tell you guys yet.”

“Y-y-you were the Evil God in one of your previous lives?!?!?! Why didn’t you say this sooner!?” Yu Ning exclaimed in a shocked voice.

“Well, now you know.” Yuan smiled.

Yu Ning fell silent the next moment, almost as though she was trying to figure out how she should react to this shocking revelation.

Yuan made it all the way to the Myriad of Techniques in complete silence.

Bai Lianhua was standing outside when he arrived.

“Sorry, did you wait long?”

Bai Lianhua shook her head and smiled, “No, I just got here myself.”

“Let’s go inside.”

Once inside, Yuan brought her straight to the highest floor and said, “Alright, go pick whatever techniques you want.”

“Are you sure about this?” she asked, still hesitant to accept such a gift from him.

“What are you saying now? Go and get some techniques for your faction. Also, you can take your time. I’ll also be looking around.”

“Okay…”

Bai Lianhua left the next moment to wander the place while Yuan also went to check out the techniques. While he doesn’t need any for himself, he figured that he might as well acquire a few for his faction.

Yet, even after looking through the entire floor, Yuan was unable to find any decent techniques. Although most people would consider the techniques at Myriad of Techniques above standard, they were merely so-so in Yuan’s eyes.

Suddenly, a voice resounded inside his head.

“I see that you’ve returned from the Vanishing Valley in one piece, Yuan. I’m relieved. Anyway, can I have a moment of your time right now? I have some urgent information for you.”Senior Bai’s voice resounded.

“I’ll be right there.”

Yuan went to find Bai Lianhua to let her know that he’d be gone for a bit before going to Senior Bai’s world.

“What’s wrong, Senior Bai?” Yuan asked after meeting up with him.

“This is about the escaped Immortals. Do you remember the third Immortal who managed to escape into the lower heavens? Well, we found the Immortal in the Fourth Heaven, where you currently are.”

“The Fourth Heaven… so what’s going to happen now?”

“There are already experts chasing after this Immortal, but their hands are somewhat tied because they don’t want to cause too much damage in the Fourth Heaven.”

“This escaped Immortal… do they have their full cultivation?” Yuan asked.

“No, their cultivation will be suppressed because of the Heavenly Law. However, they won’t be fully suppressed, so this Immortal is still strong enough to wreak havoc within the Fourth Heaven.”

Yuan began pondering. Since this Immortal has their cultivation restricted, he might be able to finish the task that required him to defeat a Cultivator nine realms above his own.

“If I happen to run into this Immortal, I can kill them, right?” he asked.

“Uhh…” Senior Bai was left speechless by his question.

“You actually want to fight the Immortal? That’s reckless—

suicidal! I gave you that treasure to detect the Immortal’s presence not because I want you to fight them but to run away and call for backup instead!”

“I know, but if the Immortal is weak enough, I might be able to catch them off guard and kill it in one go,” Yuan responded calmly, despite talking about fighting an Immortal.

chapters.title: Chapter 1547 Tian Yingzhe
chapters.body:
Chapter 1547 Tian Yingzhe

Chapter 1547 Tian Yingzhe

1547 Tian Yingzhe

“I understand your immense talent and prowess, and I’ve witnessed the full force of your attacks. But, my dear little friend, these Immortals are not like anything you’ve encountered. They are a force beyond your wildest imagination, and they won’t simply stand there to be defeated. I implore you to reconsider your decision.” Senior Bai’s words carried a weight of urgency, hoping to sway Yuan’s determination.

“Don’t worry, Senior Bai. I’ve grown a lot stronger since my visit to the Vanishing Valley. I am also not as reckless as you think. If I deem the opponent too dangerous, I’ll run away.”

“And what if by the time you realize the danger, it’s already too late? It would be far wiser to avoid them altogether,” Senior Bai sighed, his disappointment evident as he contemplated Yuan’s unwavering resolve to face this immortal.

If anyone else were in his shoes, they wouldn’t even think of such a thing.

‘I guess this is what differentiates the true prodigies from the talented. They are truly incomprehensible beings. There is also a saying that the more talented an individual is, the more they tend to attract danger. Unfortunately, this is also why they tend to perish earlier than others despite having more potential.’

Normally, Senior Bai would stop trying to persuade Yuan to fight the Immortal, but knowing firsthand how dangerous the Immortals from the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto are, he continued trying.

“The criminals within the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto, the Immortals you’re so eager to fight, are arguably even more dangerous than the ones sealed within the Primordial Realm. They are also much harder to kill than ordinary Immortals due to their unique physiques, such as the one within the Fourth Heaven, who has the Shrouded Physique, which makes their appearance impossible to perceive even with divine sense.”

“And because you cannot see their body, you won’t be able to predict their attacks or attack them effectively.”

“I see…” Yuan turned silent with a pondering face, but it wasn’t because he was reconsidering fighting the immortal. He was wondering how he could effectively deal with them.

“Anyway, thank you for warning me about the immortal. I’ll keep my eyes out for them. Also, I would like you to pass on a message to Lady Xu for me.”

Senior Bai narrowed his eyes at Yuan, wondering if he’d given up on fighting the immortal or the opposite.

“What’s the message?”

“Can you ask her if she knows the name Tian Yingzhe? That’s all.”

“Tian Yingzhe? Okay, I’ll let her know.”

“Then I’ll see you next time.”

“I’m going to warn you again, just in case. Don’t engage with the immortal! Just run away and notify me if you sense them nearby!”

“I understand.” Yuan nodded with a mysterious smile on his face.

Senior Bai still felt concerned for some reason.

Once Yuan left, he sighed out loud, “Alas… even there’s nothing I can do if he truly wants to fight the immortal…”

“Hm? Wait a minute. I can just have someone watch over him!”

With this idea in mind, Senior Bai left his world to contact someone from the Celestial Overlord.

Sometime later, Senior Bai arrived at a massive outdoor training ground that was as large as several major cities.

“This junior greets Overlord Bai.”A half-naked, tall, and robust man stopped his training and respectfully bowed to Senior Bai after noticing his presence.

“Sorry for the sudden visit, Kelan.”

“You’re always welcome, no matter the time and day.”

“Are you busy nowadays?” Senior Bai asked.

“It’s the same as usual.”

“Then, do you mind doing me a favor? I’d like you to watch over a junior of ours in case he does anything reckless.”

“A junior of ours? So he’s a member of the faction?”

“Yes, he joined very recently.”

“This junior must be very special for you to go out of your way to do this.” Kelan smiled.

“You have no idea. He was recruited by Celestial Overlord Xu herself.”

“What?! Celestial Overlord Xu recruited him?! Alright, I’ll take a look at this junior. Where is he?”

“Fourth Heaven.”

“Huh? But we’re currently in the Eighth Heaven.”

“I am aware.”

Kelan raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

“Oh, was this junior sent to the lower heavens to find a certain somebody?” Kelan suddenly came to this realization.

“No, this junior was recruited in the lower heavens. He’s only a Spirit King, so he’s a real junior.”

“N-no way…” Kelan wore a look of disbelief on his face.

When was the last time they had someone with such low cultivation amongst their ranks?

“Don’t look down on this junior, Kelan. Even though he’d only recently joined, he’s already a General.”

“Already a General?! That’s impossible!” Kelan cried out loud.

Senior Bai smiled, “If his rank had been lower, I would’ve asked someone else. You’re the only Commander with so much free time, too.”

“Please don’t make me sound like a slacker, Overlord Bai. Training is also working, and I am still healing from my injury.”

Senior Bai glanced at the obvious scar that covered Kelan’s arm and said, “I’m just joking with you. Anyway, can you do this favor for me? You’ll also be helping Celestial Overlord Xu since she cares about him, too.”

“Of course. You just need me to watch over him, right?”

“Yes, since he might pick a fight with one of the escaped immortals who was found in the Fourth Heaven.”

“A mere Spirit King daring to pick a fight with an escaped immortal from the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto? This kid’s got guts! We might get along pretty well.” Kelen laughed out loud at this information.

“His name is Yuan. Also, he’s from outside the Nine Heavens, if you know what I mean.”

“I know. The Lost Ones, right? Do you mind if I talk to him, or am I just supposed to tail him like a spy?”

“You can interact with him if you want.”

“Alright.”

“Don’t bully him too much, or you might catch a beating from Celestial Overlord Xu.” Senior Bai chuckled.

“When have I ever bullied a junior?” Kelan laughed out loud.

“Then I’ll make arrangements for your descent after I speak with Celestial Overlord Xu. You should prepare for your departure, too.”

“Understood. I’ll see you later, Overlord Bai.”

After Senior Bai left, Kelan smiled, “A Spirit King and a General, huh? I have high expectations, so don’t disappoint me, junior.”

After his meeting with Kelan, Senior Bai went to find Xu Jiaqi.

“It feels like I’ve been seeing you much often lately. Is this related to him?” Xu Jiaqi spoke the moment Senior Bai entered her office.

“Yes, it’s about Yuan. I wanted to let you know that I had sent Commander Kelan to watch over him.”

“Why?” Xu Jiaqi raised an eyebrow at this unexpected news.

“One of the escaped immortals was spotted in the Fourth Heaven, and when I warned him about it, he decided to fight them instead of running away. I advised him not to fight them, and even though he said he understood, I cannot help but doubt him.”

“Is that so? Fighting an escaped immortal, huh? If he somehow manages to defeat an immortal, our reputation would skyrocket.”

“You want him to fight the immortal?” Senior Bai stared at her with wide eyes.

“Of course not. I’m just saying. Anyway, you did the right thing. We cannot let someone as talented as him die in vain. Is that all?”

“There’s one more thing. Yuan asked me to pass a message on to you.”

After clearing his throat, Senior Bao repeated, “Do you know the name Tian Yingzhe?”

“…What?”

Xu Jiaqi’s expression froze after hearing this name.

Seeing her reaction, Senior Bai swallowed nervously.

After a moment of silence, Xu Jiaqi suddenly stood up, slammed the desk before her, and loudly inquired, “Where did he learn of this name?!”

“Uh… I don’t know. I didn’t ask since it seemed like an innocent question. Who is Tian Yingzhe? Should I be worried?”

Xu Jiaqi didn’t immediately respond and fell back onto her chair.

After another moment of silence, she sighed, “He… was the founder of the Celestial Overlord.”

“What?! The founder?! But I thought…”

Xu Jiaqi nodded and said, “The founder had two names. One was his true name, and the other was used for the Celestial Overlord. Even among the other Celestial Overlords, only I knew his true name.”

“Oh… then was it okay for you to have revealed it to me…?” Senior Bai asked.

“It’s fine. Since you would’ve learned it sooner or later after. However, how did Yuan learn of this name?”

“I’ll ask him the next time I see him.”

“Please do.” Xu Jiaqi nodded with a serious expression on her face.

“Also, although it’s just a name, keep it to yourself.”

“Of course. I won’t tell a soul.” Senior Bai nodded.

“Then what about your response?” he then asked.

“Just tell him what I told you.”

“I understand.”

Meanwhile, Yuan returned to Bai Lianhua’s side after leaving Senior Bai’s world.

“You’re really not being humble.” Yuan smiled when she saw the piles of techniques Bai Lianhua had picked out.

“Is this too much? I knew it, but…”

“No, it’s fine.” Yuan chuckled.

“Are you sure I can have all of this for free?”

“I’m certain.”

Once they were prepared, Bai Lianhua brought all of the techniques to the register, shocking the worker.

But before the worker could even open his mouth to speak, Yuan showed his General symbol, shocking the worker even more.

“Thank you for your service!” The worker bowed to Yuan.

Yuan nodded at him before turning to look at Bai Lianhua and speaking, “It’s done. You can put them away.”

“What? That’s all?” she stared at him with a gawking expression, as she had anticipated a much lengthier process.

“That’s all.” He confirmed with a smile.

chapters.title: Chapter 1548 Messing With The Qin Family
chapters.body:
Chapter 1548 Messing With The Qin Family

Chapter 1548 Messing With the Qin Family

1548 Messing With the Qin Family

After acquiring the techniques, they left the Myriad of Techniques. However, even after successfully leaving the store with the techniques, Bai Lianhua couldn’t believe that she’d managed to acquire so many powerful techniques for free.

Even if she had her family’s full backing, it would still be difficult for them to purchase this many rare and valuable techniques without severely damaging their finances. Yet, she was able to acquire them for free because of Yuan.

“How exactly did you manage to do that again?” she looked at him with wide eyes.

“Did you know that factions also exist in Cultivation Online? I am part of the faction that owns the Myriad of Technique, so it’s a member’s privilege…of sorts.”Yuan gave a brief explanation.

“Do you think I can also join your faction?” Bai Lianhua knew the answer even before she asked, but she still had to take her chances.

“Probably not, and I am not looking down on you when I say this, but their requirements are quite high, as they’re one of the top factions in the upper heavens. In fact, you won’t even meet them unless you ascend at least to the Sixth Heaven.”

Bai Lianhua showed a bittersweet smile and shook her head, “No, it’s fine. I completely understand. I am not foolish enough to think that I have even a fraction of your talents.”

“There’s no need to drag yourself down like this. You’re plenty talented. With enough experience and time, you’ll definitely qualify for my faction.”

Bai Lianhua nodded and said, “So, what are you going to do now? Return to the Fourth Heaven?”

“Not yet. I still have some business in Third Heaven. By the way, do you know of a Qin Family?” Yuan said.

“I know of several. Which one are you referring to?”

“The one close with the Walkers Family and the Gu Family.”

“Oh, yeah, I know of them. They’ve been quickly rising in reputation and power recently with the Walkers Family and Gu Family’s help, after all. Why are you asking about them? Did something happen between you guys?”

Yuan nodded, “I’m not directly involved, but they’ve been bullying the family of someone who’s very close to me, even going as far as to use poison.”

“Poison? What a nasty bunch… but I’m not surprised that someone closely affiliated with Fiery Queen has such a nature. What are you going to do with them?”

“I’m not sure yet. The family they’ve been targeting has been losing business because of the bullying, so I’m thinking about messing with their operations within Cultivation Online as a warning.”

“If you want, I can help you locate their businesses. It shouldn’t be too hard with my family’s resources.”

“Really? That’d be great.”

“Consider it my payment for the techniques—even though it’s nowhere enough to pay for everything.” Bai Lianhua showed a bashful smile.

She continued, “Then I’ll contact you once I have the information prepared. It shouldn’t take too long. A few hours at most.”

“Alright. By the way, take this.”

Yuan handed her a spatial ring filled with treasures that he had picked up but had no use for.

“T-this is a lot… There are even Divine-grade treasures inside! Are you sure about this, Yuan? The techniques are already more than enough…”

“Just take it.” He nodded.

“Thank you… really…”

Bai Lianhua logged off the next moment.

With nothing else to do, Yuan also decided to log off.

While he waited for Bai Lianhua to scout the Qin Family’s business, Yuan went to visit the Chu Family.

“I’ve decided to mess with the Qin Family’s business within Cultivation Online.”

“Mess with…? If you don’t mind me asking… how do you plan on doing that?” Chu Shijian asked.

“It depends on the kind of operation they have, but I’ll most likely end up destroying all of their business.” Yuan casually shrugged.

“Is that so…”

“By the way, any news from the Qin Family?” Yuan then asked.

“Well, they’ve figured out that we know about Chu Wuyang being their spy when they haven’t been able to contact him since we locked him up. Just yesterday, they threatened to completely destroy our business within Cultivation Online in a few days if we don’t obey them.”

“A few days, huh? Not before I destroy them in a few hours.” Yuan smiled.

“To do so much for us… I really don’t know how to thank you…” Chu Shijian sighed.

“You don’t need to thank me because I am not doing this for you. I am doing this for Lulu.” Yuan calmly corrected him.

“We still end up receiving your help, so you have my gratitude.”

Three hours after they logged off, Bai Lianhua contacted Yuan.

“The Qin Family has three major businesses in the Third Heaven. The first and most profitable business is their monster hunting business. They have about 50 different teams that specialize in monster hunting throughout Third Heaven, and you can identify them through the purple badge by their waist. Their strongest and highest-grossing team is in Night Wing City, most of them being Spirit Lords. While they don’t have a store, they typically meet up at the Purple Lizard Pavilion before going on their hunt.” After giving Yuan their location, Bai Lianhua proceeded to give him some of their names and their physical descriptions.

She continued, “Their second business, the dungeon hunting team, is currently staying at the Eight Point Hotel in Yun Yun City.”

“Their third business is a medicine store called Perfect Qin Medicine, which is also located within Night Wing City.”

“You’ve gathered so much information so quickly. I was told it wasn’t easy learning about another’s business without having insiders.” Yuan smiled.

Bai Lianhua chuckled, “Who in the Ten Great Families doesn’t have a spy or two? With how quickly the Qin Family has been growing, I’m sure there are many keeping eyes on them.”

“Makes sense. Then I’ll see you later.”

“Yes, see you later.”

After returning to Cultivation Online, Yuan made his way to Night Wing City while Bai Lianhua sorted out all of the treasures Yuan gave her.

Sometime later, Yuan arrived at the Purple Lizard Pavilion and, using his divine sense, looked at every single individual inside the pavilion.

“It must be them.”

Yuan quickly found the players who matched the description Bai Lianhua gave him.

He didn’t approach them and simply watched them from afar.

There were eleven players in their group, all of whom were between the sixth and ninth levels of Spirit Lord.

Although Yuan wanted to kill them immediately, he decided to mess with them a bit first.

Half an hour later, once they were prepared, the group of players left the city and entered the nearby wilderness, where magical beasts between the third and sixth levels of Spirit Lord roamed.

They quickly encountered their first magical beast. It was at the fifth level of Spirit Lord.

Yuan didn’t interfere and watched them hunt the magical beast together. Their movements and great teamwork indicated that they’d been working together for a long time.

It took them almost ten whole minutes to defeat the magical beast, but only five out of eleven had fought.

After defeating the magical beast, they stripped any valuable materials from the corpse before resuming their hunt.

For their next encounter, the other six that didn’t participate in the first encounter fought.

They rotated between two groups to save energy, as they planned on hunting until night.

However, during their third encounter, right as the group was able to defeat the seventh-level Spirit Lord magical beast, Yuan appeared out of thin air to steal their kill.

“What the—?!”

“Fuck! Who the hell are you?!”

Yuan was wearing a mask, so none of them could identify him. Moreover, Yuan had killed the magical beast so badly that even its materials could not be salvaged.

“Identify yourself!” The group demanded.

However, Yuan didn’t say anything and merely escaped from the scene, vanishing like a ghost.

“What the fuck was that about…?”

“I have no idea…”

“He was probably just some weirdo passing by. Let’s forget about him and continue our hunt.”

The group didn’t bother chasing after Yuan and resumed their hunt.

However, their fourth and fifth encounters had all been sabotaged by Yuan, who refused to explain anything and vanished as soon as he was done.

“God damn it! What the fuck is going on?!”

“Does that person have a grudge against us or something? He’s not even letting us collect the materials… I have never seen or heard of anything like this before…”

“That’s it! If he dares to show up again, we’re all going to attack him without warning! Keep your eyes out!” The leader of their group declared.

They encountered their sixth magical beast soon.

Once again, Yuan showed up at the end to claim the kill for himself.

The group of hunters was prepared this time and attacked him the moment he showed himself.

However, none of them had expected Yuan to block all of their attacks with ease, and before they could attack again, Yuan vanished into thin air.

“God fucking damn it! What the hell is that bastard’s problem?!?!”

“We’ve been out here for four hours and only managed to secure one kill!”

“He’s probably hiding his face because he’s afraid we might recognize him.”

“Let’s see how long he can keep this farce up! He must’ve used a considerable amount of spiritual energy to defeat the last few magical beasts. I doubt he’ll be able to continue this for long! He will eventually slip up, and that’s when we’ll get that bastard!”

“Yeah!”

The group continued their hunt, fueled by anger and the desire to catch Yuan.

chapters.title: Chapter 1549 Messing With The Qin Family(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1549 Messing With The Qin Family(2)

Chapter 1549 Messing With the Qin Family(2)

1549 Messing With the Qin Family(2)

Several hours have passed since Yuan started messing with the Qin Family’s strongest hunting group. They figured he would eventually run out of energy and mess up, but Yuan continued stealing their kills with seemingly no effort at all.

“Fuck! I’ve had enough! Get out here and face us like a man, you fucking coward!”

“Come out!”

“Get out here and fight me, you bastard!”

The group of players eventually had enough and started yelling out loud like a group of maniacs.

Seeing this, Yuan smiled inwardly, ‘I guess it’s about time I stop messing around and deal with them for real.’

With that in mind, he emerged from his hiding spot and appeared before them.

“You’ve finally appeared, you kill-stealing bastard! I have never seen anyone as shameless as you!”

“Let me give you a good beating!”

The group of players approached Yuan with enraged faces.

However, Yuan raised his hand and gestured for them to stop, “Wait a minute. I have some questions for you before I decide your fates.”

“And who the fuck are you to decide our fate?! Get him!” The leader of their group shouted.

But before any of them could move, Yuan released his suppressed cultivation base at the peak of Spirit King.

The pressure was so immense and unbearable for these Spirit Lords that they collapsed on the ground, unable to breathe.

“A-a Spirit King!”

“Please! Have mercy!”

Yuan retrieved his aura, allowing them to breathe again.

“I think you might have mistaken us for someone else. I don’t ever recall offending someone like you!”

Hearing such words, Yuan spoke, “So you guys aren’t working for the Qin Family?”

“Huh? This is about the Qin Family?”

He silently nodded.

“We do work for the Qin Family, but what’s that got to do with this?”

“I’ll keep this short, but I’m going to shut down the Qin Family’s business, so if you could do me a favor and just quit, I won’t make things difficult for you.”

“What?! This is too sudden! We can’t do that even if you threaten us!” One of them quickly said.

“Why are you targeting the Qin Family? They have a lot of influence nowadays, especially with the Ten Great Families’ help.”

“I am just giving them a taste of their own medicine. They dared to mess with the Chu Family’s business, so I am now messing with theirs.”

“The Chu Family…? Look here, we have no involvement in businesses outside hunting magical beasts. And we’re bound by a contract. If broken, we’d need to pay a huge fine, so we cannot quit even if you threaten us.”

“How much are these fines? I’ll pay for them.” Yuan said.

“You’ll pay for our fine? That’s ridiculous…”

“If not, I’ll give you a real excuse to not work anymore—by killing you until you lose your ability to cultivate.” He calmly threatened, leaving them speechless.

The players trembled in fear upon hearing Yuan’s words, which were definitely effective.

They exchanged glances with each other and, without uttering a word, nodded at each other.

“Alright, we’ll quit if you can pay for our fines. It’s a million dollars for each violation.”

If Yuan were truly willing to pay for their fines, it would definitely be better than getting killed and losing their ability to cultivate, as they would be able to change employers.

“A million each, right?” Yuan retrieved a random Heaven-

grade treasure and showed it to them.

Although Heaven-grade treasures have gone down significantly in price—at least compared to when Yuan sold the Frozen Jade Zither—as they’re becoming more accessible, they can still be fetched for over ten million dollars with ease.

“You can keep the change. Also, if I find out that any of you were involved with messing with the Chu Family, I’ll hunt you down not just in Cultivation Online but also on Earth, so this is your last chance to come clean.” Yuan said as he handed the treasure to the one who seemed to be the leader of the group.

“I swear we’re not involved! I don’t know about the other teams, but we’re definitely clean!”

Yuan nodded and said, “Also, if you’re still working for the Qin Family after this, you’ll die before you even realize it.”

“We wouldn’t dare!”

“Ah, before you leave, tell me about the other hunting teams.” Yuan then said.

“I have their schedule and hunting grounds all listed here.” The leader of the group handed Yuan a jade slip that contained all sorts of useful information that allowed him to hunt the others down more efficiently.

“Thank you.”

Yuan left shortly after.

While Yuan went hunting down the other teams, the group of 11 players immediately went to sell the Heaven-grade treasure on COPlayerAuctions. The treasure was sold in under one hour for 15 million dollars.

Over the next several days, Yuan traveled all over the Third Heaven and bribed all of the Qin Family’s monster-hunting teams to quit. Those who refused to accept his bribes were threatened with death, and the rude ones were beaten until they were on the verge of death, which only took a single punch from him.

When the Qin Family was suddenly told by their strongest and highest-earning monster-hunting team that they’d quit, the Qin Family was left baffled and confused.

They became even more bewildered when every member of the team paid the fine for breaking the contract.

When asked why, the team told the truth—that they were threatened by a powerful cultivator to quit working for the Qin Family or have their cultivations crippled.

Soon, more and more teams from their monster-hunting business stopped working with them.

“Fuck! Which bastard is responsible for this?! And why are they doing this?!”

Qin Mulan, the head of the Qin Family, slammed his table in anger, causing it to split in two.

“For it to happen right after Chu Wuyang was caught… I don’t think this is a coincidence.” Qin Long, his eldest son, said with a pondering expression on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1550 Messing With The Qin Family(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1550 Messing With The Qin Family(3)

Chapter 1550 Messing With the Qin Family(3)

1550 Messing With the Qin Family(3)

“So the Chu Family is involved in this, huh? But how did they acquire information on all of our monster-hunting teams?” Qin Mulan pondered out loud.

“I’d wager one of our own teams betrayed us, providing the Chu Family with the information they needed. But for them to resort to hiring a Spirit King, they must be quite desperate.” Qin Long said.

“Long’er, I’ll leave this troublemaker in your hands..” Qin Mulan said to his son.

“I will crush this bastard for daring to mess with my Qin Family!” Qin Long nodded with a cold expression on his face.

Meanwhile, after Yuan made all of the Qin Family’s monster- hunting teams quit, he made his way to Yun Yun City, where their dungeon-hunting team was residing in the Eight Point Hotel.

Due to how rare dungeons typically were, most businesses put most of their resources into one team.

The Qin Family’s dungeon team consisted of thirty players, all of whom were between Spirit Grandmaster and Spirit Lord.

When Yuan arrived, he used divine sense to scout the hotel and immediately found 20 players with the Qin Family’s identification token. According to the intel provided by some of the monster-hunting teams, all of the members of the dungeon team stayed at the same hotel.

“Are the others offline or scouting? Well, it doesn’t matter.” Yuan pondered what he should do with them.

Sometime later, he forced his way into one of the rooms through the window. Inside, four players were looking at a large map together.

“Who the fuck are you?!”

Yuan’s intrusion immediately alerted them because he made no effort to be stealthy.

They swiftly pulled out their weapons and pointed them at Yuan.

“Calm down; I’m not here to hurt you.” Yuan calmly raised his hands as if to show that he was harmless.

“Then why are you here?” One of them asked with a suspicious frown.

“I’m here to make you quit working for the Qin Family. I’ll even pay for whatever fine you may incur for breaking the contract.”

“What…? And why are you doing this?”

“I have some beef with the Qin Family, that’s all. So, what do you say?”

“I say… screw off!” The four players in the room suddenly pounced at Yuan with their weapons flying.

However, Yuan didn’t even bother dodging their attacks and used his body to block them.

“What the fuck?!”

The players were shocked when their weapons couldn’t penetrate Yuan’s skin, much less injure him. It felt like they were trying to cut metal with a butter knife.

“It’s pointless. You won’t be able to kill me even if I went to sleep right now. Anyway, I’ll give you one more chance to keep your life. Quit working for the Qin Family or die to me with a chance of becoming a cripple.”

Yuan stood before them like a domineering warlord, his aura so intense it made the players gasping for breath.

“W-we will quit…”

They eventually said.

“Alright. One more thing. Are you guys, by any chance, responsible for bullying the Chu Family?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“Huh? The Chu Family…?”

“N-no… we had nothing to do with that…”

“Right? I have no idea what you’re talking about.”

These players suddenly became nervous, and it was written all over their faces.

“Is that so?”

Yuan suddenly pulled out the Grail of Truth, which was already emitting a bright light.

“Sorry, but it looks like my offer will no longer apply to you anymore.”

“W-wha—”

Before any of them could even utter a single word, Yuan waved his sleeve with Sword Aura pouring out.

Three of the players immediately turned into a cloud of blood mist, while one of them only lost an arm.

Of course, he survived only because Yuan allowed him to live.

“Tell me, who else was involved with the Chu Family? Just the four of you, or the entire team?” Yuan asked him, who was kissing the floor with his buttocks, his face filled with fear as snot and tears flowed down his face.

“A-all of us! We were all paid a bonus by the Qin Family to mess with the Chu Family’s business!” The player immediately revealed everything.

The Grail of Truth had no reaction, meaning he was telling the truth.

“Is that so? Make sure to tell your buddies to quit, or I’ll come after you until you can no longer cultivate.” Yuan killed the last player after finishing his sentence.

He made his way to the next room shortly after.

“Who the hell are you?!”

There were five players in this room.

Yuan went straight to business and said, “I’m here to avenge the Chu Family. If you don’t quit working for the Qin Family after I kill you, I’ll continue to kill you until you lose your ability to cultivate. If you’re unlucky to be crippled after one death, that’s too bad.”

Before any of the players in the room could even respond, Yuan killed all of them with Sword Aura.

He continued doing this until every member of the Qin Family’s dungeon team was wiped out. However, there were several that weren’t at the hotel, so he waited a few hours for them to show up before moving to his next target.

After waiting for six hours, the rest of the team showed up at the hotel, and all of them were killed after being told to quit working for the Qin Family.

The Qin Family was immediately alerted when Yuan killed the first four players.

“What?! He killed all of them because they were involved with the Chu Family?! This confirms it! That bastard is avenging the Chu Family and targeting our businesses in Cultivation Online!” Qin Mulan slammed his desk that was just replaced, destroying it again.

“Long’er, how are you going to deal with this bastard?” he turned to look at Qin Long, who had a pondering face.

“Since he’s targetting our businesses, he’ll most definitely show up at our medicine store. I’ll wait for him there,” he said.

“How are you going to stop him? He’s a Spirit King!” Qin Mulan said with a frown.

“So what if he’s a Spirit King? I have with me a magic talisman that can instantly kill even a Spirit Emperor! A Spirit King is nothing before me!”Qin Long laughed out loud.

“Good!” Qin Mulan nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1551 Qin Long
chapters.body:
Chapter 1551 Qin Long

Chapter 1551 Qin Long

1551 Qin Long

After he met with his father, Qin Long immediately went to his family’s medicine store to wait for Yuan.

“Welcome, Young Master Qin. Are you looking for some medicine today?” The workers there greeted Qin Long.

“This place will be attacked soon. Close down the shop and get everyone out of here,” he said to them.

“What? Who would dare to attack us?” The workers there were shocked to hear this news.

They had the Ten Great Families’ backing. No sane player would dare to offend them, much less attack them.

“I don’t know, but they undoubtedly work for the Chu Family. Anyway, get out of here. Things will definitely get messy here.”

The workers quickly closed down the store and left, leaving Qin Long alone in the store.

Sometime later, Yuan arrived at Night Wing City once again. He used divine sense to inspect the place.

‘Only one guy, huh? Looks like he’s expecting me.’

The lone individual at the store had a confident and arrogant air around him as he sat at the entrance like a commander in battle.

After pondering for a moment, Yuan casually approached the store without hiding his presence.

Qin Long immediately noticed him due to his mask and said, “So, you’re the bastard that’s been trying to ruin my family’s business, huh? How much did the Chu Family pay you? I’ll triple it if you stab them in the back.”

“Who said anything about getting paid? I’m doing this without any payment.”

Qin Long frowned at this unexpected information and said, “Do you even know who you’re messing with? We have two of the Ten Great Families backing us. Once we figure out your identity, you can kiss your life goodbye.”

“Oh? Is that so? Then I’ll make it easy for you.” Yuan spoke as he reached for his mask and removed it, revealing his handsome face to Qin Long.

“Y-you! Player Yuan!” Qin Long immediately recognized him.

‘It’s been Player Yuan this entire time?! I should’ve realized this sooner!’ Qin Long nearly facepalmed.

Qin Long was aware that Chu Liuxiang had left the family to live with Yuan, so it should’ve been obvious that he would be the one messing with them when they said he was a Spirit King.

“Did Chu Liuxiang ask you to do this?” Qin Long asked.

“No, I am doing this because I owe the Chu Family. You must be from the Qin Family. What do you think you can achieve by yourself?”

“You’ll find out soon enough, bastard.” Qin Long sneered in disdain.

He’d always wanted to deal with Yuan for stealing Chu Liuxiang from him, but he couldn’t find a way to do so since Yuan lived inside the Dragon Spiral Mountain and barely interacted with other players in Cultivation Online, making it difficult to track him.

Yuan smiled and said, “You seem awfully confident. Is that thing in your pocket really that powerful?”

“!!!”

Qin Long’s expression immediately darkened after realizing Yuan was aware of his magic talisman.

“Judging by its spiritual energy, is it a magic talisman? The same one you gave to Chu Wuyang?” Yuan continued.

Qin Long immediately retrieved the magic talisman from his robe and pointed it at Yuan.

“So what if you know about it? It’s too late to run. I only need half a second to activate it, and then you’ll go boom.”

“Before I kill you, let me ask you something.”

Yuan calmly nodded with a carefree smile, seemingly unfazed by his threat.

“How far have you gotten with Chu Liuxiang?” he asked.

“You don’t need to concern yourself with our relationship, but if you really need to know, we’re very intimate with each other.”

Qin Long’s entire face twisted in anger, his face flushed red. If one’s glare could kill, his was definitely intensive enough to commit mass murder.

“Why are you looking at me like I stole your lover? She was mine to begin with. You were the one using underhanded tactics trying to steal her from me.” Yuan casually shrugged.

“Silence, you bastard! I’ve had my sights on her for years!”

“So what? You were never fated to be with her. Simple as that.”

“And you are fated to die here! Die for me, you bastard!”

Yuan could tell that Qin Long had begun pouring his spiritual energy into the magic talisman and activated Sword Aura.

Before Qin Long could fully activate the magic talisman, his entire arm was severed by Yuan’s Sword Aura.

“Ahh!”

Qin Long fell back while screaming.

“Think about our surroundings. Using such a powerful item in the middle of a city would be disastrous.” Yuan shook his head.

If they were not in a bustling city surrounded by people, Yuan would’ve let Qin Long use the magic talisman on him. Unfortunately, the magic talisman was simply too strong and had the capability to level the entire city.

Yuan collected the magic talisman and tossed it into his spatial ring.

He turned to look at Qin Long and said, “Any more tricks up your sleeve? If not, I’ll kill you now.”

“You’ll pay for this, Player Yuan!”

“I’ll be waiting.” Yuan casually flicked his arm, decapitating Qin Long with Sword Aura.

After killing Qin Long, Yuan destroyed the entire building before him so the Qin Family could no longer use it for business.

His action shocked everyone around him, but nobody dared to call for help.

By the time the city guards arrived, Yuan had long left the city.

‘Now that I’ve dealt with the Qin Family’s business, let’s wait a little to see how they’ll react.’

Yuan logged off for the day and notified the Chu Family of the situation.

“I’m sorry, father. I couldn’t even activate my magic talisman because he used some invisible skill.” Qin Long also notified his father of his failures.

“What happened to our store…?”

“By the time I returned, it had turned to rubble…” he sighed.

Bang!

Qin Mulan slammed his desk, destroying it.

A moment of silence filled the room before Qin Mulan spoke in a grim voice, “I’ll contact the Walkers Family and the Gu Family to see if there’s anything they can do about our situation.”

Qin Mulan contacted them shortly later.

“Player Yuan? Sorry, but there’s nothing we can do against him in Cultivation Online.” The Walkers Family refused to deal with Yuan while he was inside Cultivation Online. They knew that he was essentially impossible to defeat, and fighting him would only result in their loss.

The Gu Family, however, did not immediately refuse to help the Qin Family.

“If you can provide us with a solid plan to deal with Player Yuan, we will assist you.”

Thus, the Qin Family proceeded to spend the next several hours planning.

“Player Yuan is helping the Chu Family, right? Why don’t we just attack all of their businesses at once? There’s no way he could stop all of us at once.”

“The Chu Family have already changed their operations, and without Chu Wuyang, we won’t be able to locate them fast enough.”

“How will attacking the Chu Family help us defeat Player Yuan? That’d only enrage him even more!”

“Player Yuan is a Spirit King. There are probably fewer than 10 Spirit Kings, even amongst the top players. It’s impossible to defeat him.”

“How about we borrow the power of NPCs? There are plenty of Spirit King cultivators in the Third Heaven.”

“Actually, I know of a faction in the Third Heaven that specializes in assassinations.” Qin Long suddenly said.

“That’s great and all, but what if he’s already returned to the Fourth Heaven by then? Won’t we be wasting our money?”

Despite spending hours on it, the Qin Family could not find a solid plan to deal with Yuan. In the end, they could only wait for Yuan to leave the Third Heaven before they resumed their businesses.

Sometime later, Qin Long returned to Cultivation Online. Since he died inside Night Wing City, he respawned in the same city.
