“Fucking Player Yuan. I will definitely have my revenge the next time I see him!” Qin Long looked at the sky and cursed out loud.

“Oh? How are you going to do that?” A familiar voice suddenly resounded behind Qin Long, greatly startling him.

He swiftly turned around to see a familiar face standing behind him.

“P-Player Yuan?! Why are you here!?” he cried out loud.

“I heard someone calling my name, so I came here to see who it was,” Yuan said sarcastically.

He continued, “Anyway, I see that you still have your cultivation. How many times do you think I’ll have to kill you before you lose the ability to cultivate?”

“Y-you… you can’t be serious!” Qin Long had a terrified expression on his face after realizing what Yuan wanted to do to him.

‘He’s planning on spawn camping me?!’

Due to how respawning works in Cultivation Online, it was possible to trap someone within a city and kill them repeatedly. However, this was only done by the foulest players and was heavily frowned upon by the player base.

“Do you not value your reputation?! Once word gets out that you’re a spawn camper, your reputation will sink to the bottom!”

“Would they even believe such rumors?” Yuan smiled.

The next moment, Qin Long found himself staring at the death notification.

‘Fuck! I must get out of the city before he finds me again!’ Qin Long immediately ran for the teleportation device upon respawning in a random area within the city.

However, before he could even travel a block, Yuan appeared before him like a ghost.

“What?! How did you find me already?!” Qin Long cried out loud, his eyes filled with shock.

Yuan merely responded with a silent smile before killing him for the third time.

chapters.title: Chapter 1552 Vengeful Slaughtering Formation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1552 Vengeful Slaughtering Formation

Chapter 1552 Vengeful Slaughtering Formation

1552 Vengeful Slaughtering Formation

After Yuan killed him for the third time, Qin Long had to wait 24 hours before respawning.

When an avatar is killed, it requires time to be reconstructed. While in this state, the player’s consciousness will be transported to a black room with a countdown.

“Fuck! How did he find me so fast?! We don’t respawn in the same location, and it’s randomized, so there’s no way he could’ve predicted my respawn point! He must have some sort of technique that allows him to find me quickly.” Qin Long quickly came to this conclusion, but he could do nothing about it.

Sure enough, Yuan found him using divine sense, which is already powerful enough to cover the entire city and more.

While Qin Long waited for his death cooldown to disappear, he started spreading rumors of Yuan being a spawn camper online, spamming this information as far and wide as possible.

Since spawn campers are widely despised and frowned upon by most players, it didn’t take long for people to respond. However, Qin Long quickly became irritated when nobody believed him.

“Hahaha… Player Yuan spawn camped you in the Third Heaven? He left for the Fourth Heaven already!”

“I’m sure Player Yuan has much better things to do than spawn camp a nobody!”

“Let’s say he did spawn camp you, for argument’s sake. I bet you deserved it. One thing I’ve learned about Player Yuan is that he isn’t the type to provoke others without reason.”

Qin Long was left fuming after seeing countless people mock him in the comments of his discussions. It also reminded him of Yuan’s words before getting killed.

Twenty-four hours have passed, and Qin Long’s death cooldown disappeared, but he didn’t immediately log back onto Cultivation Online and waited an extra day before doing so.

However, when he logged back in and respawned, Qin Long was terrified by a particular change that occurred within his body.

“M-my cultivation is gone!” he cried out loud.

When a player dies, one of three things can happen. One, nothing changes. Two, they lose all their cultivation and must start from scratch. Lastly, and arguably the worst outcome, they lose the ability to cultivate entirely, becoming a cripple.

Players around the world collaborated to determine the probabilities of these three outcomes. With contributions from hundreds of millions of players, the chances of each outcome were finally revealed.

Upon experiencing death, there was a 96 percent chance that nothing would happen to the player. There was a three percent chance they would lose all of their cultivation, and there was a one percent chance they would lose the ability to cultivate entirely.

Although the probability of becoming a cripple was low, it was not zero, and millions of players around the world have already lost their ability to cultivate.

Of course, losing all of one’s cultivation was still better than becoming a cripple. That said, Qin Long had spent a fortune getting to Spirit Lord, unlike most ordinary players, so it was a devastating blow to him regardless.

“M-my cultivation! Ahhhhhh!!!” Qin Long fell to the ground and bawled his eyes out on the street with many spectators around him. However, he was too devastated to care about the people around him, even if they saw him as a madman.

For many minutes, Qin Long did not stop crying. Due to his commotion, the city guards were eventually called. When they showed up, they treated Qin Long like a madman and dragged him away, returning peace to the city.

However, as they dragged Qin Long away, a masked figure appeared before them, blocking their path.

Before the guards could even speak, the masked man said, “Qin Long, right? I see that you’ve lost your cultivation base after the last death. Unfortunately, but at least you can still cultivate.”

Qin Long snapped out of his daze after hearing Yuan’s voice.

However, before he could speak, Yuan continued, “At least for now. If you do not give the Chu Family your word that you will never bother them again, I will keep killing you until you do. You will never be able to play Cultivation Online again because you will immediately die after respawning.”

Once he finished his sentence, Yuan used his Sword Aura to kill Qin Long again, shocking the guards. Yet, before they could even react, Yuan vanished into thin air like a ghost.

While Qin Long waited for his cooldown to disappear again, Yuan spoke to Yu Ning.

“Do you know any formation that can keep attacking someone even if I am away? I don’t plan to stay here much longer, but I can’t leave without ensuring the Chu Family’s safety.”

“Yes, I do. I can create a formation around this city that will immediately attack a specific individual whenever they enter its range, and it can operate even if you’re not present, but it’ll only be able to target people you’ve interacted with before.” Yu Ning responded.

“That’s perfect. Since he’ll keep respawning within this city, this will keep him inside the city for as long as I want… Alright, let’s do this.” Yuan said.

Yu Ning took control of Yuan’s body the next moment to create the formation.

Sometime later, Yuan regained control and activated the formation.

<Vengeful Slaughtering Formation has been activated>

He wanted to witness the formation in action at least once before he left, so Yuan remained in the city until Qin Long’s return.

‘I’ll give you three days, Qin Long. Hopefully, you won’t immediately budge and die a few more times before coming to your senses.’ Yuan smiled inwardly.

Meanwhile, Qin Long was losing his mind inside his room.

“That fucking bastard, Player Yuan! I swear to god that I will fucking kill him even if it’s the last thing I do!”

Despite Yuan’s warning, Qin Long had no intention of listening to his demands.

“I refuse to believe that you’ll remain in the Third Heaven forever just to kill me!”

Qin Long decided to wait an entire week before returning to Cultivation Online. In the meantime, he spoke to his father about his situation.

Of course, Qin Mulan was unhappy to learn that his son had lost all his cultivation, wasting the fortune they’d spent on resources to boost his cultivation. But there was no use crying over spilled milk.

Three days later, seeing that Qin Long hadn’t returned yet, Yuan left the city and made his way back to the Stairway to Heaven to return to the Fourth Heaven.

“Welcome back, Master. Are you returning to the Fourth Heaven?” Tian’er asked him.

“Yes.”

“Okay.”

Tian’er created a staircase straight to the Fourth Heaven for him.

“Thank you.”

Yuan ascended, returning to the Fourth Heaven shortly after.

Upon returning to the Fourth Heaven, Yuan contacted Dong Ye.

“Dong Ye, I need you to find someone for me. They are an escaped immortal from the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto. This immortal has the Shrouded Physique. Apparently, he was spotted in the Fourth Heaven.”

“Of course, I shall find him even if it’s the last thing I do.” Dong Ye immediately agreed without asking for his reason for wanting to find this immortal.

“Thank you.”

“Now, I have some time to spare while waiting for Dong Ye. What should I do in the meantime?” Yuan pondered out loud.

After thinking for some time, he remembered Xi Shengmo telling him about the Pavilion of Knowledge, which may have information on the Immortal Monarch.

‘Let’s give them a visit with Xi Meili.’

With his next destination in mind, Yuan went to find Xi Meili at the Cultivators’ Haven.

When he arrived, he went directly to their training rooms. However, they were all closed.

He didn’t want to disturb them, so he used his divine sense to peek inside Xi Meili’s room to see if she was busy.

Xi Meili was partially transformed inside her training room. Her body emitted a profound and oppressive aura as she sat in a cultivating position. The walls around her were also heavily damaged, even though they could resist attacks from Spirit Sovereigns without any problem.

Seeing this, Yuan didn’t dare to disturb her and waited for her to finish.

Several days later, Yuan suddenly received a notification.

<Vengeful Slaughtering Formation has been initialized>

‘So he’s finally decided to return to Cultivation Online, huh?’

A few seconds after this notification, another notification appeared before him.

<You have killed ‘Qin Long’>

Meanwhile, when Qin Long finally logged into Cultivation Online for the first time in a week, his presence triggered the formation.

The formation located Qin Long’s precise location and activated in less than three seconds, and before Qin Long could even fully see his surroundings, his body was bombarded by powerful attacks that would instantly obliterate even a Spirit King, so even if he still had his original cultivation, it wouldn’t have saved him.

<You have died>

Qin Long had only managed to take a single breath in Cultivation Online before being killed, and he wasn’t even able to release the breath he took in.

Even after his death, it took a long minute before Qin Long comprehended that he’d just been killed for the fourth time.

“What the fuck?! He actually waited an entire week for me?!” Qin Long was more shocked by Yuan’s stubbornness than his sudden death.

“Does he really intend to spawn camp me until our family stops messing with the Chu Family?! I don’t even have the authority to make such a call!” he cried out loud.

Only the family head, his father, had the authority to fulfill Yuan’s demand, but he did not want to embarrass himself further, putting him in a perplexed situation.

chapters.title: Chapter 1553 Purple Sea
chapters.body:
Chapter 1553 Purple Sea

Chapter 1553 Purple Sea

1553 Purple Sea

After his death, Qin Long immediately logged off Cultivation Online and tried to cultivate. He sighed in relief after realizing that he could still cultivate properly.

Even though the chances of becoming a cripple were only one percent, it was enough to stress any player out, much less someone like Qin Long.

“Damn it! What should I do?! I cannot keep letting him get away with this! At this rate, he’ll really cripple me!”

After pondering for a long moment, Qin Long suddenly thought of a good idea.

“Since nobody believes me that Player Yuan is spawn killing me, I shall let the entire world witness it! If there were thousands of players watching, I doubt Player Yuan would dare to kill me in front of so many people! This will allow me to get away safely!”

He went online and started another campaign. This time, he advertised his own demise, telling people to visit Night Wing City if they wished to see Player Yuan spawn camping him.

Sure enough, his antics were effective, and many players visited Night Wing City over the next several days. However, many of these players just wanted to see Player Yuan and couldn’t care less about Qin Long being killed.

News agencies also sent people to witness Player Yuan spawn killing, as it would give them something to talk about for a while.

Soon, tens of thousands of players could be seen wandering Night Wing City.

Four days after his last death, Qin Long logged back into Cultivation Online. Night Wing City was bustling with activity, with players thronging every corner, most of them hoping to meet Player Yuan.

After respawning, Qin Long shouted out loud, “Witness me!”

The players around him instantly turned their heads to look at Qin Long, and just as they finished turning their heads, several powerful attacks descended from the sky to attack Qin Long, instantly killing him.

Qin Long was shocked by his swift death. He could’ve never anticipated Yuan would be so ruthless as to kill him without hesitation despite the presence of so many players.

As for the players, they immediately looked at the sky, expecting to see Yuan hovering above them. However, they were left puzzled when nobody was in the air where the attacks were shot from.

“Where’s Player Yuan? I don’t see him.”

“Did he leave already?”

“That quickly? No way. I don’t think he was even here.”

“Hahaha! What kind of scam is this? I knew this Qin Long was trying to slander Player Yuan all along, but I was still expecting something! How foolish of me!”

“Who was the one that just got killed? I couldn’t even see his appearance clearly before he died.”

“Probably some poor soul paid to die so that they could sell this nonsense to us.”

Qin Long quickly became a laughing stock once news of what had transpired within Night Wing City spread on the internet.

[Qin Long, eldest son of the Qin Family, slanders Player Yuan]

[Player Yuan is innocent]

Many articles about Qin Long quickly surfaced, spreading through the community like wildfire.

Qin Mulan quickly learned of Qin Long’s embarrassing situation and summoned him.

“Long’er! What the fuck are you doing?! I asked you to take care of Player Yuan, but you dragged our family name through the mud instead! You’re a laughing stock right now!” Qin Mulan slammed his desk, destroying it.

Now that his reputation had hit rock bottom, Qin Long no longer cared about losing some more and revealed the entire situation to his father.

“Player Yuan threatened to continue killing me until I lose my ability to cultivate or we stop messing with the Chu Family. At this rate, I will never be able to play Cultivation Online properly again. Please do something about this, Father! Let’s just forget about the Chu Family! They’re not worth our attention anymore!” Qin Long appeared to be on the verge of tears as he revealed this information.

Qin Mulan wore a deep frown on his face after hearing this and said, “Our Qin Family and the Chu Family have been at war with each other for many generations! How will we face our ancestors if we give up?! We’d essentially be acknowledging our defeat! We will not stop until the Chu Family is completely bankrupted!”

“As for your situation,just take a break from Cultivation Online to focus on your real cultivation. I doubt Player Yuan will spawn camp you forever. He will eventually have to give up and return to doing whatever he’s doing. Also, he has an upcoming Faction War with the Fiery Garden of Hell. He won’t be able to play Cultivation Online during that time, so you can use that time to leave the Night Wing City.”

Qin Long looked at his father with wide eyes.

“Y-you’re a genius, Father! I completely forgot about his upcoming Faction War! Alright, I’ll do that and take a break from Cultivation Online.”

Qin Mulan nodded and said, “I will try to do something about those mocking you online. While I won’t be able to censor everything, it’ll definitely reduce the amount of people talking about you.”

“Thank you, Father. I apologize for this failure. I will definitely make it up to you one day.”

With that, Qin Long stopped logging onto Cultivation Online and focused on his cultivation on Earth.

Qin Mulan quickly started paying people to censor anything negative about Qin Long, slowing down the attention he was receiving.

Meanwhile, Yuan knocked on the door to Xi Meili’s training room once he noticed she’d finished her cultivation and appeared to be taking a break.

Xi Meili walked to the door, and she muttered to herself, “I’m pretty sure I still have plenty of time left in his place.”

“Huh? Yuan? You’re back?” she was pleasantly surprised to see him standing in front of her training room.

“Yes, and I plan on visiting the Pavilion of Knowledge, operated by the Xuan Family. Since your father had a connection with them, I was wondering if you want to come with me.”

“Of course! And I hope you didn’t wait too long for me.”

“It wasn’t too bad.”

They left the Cultivators’ Haven together shortly after.

“Do you know where the Pavilion of Knowledge is?” Xi Meili asked him.

“No, I don’t, but someone might.”

He asked Dong Ye about the Pavilion of Knowledge.

“The Pavilion of Knowledge is located on a secluded island in the middle of the Purple Sea, north of Keen City.”

“Purple Sea, huh? Alright.”

Yuan used the teleportation formation to travel to Keen City and, from there, traveled north for several days until he encountered a purple-colored sea.

“I wasn’t expecting a literal purple-colored sea…” Xi Meili muttered with a surprised look on her face. This was her first time seeing something so unusual before.

With their divine sense, they could see thousands of fishermen at the docks.

“Hey, what are they doing?” Xi Meili, who had never seen fishermen before, asked.

“They’re fishermen, and they are fishing—trying to catch the fish in the water,” Yuan explained.

“Fishing…? What an odd thing to do.”

“Want to take a closer look?” Yuan then asked.

“Yes!” she immediately nodded, her face filled with anticipation.

They entered the docks and watched the fishermen.

Sometime later, Xi Meili approached one of the fishermen and asked, “What are you trying to catch?”

“Hm? Are you new to the Purple Sea?” The fisherman asked.

“Yes. This is my first time visiting this place,” she nodded.

“Well, there’s only one thing we can catch from the Purple Sea

—Violet Tuna. However, they’re exceedingly rare. Even though there are thousands of us fishing every day around the clock, only around ten of us will catch a single Violet Tuna for the entire day.”

“Why are they so rare? And why are they so desirable?”

“Well, for one, they’re incredibly delicious. But that’s not the main reason they’re so coveted. Violet Tuna are infused with potent spiritual energy, and consuming one can significantly boost a cultivator’s strength. They’re not just a delicacy; they’re a treasure for anyone seeking to enhance their cultivation,” said the fisherman.

“What’s the history behind the purple-colored sea?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“Legend has it that a mighty dragon was slain near these waters, and its purple blood seeped into the sea, forever staining it with its vibrant hue,” said the fisherman.

“A dragon with purple blood? That would be the Ancient Galaxy Dragon. They’re incredibly rare and powerful, even among the dragon race. I heard less than ten of them have existed since ancient times.” Xi Meili muttered after hearing this information.

“Do you know anything about the Pavilion of Knowledge?” Yuan then asked.

“Ah, those weirdos that live in the middle of the Purple Sea? I know they live in the middle of the Purple Sea and rarely leave their place. There’s also a rumor that says they will reward you if you can provide them with useful information they don’t already know.”

“I see. Thank you for the information.”

Yuan suddenly pointed two fingers at the Purple Sea. Then, with a gesture, he fished out a sizeable purple-colored fish from the water with his spiritual energy.

This shocked every fisherman there and left them speechless.

Once the fish hovered before Yuan, he said, “For taking the time to speak with us.”

The fisherman accepted the Violet Tuna with a dazed look on his face, unsure how to react.

But before he could say anything, Yuan and Xi Meili flew away, disappearing into the horizon.𝔣𝖗𝔢𝔢𝔴𝖊𝖇𝖓𝔬𝔳𝖊𝔩.𝔠𝔬𝖒

chapters.title: Chapter 1554 Pavilion Of Knowledge
chapters.body:
Chapter 1554 Pavilion Of Knowledge

Chapter 1554 Pavilion of Knowledge

1554 Pavilion of Knowledge

“How did that young man do that? Divine sense shouldn’t work in the Purple Sea…” The fishermen were shocked by what Yuan had done.

The Purple Sea had a special property that blocked all divine sense from peeking underneath the water’s surface, hence why nobody there could repeat what Yuan did despite being cultivators themselves.

Meanwhile, Yuan and Xi Meili continued flying north for several days until they could see a tall silhouette on the horizon.

“We’re almost there.” Xi Meili’s voice was brimming with excitement, as this would mark her first encounter with another dragon clan outside the Ancient Dragon City.

After a few more hours of flying, they finally arrived at the Pavilion of Knowledge, situated in the center of a large island.

The Pavilion of Knowledge was an imposing, tower-like structure with a spiraling design that ascended towards the heavens, its peak lost amidst the clouds. The island looked like any tropical island, but magical beasts wandered freely. Most of them were around Spirit Emperor level, with a few at Spirit Sovereign level.

However, before they could even reach the island, Yuan and Xi Meili stopped when they noticed a figure approaching them from the island.

This individual was a middle-aged man with blue hair and azure-colored eyes who boasted the cultivation of Spirit Enlightenment.

“Welcome to the Pavilion of Knowledge. I am Xuan Wei, one of the Seven Scroll Elders here. How can we assist you today?”

When Xuan Wei spoke, his gaze was mainly on Xi Meili, almost as if he was aware of her dragon bloodline.

“I’m here to learn more about the Immortal Monarch,” Yuan said.

Xuan Wei turned to look at him with a frown and replied, “You want information on the Immortal Monarch? Not only are you not worthy, but you won’t even be able to afford it.”

Xi Meili frowned and said, “Who are you to decide whether he’s worthy or not?”

“Any information on the Immortal Monarch is sacred and priceless, and our Pavilion of Knowledge does not accept money or treasures for payment. The only payment we accept is information of equal value, and there’s no way in hell a nobody from the Fourth Heaven would have information as valuable as information on the Immortal Monarch.” Xuan Wei sneered.

“You’re making a lot of assumptions…for a nobody.” Xi Meili sneered back.

Xuan Wei frowned and said, “I showed you respect by personally coming out to greet you because I could tell you’re of dragon bloodline, and this is how you treat me? You can leave now. The Pavilion of Knowledge will not serve you.”

“If you want to show respect, you should’ve sent the head of this place to greet me. What is a mere servant’s greeting worth?” Xi Meili shook her head, seemingly genuinely disappointed.

Xuan Wei’s face darkened after being mocked as a servant by Xi Meili.

“If you think I won’t harm you because we have dragon blood flowing through our veins, you’re sorely mistaken, Junior!” Xuan Wei roared.

“Oh, yeah? I’d love to see you try!” Xi Meili retrieved Furious Dragon and went into a combative position.

Seeing this, Xuan Wei merely released his cultivation base.

“How brazen of you, Junior! This Senior shall teach you a lesson your parents never taught you today!”

Yuan shook his head at their situation before looking at the Pavilion of Knowledge and speaking, “To the Senior watching this, are you sure you want to let them continue? Her surname is Xi, and her father’s name is Xi Shengmo.”

“?!?!”

After hearing this information, the individual who had been watching them from the peak of the Pavilion of Knowledge experienced a great shock, causing him to fall back on his chair.

“Stop it, Xuan Wei!”

A loud voice boomed the next moment, instantly halting Xuan Wei’s movements. He turned to look at the Pavilion of Knowledge with a puzzled expression on his face, and a silhouette approached them from the tower before he could even utter a word.

This silhouette belonged to an elderly man with long white hair, a short blue beard, and azure-colored eyes shimmering with profound knowledge. He donned a black outfit adorned with a blue scroll-shaped symbol.

“Grandfather, what are you doing out here? And why did you stop me from punishing these rude Juniors?”

However, the old man completely ignored him and respectfully bowed to Xi Meili.

“I apologize for my grandson’s lack of manners, Young Lady. May I have your name?” 𝚏r𝗲ewe𝚋𝐧𝚘vel.𝚌𝚘m

“Xi Meili. Who are you, old man?”

“Y-you! How dare you address the master of the Pavilion of Knowledge as ‘old man’?!”

“Just shut up for a moment!” The old man snapped at him before returning to smile at Xi Meili.

“As he said, I am the master of the Pavilion of Knowledge and the previous Patriarch of the Xuan Family who operates the Pavilion of Knowledge within the Fourth Heaven. My name is Xuan Kun.”

“Is it true that your father’s name is Xi Shengmo?” he then asked.

“That’s right. You know my father?”

Xuan Kun nodded and said, “I didn’t just know him. I—our Xuan Family used to serve the Xi Family and his father—long before they entered the Ancient Dragon City.”

“What?! But they haven’t left the Ancient Dragon City for millions of years! Why would they come out now?!” Xuan Wei cried out loud after hearing this.

“Since you’re out here, does that mean your father is also…?” Xuan Kun asked, his eyes flickered with a hint of anticipation.

“Nope. I left the Ancient Dragon City by myself to travel with Yuan.” Xi Meili shook her head.

“Oh… is that so…” Xuan Kun immediately became dejected. “I was hoping to speak with him again after so long.”

“Anyway, let’s go somewhere more comfortable and continue our conversation there, shall we?”

Xi Meili nodded.

Yuan and Xi Meili proceeded to follow Xuan Kun to the Pavilion of Knowledge.

“Grandfather, are you sure we can trust them? What if they’re impersonating the Xi Family?” Xuan Wei asked him through divine sense.

“Shut up, you idiot. You’ve never seen someone from the Xi Family, so you wouldn’t know, but I can tell that the Young Lady is definitely from the Xi Family. I am confident enough to put my life on the line. I also expect you to give her a proper apology.”

“Why are you treating her like you still serve the Xi Family? We haven’t worked for them since they went into hiding within the Ancient Dragon City!” Xuan Wei couldn’t comprehend why they had to lower their heads to someone they used to work for.

“I bet our current standing isn’t lower than the Xi Family!”

“You better watch your mouth, Xuan Wei. If you think I won’t hit you because you’re my grandson, you can test me! Our Xuan Family has served the Xi Family for generations since ancient times. Even if we no longer work for them, we will always put them above us!”

“…” Xuan Wei could not understand Xuan Kun’s point of view and decided to remain silent.

Once they arrived at the Pavilion of Knowledge, Xuan Kun led Yuan and Xi Meili to one of the guest rooms for esteemed guests.

“Xuan Wei, go bring them some tea. You can use my tea leaves.”

“What? We have servants for that.” Xuan Wei wore a look of disbelief on his face.

How could he, one of the Seven Scroll Elders, stoop as low as serving tea to guests? His position was only below that of his father and Xuan Kun.

“Don’t make me repeat myself.” Xuan Kun narrowed his eyes with a cold glint in them.

Xuan Wei could only swallow his frustration and listen to Xuan Kun’s command.

“I guess I wasn’t wrong to call you a servant.” Xi Meili chuckled, rubbing salt on his wounds.

Xuan Wei’s body trembled in anger, and he stopped walking for a second, but he didn’t say anything and quickly left.

“Don’t tease him too much, Young Lady. He has a short temper.” Xuan Kun smiled.

“That’s what he gets for looking down on Yuan,” she sneered.

“Yuan… you must be her friend.” Xuan Kun turned to look at Yuan, who appeared unremarkable at a glance, but once he tried to peek deeper, Xuan Kun was surprised that even he couldn’t inspect Yuan’s body, who was only a Spirit King.

“Thank you for calling out to me earlier. I would no longer be able to face my ancestors in the future if someone from our Xuan Family had harmed a member of the Xi Family.”

“He’s more than just a friend to me, Senior Xuan. He’s essentially a member of our family—with my parents’ approval, of course.”

“What? His Majesty accepted a human into the Xi Family? That’s unthinkable…” Xuan Kun stared at her with wide eyes.

He could still vividly recall Xi Shengmo’s hatred for humans, which forced his family to escape into the Ancient Dragon City.

“You don’t have to believe me. Yuan, show him your robe and ring.” Xi Meili winked at him.

Yuan nodded and retrieved the Golden Dragon Robe and the Dragon Spatial Ring, showing it to Xuan Kun, who stood up from shock.

“The Dragon Spatial Ring?! And even the Golden Dragon Robe?! Heavens! I never thought I would see these precious items in a human’s possession!” he exclaimed.

“You have no idea how much he helped my Xi Family. If it weren’t for him, the Azure Dragon Family would’ve ended our family’s lineage.” Xi Meili sighed.

“The Azure Dragon Family? Those ambitious bastards tried to do something like that? Though, I can’t say I’m surprised. They have always been hungry for power and authority—even before they moved to the Ancient Dragon City.” Xuan Kun shook his head at this information.

chapters.title: Chapter 1555 Pavilion Of Knowledge(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1555 Pavilion Of Knowledge(2)

Chapter 1555 Pavilion of Knowledge(2)

1555 Pavilion of Knowledge(2)

“Young Master, on behalf of the Xuan Family, thank you for helping the Xi Family.” Xuan Kun respectfully lowered his head at Yuan.

“Don’t even mention it. I only did what was natural.” Yuan smiled.

“Anyway, you’re here for information on the Immortal Monarch, correct?” Xuan Kun then asked.

“Yes, that’s correct.”

“I see… Unfortunately, as Xuan Wei had already said, any information on the Immortal Monarch is incredibly sacred to us, so even if you are with the Xi Family, I cannot give you any information without payment. This is also a rule of the Pavilion of Knowledge I cannot break as its Master.”

“No, I completely understand. I never thought it was going to be free, anyway. If you provide me with new information on the Immortal Monarch, I will be willing to share information on the Immortal Monarch that you may not be aware of. That sounds fair, correct?” Yuan said.

Xuan Kun looked at Yuan with a doubtful gaze. Information on the Immortal Monarch was incredibly rare and not something that could be found within the Fourth Heaven in this day and age, after all.

However, he only remained doubtful and didn’t outright dismiss Yuan as a fraud.

“By the way, how does the Pavilion of Knowledge verify the information they’re given?” Xi Meili suddenly asked.

“We have a treasure called the Heavenly Scroll of Validity. It can tell whether someone is being genuine or not,” explained Xuan Kun.

‘Sounds like my Grail of Truth.’ Yuan thought inwardly.

While the Heavenly Scroll of Validity and the Grail of Truth had similar functions, they were actually very different. The Grail of Truth only had the capability to tell whether someone was lying or not, so it wouldn’t be able to detect anything deeper than that.

As for the Heavenly Scroll of Validity, it directly reads one’s soul to tell if they are being genuine or not.

“If you truly wish to purchase information on the Immortal Monarch, we will need to verify your information before the Heavenly Scroll of Validity.” Xuan Kun continued.

“I’ll do it.” Yuan nodded.

“Let’s do it after we drink some tea. I’d like to chat more with the Young Lady here, as well.”

“I don’t mind.”

Xuan Wei returned sometime later with three cups of tea.

He placed the tea on the table, immediately turned around, and walked back out to the door without uttering a single word, clearly still upset that he was treated like a servant.

“Wait.” Xuan Kun suddenly stopped him.

“The Young Lady here hasn’t received an apology from you yet.”

“Why do I have to apologize to her?! I did nothing wrong!” Xuan Wei roared.

“Do I really have to repeat myself?” Xuan Kun frowned, his domineering aura growing more noticeable.

Xuan Wei’s entire body trembled in anger, but he didn’t dare to talk back.

He turned to Xi Meili and reluctantly lowered his head before stiffly speaking, “I’m sorry.”

“Apology accepted.” Xi Meili could’ve remained silent, but she thought it would annoy him more if she responded, and she was right.

Xuan Wei stormed out of the room the next moment, his face flushed red.

“Now then… Young Lady, can you tell me about your life in the Ancient Dragon City? And how is the Xi Family fairing nowadays?” Xuan Kun asked.

Xi Meili casually nodded and began recalling her life in the Ancient Dragon City.

Although Xuan Kun’s request seemed innocent and out of curiosity at a glance, he was actually trying to acquire information on the Ancient Dragon City.

Due to Ancient Dragon City’s seclusive and secretive nature, any information on them would be precious to the Pavilion of Knowledge, especially information on the Xi Family, a dragon clan with royal blood that hasn’t been seen in the outside world for millions of years.

Yuan silently enjoyed the tea as he listened to their chat.

In the blink of an eye, several hours have passed since Yuan arrived at the Pavilion of Knowledge.

“I see… I’m glad that His Majesty is doing well these days. It’s unfortunate that he can no longer progress his cultivation due to the lack of spiritual energy there, but he knew this before going inside the Ancient Dragon City. Now let me tell you about the history of the Xuan Family with the Xi Family.” Xuan Kun said.

“Our Xuan Family has served the Xi Family for generations— even before the Divine Heavens split into Nine Heavens. It all started when your ancestors saved our ancestor’s life. After our ancestors swore loyalty to the Xi Family, we started working as their servants until we were promoted to being their record keeper, where we record significant moments or their achievements.”

“Everything was great…until the humans started hunting down dragons like madmen. The Xi Family didn’t want to deal with humans and decided to move into the Ancient Dragon City, a world of its own, created by none other than the Dragon Ancestor. However, the Xi Family didn’t force their retainers to follow them and gave us a choice—to follow them or remain in the Nine Heavens.”

“Our Xuan Family wanted to follow the Xi Family, but alas, we found a new objective in life when a certain someone showed up—the Immortal Monarch. This individual was so awe-

inspiring and special that we wanted to remain outside to witness and record his greatness, which was the start of the Pavilion of Knowledge.”

“This is why we regard information on the Immortal Monarch sacred. It’s the foundation of the Pavilion of Knowledge.”

“Anyway, we can start verifying your information now.”

Yuan nodded and stood up, “Let’s go.”

Xuan Kun proceeded to bring them to a large and spacious room on the upper floor. This room only had four things inside—a table, two chairs, and a golden scroll that was floating above the table.

This scroll emitted a profound, almost otherworldly aura that permeated the room.

“Please, have a seat.” Xuan Kun gestured toward one of the chairs before sitting down on the other one.

Once Yuan sat across from him, Xuan Kun activated the Heavenly Scroll of Validity.

“From this point forth, the Heavenly Scroll of Validity will enter your soul to determine whether your information is genuine or not.”

“Let’s start with something simple. What is your name?”

“Yuan.”

The very next moment, the Heavenly Scroll of Validity emitted a golden glow that made the room appear divine. 𝚏𝗿𝗲ew𝐞b𝐧o𝚟𝚎𝗹.com

“Good. Now, lie to me. Do you know Xi Meili?”

“No.”

The Heavenly Scroll of Validity did not glow and released an imposing pressure that filled the room, as though a judge had just sentenced someone to death.

“Last test. I won’t ask you anything. Just tell me something. It doesn’t have to be real, either.”

Hearing such words, Yuan showed a mysterious smile on his face as he spoke, “The Immortal Monarch is still alive.”

“?!?!” Xuan Kun’s eyes widened with surprise after hearing Yuan’s information.

As someone with millenniums of experience verifying information, he could usually tell when someone was farting or not, even without the Heavenly Scroll of Validity’s help. Yet, he somehow found Yuan’s information to be real even though it shouldn’t be possible.

Xuan Kun immediately turned to look at the Heavenly Scroll of Validity for its response. He wanted it to permeate the room with its golden glow so badly, but he knew that it wasn’t going to happen, as that would have severe and significant implications.

A moment later, the room brightened as the Heavenly Scroll of Validity glowed gold.

“WHAT?!”

Xuan Kun stood from his chair and slammed the table, looking like he’d just witnessed his ancestors crawling out of their graves.

After standing there for a long minute with a bewildered look on his face, Xuan Kun looked at Yuan and asked in a trembling voice, “W-where did you get this information?!”

However, Yuan merely shrugged and said, “Who knows, but the information I just gave you should be pretty valuable, right? I wonder if it’s valuable enough for you to give me what I want.”

Xuan Kun’s jaw dropped after hearing his words. He was rendered completely speechless, and his mind was already in a mess from the earlier information.

Eventually, he took a deep breath and sighed, “Very well… I shall give you all the information we have on the Immortal Monarch. But honestly, the information you gave us is already more valuable than everything we’ve gathered since our foundation.”

“That’s fine.”

“Follow me…”

Xuan Kun tried to walk to the door, but he stumbled and fell on the floor like an old man who suddenly had his cane taken away.

“Sorry, but can you give me a moment?” he asked with an embarrassed look on his face.

“Take your time.” Yuan nodded with a smile.

Xuan Kun remained seated on the floor for several minutes, contemplating the information that the Immortal Monarch was still alive out there somewhere.

‘The Immortal Monarch is alive…? The Immortal Monarch is alive?! Why hasn’t he made an appearance for so long?! What about the Nine Divine Supremes?! Just who is this young human?!’ Xuan Kun knew Yuan was an extraordinary existence when he managed to visit the Ancient Dragon City and receive the Xi Family’s approval, but this was truly far beyond his expectations.

‘This young man… could he be related to the Immortal Monarch? Perhaps his descendant? No… he wouldn’t need to look for information on the Immortal Monarch if he were the Immortal Monarch’s descendant…’

Sometime later, once Xuan Kun could finally walk properly, he led Yuan to the room on the highest floor of the Pavilion of Knowledge.

chapters.title: Chapter 1556 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch
chapters.body:
Chapter 1556 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch

Chapter 1556 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch

1556 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch

“This is the room where we keep all of the Immortal Monarch’s information. We call this room the ‘Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch,'” Xuan Kun said as they approached a metal door crafted from a black metallic material adorned with the image of a human figure surrounded by nine beastly entities.

“You will be the first guests to visit this room. Welcome to the Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch.”

Xuan Kun proceeded to bite his thumb and press it against the door, feeding it with his blood. The black door slowly creaked open, and Xi Meili swallowed nervously in anticipation. However, once the door was fully opened and they stepped inside, the interior was quite ordinary.

Inside, tall wooden shelves hugged every wall in the room, and thousands of scrolls were neatly placed on them.

“Please, take as much time as you need here. They are all labeled, too. I will be leaving to deal with something else. If you need anything, don’t hesitate to call me. I will be in the room across from this one.” Xuan Kun said to them before closing the door for them. Then, he went into the other room and sealed the door shut.

“Where should we start?” Xi Meili asked as she looked around.

Yuan used his divine sense to look at all of the labels quickly and pointed to the shelf by the door.

“That’s the beginning, so we can start from there.”

Xi Meili nodded and walked to the shelf.

“Immortal Monarch’s First Appearance,” she read the label out loud.

She picked out the scroll labeled with the number ‘one’ and looked at Yuan, asking, “Can I do the honors of reading it?”

He nodded with a smile, “Go ahead.”

Thus, Xi Meili began reading the records of the Immortal Monarch’s first appearance.

According to the records, the Immortal Monarch’s appearance was very sudden, like thunder in a clear blue sky, and when he appeared, there were already three Divine Supremes by his side.

“When he first appeared in the world, he was already accompanied by three supreme entities that would eventually be known as the Nine Divine Supremes.”

“The first was Dragon Goddess Yeyou, also known as the Dragon of Origin, one of the first dragons to exist in the Divine Heavens alongside the Dragon Ancestor and the Great One. Unlike the other two Dragons of Origin, who spread their names far and wide, Dragon Goddess Yeyou preferred being lowkey and seclusive. She was rumored to be the strongest divine beast during her time. Unfortunately, she disappeared along with the Immortal Monarch.” Xi Meili read, her hands trembling with excitement.

“The second supreme entity who followed the Immortal Monarch was the Primordial Phoenix, one of the first phoenixes to exist. Her prowess rivaled that of the Dragons of Origin, except the Dragon Goddess, who was stronger than the other two Dragons of Origin. The Primordial Phoenix’s body burned so intensely that she would set entire stars ablaze just by being in its vicinity. She was rumored to have true immortality, where she would not die even if her soul perished. She also disappeared alongside the Immortal Monarch.”

“The third supreme entity that followed the Immortal Monarch was also the most mysterious and unique of the Nine Divine Supremes. Eater of Stars, once known as a natural calamity that devoured stars and everything unfortunate enough to enter its path as though it was her only purpose. We don’t know her name or how the Immortal Monarch tamed an entity like her, but she was the most obedient of the Nine Divine Supremes. Almost everything about her is a mystery. From her origins to her true strength, they all remained unknown even after the Immortal Monarch’s disappearance.”

“The Eater of Stars disappeared alongside the Immortal Monarch but was spotted nine hundred thousand years later. She had returned to being the Abyss Calamity she was once infamous for, devouring the stars and everything in the galaxy more intensely than before. However, there hasn’t been any news of her since her initial reemergence.”

Yuan’s body trembled after learning that Eater of Stars had returned to her life before she met the Immortal Monarch, most likely because he’d perished and could no longer satisfy her insatiable hunger.

He looked to the ceiling with a woeful frown on his face, wondering if she was still living such a harsh life in the starry sky.

‘If she’s still out there, I need to find her as soon as possible so she can live a normal life again…’ Yuan clenched his fists until they bled, his eyes filled with newfound determination.

However, in order to traverse the starry sky without the help of treasures, one must at least be a Golden Immortal. As for treasures that allow one to traverse the starry sky, most of them are insanely expensive.

“The Immortal Monarch and these three supreme entities traveled the Divine Heavens for thousands of years with one goal—to stop the war between humans and beasts and start their coexistence.”

“The first intervention by the Immortal Monarch was between the Blood Mastiffs and humans. Due to their highly desirable blood, Blood Mastiffs were hunted by humans to near extinction. As revenge for his bloodline, the strongest Blood Mastiff, known as the Crimson Terror, went on a carnage and destroyed hundreds of human settlements and consumed countless humans.”

“In order to calm the Crimson Terror, the Immortal Monarch had to fight with it. Their fight lasted seven hundred years until the Crimson Terror was defeated. After their fight ended, the Crimson Terror swore loyalty to the Immortal Monarch and became the fourth Nine Divine Supreme.”

‘Crimson Terror… he was the Lord’s father…’ Yuan recalled what the Lord told him not long ago.

Xi Meili continued, “After turning the Crimson Terror into his subordinate, the Immortal Monarch swiftly dealt with the humans hunting the Blood Mastiffs. He declared that if any humans hunted Blood Mastiffs solely to benefit from their existence—or any beasts, for that matter—he would personally wipe them out. This was the true beginning of his campaign to bring peace to humans and beasts.”

“Over the next several thousand years, the Immortal Monarch stayed true to his words, killing humans that hunted beasts for their materials. As for beasts that hunted innocent humans for revenge, the Immortal Monarch also dealt with them. A lot

—countless humans and beasts died at the hands of the Immortal Monarch. In the end, the war between humans and beasts was forced to end by a third party with overwhelming power that could wipe out either side.”

“The Immortal Monarch appeared to be a gentle and kind individual at a glance, but he was a true tyrant who made the world obedient with fear and power. However, he also made the world a better place by doing so.”

“A tyrant…? That was unexpected.” Xi Meili looked at Yuan, who also looked surprised by this information.

He thought the Immortal Monarch had ended the war between humans and beasts through other means, but it turned out that he had simply forced it to end by making everyone fear him.

“To end the war by force… it may be a morally questionable method, but it was undeniably effective,” Yuan sighed out loud.

“No… it may have been the only method…” Xi Meili suddenly said.

“Not just humans, but beasts can also be unreasonable beings. If you wish to have two objects that naturally repel each other join together, it can only be done with force.” Xi Meili said.

For example, attempting to make two magnets of opposite poles touch requires force, as their natural state is to repel each other.

Yuan nodded, agreeing with her.

Xi Meili continued reading the scrolls.

“Once the war ended, the Immortal Monarch began working towards bringing humans and beasts together, something that could not be forced.”

“In order to do this, he started with befriending the beasts to let them know that it was possible to coexist with humans. The Immortal Monarch started with the Dragon Clan due to his connection with Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

“The Immortal Monarch started from top to bottom, so he first met with the Dragon Ancestor, who he quickly befriended. Due to the Dragon Ancestor’s immense influence in the Dragon Clan and with two of the three Dragons of Origin by his side, convincing the rest of the Dragon Clan required little to no effect.”

“After gaining the Dragon Clan’s approval, the Immortal Monarch sought the approval of the Great One. However, for reasons unknown, the Great One refused, even antagonizing the Immortal Monarch. Rumors suggested that the Great One was not on friendly terms with the other two Dragons of Origin and thus could not accept someone they already accepted.”

“The Immortal Monarch and the Great One weren’t on good terms with each other? Now that’s another piece of information that I did not expect…” Yuan muttered after hearing this information.

He thought he had some kind of fate with the Great One at the beginning of his journey in Cultivation Online, but after their last interaction, their relationship with each other had become somewhat clouded.

With this new information, his future relationship with the Great One became even more cloudy.

‘It’s a pity that we might be enemies in the future. I even learned Dragon’s Gaze from him in this life…’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

However, he didn’t immediately consider the Great One an enemy, as there were still many uncertain things.

Xi Meili continued, “One day, the Immortal Monarch and the Great One had a massive fight that shook the Divine Heavens, and they became sworn enemies since that day.”

“…”

Yuan became speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 1557 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1557 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch(2)

Chapter 1557 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch(2)

1557 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch(2)

“Sworn enemies with the Great One? Now that’s an achievement one can brag about.” Xi Meili chuckled at this information.

The Great One was a supreme entity that stood at the pinnacle of the world. To be noticed by him was an honor beyond measure, and to be considered a sworn enemy was a mark of unparalleled distinction. Of course, whether that was a good thing or not was debatable.

“What do you think caused the fight?” Xi Meili looked at Yuan.

He shrugged, “I can’t imagine. From my few encounters with him, I can tell that he’s a very prideful and fulfilled individual. I cannot imagine someone like him would fight over something simple.”

Xi Meili then said, “I always thought the Dragons of Origin were close to each other since they were the first dragons to come into existence and might see each other as siblings, but I guess I was too naive.”

“Don’t siblings tend to argue with each other?”

Yuan, drawing from his own experiences, mused, “Although most of my time with Yu Rou was pleasant, there were moments when we had fought in the past. So, I would understand it if the Dragons of Origin had similar experiences.”

“Sibling fights, huh? I totally understand. I am always butting heads with Murong.” Xi Meili chuckled.

She continued reading the next scroll a moment later.

“After securing the Dragon Clan’s approval, the Immortal Monarch set his sights on the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, a formidable yet relatively small clan renowned for their ferocity and tumultuous nature. Ranked as the seventh most powerful clan, their influence belied their size. The males, in particular, were notorious for their insatiable lust, a trait embedded deeply within their bloodline, which often led them into conflict with other clans.”

“Although the females were not as lustful as the males, they were known to go to extreme lengths to acquire a partner once they fancied someone. Due to their nature, they did not make a good first impression, and the entire clan received a massive beating from Dragon Goddess Yeyou when the males tried to go after her and the females tried to get overly friendly with the Immortal Monarch.”

“Their leader, the Heavenly White Tiger God, was particularly affected. He was beaten so severely that he lost all sexual desire, becoming as pure and chaste as a monk. He would also become the fifth of the Nine Divine Supremes.”

“Che. The Heavenly White Tigers.” Xi Meili sucked her teeth in an annoyed manner after being reminded of her encounter with Bai Xutao.

“The Dragon Goddess should’ve just killed all of them back then,” she casually commented.

She looked at Yuan and continued, “By the way, how’s Feng Feng’s recovery?”

“I’ve been feeding her my blood almost every day. Her appearance’s changed a lot, but I still don’t know when she’ll return.”

“I see… Hopefully, she’ll return to us soon.” Xi Meili said before returning to the scroll.

“During his campaign to receive the approval of all major beast clans, the Immortal Monarch would occasionally hunt the surviving demons that had managed to flee the war.”

“Demons, huh? I never got to see one. They must be all gone by now, right? What a pity.” Xi Meili muttered.

Yuan smiled and said, “You should be glad that you never got to experience their disgusting presence. Unfortunately, demons still exist in this world—and mine. They’re just in hiding now.”

“Have you seen one?” she then asked.

He nodded, “Yes, and more than just one, unfortunately.”

“Then did you fight them? They’re supposedly immortal—with an indestructible body.”

“Their body is definitely destructive. They just have insane recovery, so any damage they take would instantly recover, and unless you get rid of their entire being in one go, they could fully regenerate even from a small chunk of flesh.”

“The only reliable method of getting rid of them would be demon-sealing techniques and sacred flames. Unless you have one of these abilities, you don’t want to meet a demon.”

“I’ll keep that in mind,” she said.

She picked up another scroll and continued, “After traveling for hundreds of years, the Immortal Monarch met the sixth of the Nine Divine Supreme, a powerful divine beast that did not belong to any clan—the Divine Yin Yang Crane.”

“The Divine Yin Yang Crane was a one-of-a-kind mythical being said to bring immense luck to anyone for the rest of their life if they are lucky enough to see it, and most people live their whole life not seeing it, including immortals that have lived for millions of years. Yet, the Divine Yin Yang Crane had personally appeared before the Immortal Monarch and requested to follow him.”

“The Divine Yin Yang Crane, huh? This is my first time hearing about such a creature.” Xi Meili commented afterward.

“Me too,” Yuan said.

“Is this information helping you regain your memories?” she then asked.

“No, nothing’s coming up.”

“Maybe you’ll recall something after hearing a little more. We still have thousands of scrolls to go through, after all.”

She moved on to the next scroll.

“The seventh to join the Nine Divine Supremes was the Celestial Turtle, a divine beast said to have the strongest and most durable body in the Divine Heaven. The Celestial Turtle has a massive body that was many times larger than the sun, and due to its size, it could only live in the starry sky.” 𝗳r𝚎𝐞w𝐞𝚋𝗻𝗼vel.𝗰o𝐦

“Rumors say that the Immortal Monarch had built a home for himself and the other Divine Supremes somewhere on the back of the Celestial Turtle, but nobody could confirm this rumor.”

“The eighth of the Nine Divine Supreme was the Shadow Dweller, a divine beast that can enter and traverse shadows. Not much is known about this Divine Supreme due to its lack of presence.”

“The ninth and final of the Nine Divine Supreme is unknown. Nobody but the other Divine Supremes and the Immortal Monarch himself have seen the Ninth Divine Supreme’s beast form.” Xi Meili closed the scroll.

“There’s not much information about the Nine Divine Supremes here. Maybe they have their own category,” she said afterward.

chapters.title: Chapter 1558 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1558 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch(3)

Chapter 1558 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch(3)

1558 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch(3)

Although there were still thousands of scrolls left to be read, there were only a few scrolls left that focused on the Immortal Monarch’s journey. The other scrolls were dedicated to miscellaneous information on the Immortal Monarch and his companions.

“After spending thousands of years convincing the beast clans that humans could coexist with them, the Immortal Monarch helped some of the beast clans settle around human settlements, allowing them to experience it firsthand.”

“Of course, the humans weren’t happy about having beasts as neighbors, but there wasn’t anything they could do to get rid of them, as the beast clans were all protected by the Immortal Monarch, someone even the Celestial Emperor had to be respectful around.”

“Eventually, after many decades of coexistence, humans and beasts grew accustomed to each other’s presence. Gradually, they began to converse and build a sense of camaraderie, transforming from wary neighbors to unlikely allies.”

“Unfortunately, not every beast clan managed to coexist with humans. The Immortal Monarch did not force this on either side and allowed the beast clans that did not want to be around humans to return to their original settlement.”

“Eventually, the notion of humans coexisting with beasts became commonplace. Many beast clans lived alongside humans, collaborating to build thriving cities and communities, breaking down the barriers that once separated them.”

“The Immortal Monarch, content with the harmony he had fostered, ceased his role as mediator between humans and beasts and vanished from the world. For several thousand years, his whereabouts and activities remained a mystery. When he finally reappeared several thousand years later, he seemed like a different person, transformed by the unknown experiences during his absence.” 𝑓𝘳ℯℯ𝔀ℯ𝓫𝑛𝑜𝓋𝑒𝓵.𝘤𝘰𝓶

“His demeanor had shifted significantly; he appeared more serious, less approachable, and carried an aura of profound gravity. The cause of this transformation was unknown, but it was unmistakable. Not long after his return, the Immortal Monarch sought a private audience with the Celestial Emperor.”

“At that time, no one thought much of his audience with the Celestial Emperor, as they were known to be quite friendly and had many conversations before. However, this time was different. For reasons unknown to anyone but the two, their conversation sparked a fierce battle that lasted seven days straight. The Celestial Palace was flipped upside-down and turned to ruins as a result.”

“The Celestial Emperor survived, albeit heavily injured from the battle. As for the Immortal Monarch, he vanished once more, now branded a criminal. Despite this, the Celestial Emperor refrained from hunting him down, wary of the Immortal Monarch’s immense power and the might of his Nine Divine Supremes.”

“News of the Immortal Monarch attacking the Celestial Emperor sent shockwaves throughout the Divine Heaven, raising questions from all corners. Speculations quickly followed, but with so little information to go on, most of these theories sounded like pure fantasy.”

“After a few more thousand years of silence, the Immortal Monarch reappeared and confronted the Celestial Emperor once again—with all Nine Divine Supremes by his side this time. The Celestial Army was prepared to fight to the death to protect the Celestial Emperor, but to their surprise, the Immortal Monarch only uttered a few words to the Celestial Emperor before leaving. Yet, the Celestial Emperor’s face remained etched with fear long after the Immortal Monarch had departed.”

“The words uttered by the Immortal Monarch were ‘Only I control my own fate, nobody else’.”

“The Immortal Monarch vanished from the world after that, never to appear in public again. Shortly after his disappearance, a catastrophic event unfolded. The sky turned a bright red, as if painted with blood, and the Divine Heaven was split into nine different realms, becoming what we know today as the Nine Heavens.”

“It’s evident that the Immortal Monarch had something to do with the event, but nobody knew why—except for the Celestial Emperor. However, the Celestial Emperor remained silent despite being questioned by the entire world.”

Xi Meili returned the scroll to the shelf with a bewildered look on her face, clearly taken aback by what she’d just read.

Even Yuan wore a pondering expression on his face.

“So the Immortal Monarch caused the Divine Heaven to become the Nine Heavens, and it had something to do with the Celestial Emperor. I wonder what happened after he accomplished his ambitions that caused him to change so drastically?” Yuan muttered to himself.

“Whatever happened, it was disastrous enough to split the entire world into pieces.” Xi Meili commented.

“Can you think of anything powerful enough to split the Divine Heaven?” Yuan asked.

Xi Meili pondered for a moment before shaking her head, “While a Cultivation God may have enough power to destroy the world, splitting the Divine Heavens into several pieces while keeping the world intact is a different story. Maybe a treasure? I have no clue, honestly.”

“What do you think?” she then asked.

“It was most likely a fight that caused it. As for the Immortal Monarch’s opponent, there’s too little information to go on, so I cannot imagine.”

“A fight? The Immortal Monarch had the Nine Divine Supremes, who were considered the most powerful entities in the world, each strong enough to rival a Cultivation God. I cannot imagine an opponent the nine—ten of them couldn’t defeat even working together.” Xi Meili shook her head, finding it hard to believe anything could defeat the Nine Divine Supremes when even the Celestial Emperor was powerless before them, not to mention the Immortal Monarch himself.

Yuan couldn’t argue with Xi Meili’s logic. However, he couldn’t shake the feeling that it was definitely a fight that caused it.

Suddenly, a particular term appeared inside Yuan’s head.

‘Outer God…’

He recalled the Black Death uttering this word for some reason.

‘What if there are existences out there that defy the laws of this world—with power that surpasses the Nine Divine Supremes?’ he wondered inwardly.

It wouldn’t be the first time something extraordinary existed beyond their knowledge. Take Earth and Cultivation Online, for example.

If it weren’t for Cultivation Online, Yuan might never have discovered the existence of the Nine Heavens, a realm far beyond their world’s understanding and reach.

chapters.title: Chapter 1559 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1559 Chronicles Of The Immortal Monarch(4)

Chapter 1559 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch(4)

1559 Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch(4)

“Well, that’s all for the Immortal Monarch’s journey. The rest of the scrolls seem to focus more on his companions and the places he’d visited than the person himself.” Xi Meili said as she looked at the other shelves.

“Let’s check out the information on the Nine Divine Supremes,” he said.

Xi Meili nodded and excitedly walked to the shelf with a sign that read ‘Dragon Goddess Yeyou’.

“Dragon Goddess Yeyou, one of the first dragons to exist, was also known as the Dragon of Origins. Beyond Dragon Goddess Yeyou and the other two Dragons of Origin, the exact order in which the three Dragons of Origins came into existence remains unknown.”

“Although she is typically in seclusion, slumbering far away from any settlement, whenever she wakes up from it, she would always find someone to spar with, earning her the nickname of Supreme Tormentor.”

“Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s cultivation reached the equivalent of Cultivation God sometime past the Evil God’s Era. Rumor has it that she was the first entity to reach such a profound cultivation.”

“We didn’t learn of this until she started traveling with the Immortal Monarch. However, she was someone who got envious easily, especially when it concerned the Immortal Monarch, and would occasionally throw tantrums.”

Yuan smiled at this information and recalled the little tantrum she had thrown at the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

The scrolls went on to talk about Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s history—at least the ones publically known. Due to Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s seclusive nature, not much was known about her in her earlier days, especially before she met the Immortal Monarch.

According to those who fought with her, Dragon Goddess Yeyou had a remarkably simple routine: she would either slumber or spar with others. If she wasn’t napping in the starry sky, she was bullying someone. If she wasn’t bullying someone, she was sleeping.

After meeting the Immortal Monarch, Dragon Goddess Yeyou only slumbered whenever the Immortal Monarch entered seclusion, and she would only pick fights with those who tried to get too close to him.

It was quite obvious that Dragon Goddess Yeyou fancied the Immortal Monarch, but nobody was bold enough to say it out loud—not even the other Divine Supremes.

After spending several hours on Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s information, Xi Meili went on to read about the Primordial Phoenix.

“The Primordial Phoenix was perhaps the only divine beast who walked amongst the humans before the Immortal Monarch’s influence. She had her own business, selling treasures she acquired, amassing a fortune rivaling even the Great One, who was known to hoard treasures like a madman.”

“The Primordial Phoenix didn’t spend as much time as the others with the Immortal Monarch and only showed up occasionally whenever she got bored or was needed, like that time when the Immortal Monarch visited the Phoenix Clan.”

“The Primordial Phoenix is highly competitive and is often seen butting heads with Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

Eater of Stars was next on Xi Meili’s reading list, but her shelf was quite empty when compared to the others, with one exception—the ninth member of the Nine Divine Supreme.

Most of the information on the Eater of Stars was about her past before she met the Immortal Monarch. Besides Dragon Goddess Yeyou, she spent the most time with the Immortal Monarch—to the point where it was nearly impossible to see the Immortal Monarch without her somewhere around him.

“The Eater of Stars was especially protective of the Immortal Monarch. If she perceived any threat to him, no matter how minor, she would immediately become aggressive, ready to unleash her wrath on anyone who dared to harm him.”

Nobody but the Nine Divine Supreme knew this, but the Immortal Monarch kept her hunger satisfied with his own blood, so it was only natural for the Eater of Stars to be protective of him since he was the reason she could live a normal life.

After finishing the scrolls on Eater of Stars, Crimson Terror was next.

“Despite the nickname he was given, Crimson Terror wasn’t born a killer. In fact, he disliked violence and would always find a method to avoid it. Unfortunately, in order to protect his own clan from the greedy humans, he was forced to kill and kill until one day, he lost his mind and became nothing but a crazed killer.”

“If the Immortal Monarch hadn’t snapped him out of it, the Crimson Terror would’ve inflicted significant damage not to just human society but the beast clans, as well.”

After being tamed by the Immortal Monarch, Crimson Terror traveled the world in his human form to fix the damage he’d done to the innocent.

“Up next, we have the Heavenly White Tiger God…”

Just as Xi Meili reached for a scroll, the door opened, and Xuan Kun walked inside.

“How’s everything so far?” he asked them.

“I’ve learned a lot of new things from here, and we’re just getting started,” Yuan said.

“I’m glad. Anyway, I have a new guest who needs my attention, so I will not be available for a little.”

“This guest must be fairly important.” Xi Meili causally said.

“Yes… this individual is not only from the upper heavens, but she’s also from the Immortal Clans that are closely related to the Immortal Monarch.”

“Someone from the Immortal Clans?” Yuan raised an eyebrow, his interest piqued.

“Which clan is she from?”

“The Heavenly White Tiger Clan.”

Yuan narrowed his eyes at this information.

‘Could it be her?’

Yuan recalled the woman who showed up to protect Bai Xutao.

Xi Meili also had similar thoughts.

‘For her to show up in this place, she must be looking for information…’ Yuan thought to himself.

“Then, I’ll let you return to your study now.” Xuan Kun closed the door and went downstairs to meet this individual while Yuan and Xi Meili returned to reading scrolls.

Even if this individual turned out to be the same woman they met, they had no reason to be in her business and decided to just ignore it for the time being.

chapters.title: Chapter 1560 Bai Ning
chapters.body:
Chapter 1560 Bai Ning

Chapter 1560 Bai Ning

1560 Bai Ning

Once Xuan Kun arrived at the reception area, he could see a beautiful young woman standing with Xuan Wei, who had a respectful smile on his face, a stark contrast to the way he greeted Yuan and Xi Meili.

“Welcome to the Pavilion of Knowledge, Lady Bai Ning. How can we assist you today?” Xuan Kun respectfully bowed to her.

Bai Ning looked at him nonchalantly and said, “I heard that this is the place to go for information. Is that correct?”

“That depends on the type of information you’re looking for, but generally, yes. We have information on pretty much everything, including information that is normally only available in the upper heavens.” Xuan Kun spoke with confidence.

The Pavilion of Knowledge gathered information from not only the Fourth Heaven but also all the other Heavens. They achieved this by contacting other information-gathering agencies in the upper heavens and sharing information with each other.

“Good. I am looking for someone,” Bai Ning then said.

She continued as Xuan Kun raised an eyebrow, “This individual has long golden hair and golden eyes, a unique scent that does not belong to any beast clan that I know of, but his aura did resemble that of the dragon clan slightly. He wields a golden Soul Weapon shaped like a spear that can easily penetrate spiritual energy. He might have a habit of wearing masks.”

“Oh, he’s also immensely talented, being able to fight even Spirit Enlightenment despite being a mere Spirit King.”

Xuan Kun wore a look of disbelief on his face after hearing the description of someone who sounded like pure fiction to him.

“A Spirit King that can fight Spirit Enlightenment? That doesn’t sound feasible at all…” Xuan Wei subconsciously uttered this after hearing such a ridiculous claim.

Bai Ning narrowed her eyes at him and sneered, “Are you calling me a liar?”

“N-not at all! But how can you be sure this information is correct?”

“Because I encountered and fought him myself in the Third Heaven. Not only did he match my strength, but he also humiliated my little brother, who had to unseal his cultivation to Spirit Enlightenment. Even then, it wasn’t enough.”

“A Spirit King from the Third Heaven was able to match you? That sounds ludicrous, but since it’s coming from you…” Xuan Kun swallowed nervously.

He began pondering seriously, but nobody that fit the description came to mind.

‘There’s no way a genius like that would go unnoticed. In that case, he must be a newcomer…’

“Do you have any more information on this individual?” Xuan Kun asked.

Bai Ning recalled every little detail Bai Xutao told her to Xuan Kun, who listened with great interest. 𝖋𝔯𝔢𝔢𝖜𝖊𝖇𝖓𝔬𝔳𝖊𝔩.𝖈𝖔𝖒

“I see… Truly a fascinating individual… As for that spear you mentioned…”

Once Xuan Kun was given more details on the Soul Weapon wielded by this individual, he immediately thought about the Dragon’s Soul, something the Xi Family had in their possession even before they entered the Ancient Dragon City.

‘A Soul Weapon that sounds like the Dragon’s Soul, and with the Young Lady’s recent emergence… is this just a coincidence, or…?’ Xuan Kun wondered inwardly, not daring to rush any conclusions.

“Oh, one more thing. This masked individual had some weird technique. Right when I was about to strike him, he shouted ‘halt’ at me in a commanding tone, and as though I was his servant, my body obeyed even though my mind rejected it.” Bai Ning suddenly said.

“What?” Xuan Kun frowned upon hearing this, and he asked, “Can you repeat that one more time? And how you felt in more detail?”

Bai Ning closed her eyes and recalled the moment her body stopped working.

“With a single word, that individual was able to take complete control of my body, forcing it to listen to his commands regardless of my own will. If he told me to kill myself at that moment, I would’ve probably done so. I have never felt so powerless in my life…” Bai Ning wore a nervous expression on her face as she recalled the sensation of being powerless, something she had never experienced since birth.

Xuan Kun swallowed nervously and took a deep breath before speaking, “I don’t want to startle you or anything, Lady Bai, but what you described sounds exactly like an authority—

something only the purest of royal bloodlines can wield.”

“What?” Bai Ning frowned upon hearing this and said, “You mean to tell me this individual has the authority to command the Heavenly White Tiger’s bloodline when we are already the purest of our bloodline? That’s nonsense!”

The Heavenly White Tiger Clan already stood at the peak of their bloodline, so it was impossible for another bloodline to have the authority to command them.

“In that case, I can only think of one possibility, even though it may sound even more ridiculous than the last…” Xuan Kun’s body trembled at this thought.

“What is it?” Bai Ning urged.

After taking a deep breath, Xuan Kun muttered in a shivering voice, “Immortal Monarch’s Dominion… That’s the only authority that could possibly surpass the Heavenly White Tiger’s authority.”

“Are you insane?!” Bai Ning suddenly roared, her voice filled with anger.

“You dare suggest that the Immortal Monarch is still alive and that the person I fought was him?!”

Just the thought of raising a finger against the Immortal Monarch left Bai Ning in utter disgust and anger, as she would rather die than do such a thing.

“Even if the Immortal Monarch were alive, he wouldn’t be a Spirit King! Moreover, why would the Immortal Monarch be bullying my little brother?!”

After Bai Xutao’s fight with Yuan, Bai Ning brought Bai Xutao, who was half dead after the heavenly tribulation, back to her family, where he had to explain everything. However, Bai Xutao gave a fake story, so Bai Ning was still unaware of the whole situation and thought Yuan was just targeting the Heavenly White Tiger Clan for whatever reason.

“I know how ridiculous it sounds, but it’s the only explanation. Furthermore…” Xuan Kun hesitated for a moment before continuing, “The Immortal Monarch… he’s alive…”

“What… did you just say?” Bai Ning and Xuan Wei stared at him with wide eyes, their faces filled with shock.

chapters.title: Chapter 1561 Bai Ning(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1561 Bai Ning(2)

Chapter 1561 Bai Ning(2)

1561 Bai Ning(2)

Bai Ning’s entire being froze like a stone statue after being told that the Immortal Monarch was alive. Her body trembled after a moment of silence, and she suddenly reached for Xuan Kun’s collars.

Once she got ahold of his robes, she lifted him into the air and roared, “I swear to god, if you’re messing with me!”

Xuan Kun was startled by her sudden aggression and hastily said, “I-it’s true! This information has already been verified through the Heavenly Scroll of Validity! The chances of this information being fake are almost zero!”

“Who? Who gave you this information!?” she growled.

Xuan Kun hesitated to reveal this information since the Pavilion of Knowledge prided itself on its confidentiality. If he revealed a customer’s name, it would set a dangerous precedent and, if exposed, would sink their reputation to the bottom of the sea.

“Please forgive me, but I cannot reveal a client’s information, especially if that individual may have a connection with the Immortal Monarch. I beg for your understanding, Lady Bai.” Xuan Kun said, his body soaked in cold sweat.

“…”

Bai Ning froze again and, after a moment of silence, released Xuan Kun and said, “My apologies. I got a little too excited after learning of the information. You’re right. This individual likely has connections with the Immortal Monarch to have such valuable information. I almost made a rash judgment there.”

Xuan Kun sighed in relief and said, “Thank you for your understanding. Although I cannot reveal this individual’s information to you, I can speak with him and see if he would be willing to meet you.”

Bai Ning’s eyes flickered after hearing this, and immediately nodded, “Please do! I must speak with this individual, no matter what! If there’s anything I can do that would increase the chances of meeting him, let me know!”

Xuan Kun pondered for a moment before responding, “I’m not sure, but he seemed very interested in information related to the Immortal Monarch. If you have information to share with him, he’d have a reason to speak with you.”

“Information on the Immortal Monarch, right? I have plenty of that.” Bai Ning quickly agreed.

As an Immortal Monarch enthusiast, Bai Ning collected all sorts of information on the Immortal Monarch, and being part of an Immortal Clan, she has access to information not available to the public.

“What about the individual you wanted to find?” Xuan Wei suddenly asked.

“I can deal with him later. This takes priority,” she said.

“Then I’ll be back. You can rest in the guest room in the meantime.” Xuan Kun said.

“Hm? You make it sound like you’ll be able to contact this person soon.”

“Well… he is currently in the Pavilion of Knowledge, after all…”

“What did you say?!” Bai Ning was pleasantly surprised by this news.

“You don’t mean…” Xuan Wei swallowed nervously as he thought of their recent rude guests.

Xuan Kun left shortly after, leaving Xuan Wei and Bai Ning alone.

Sometime later, Xuan Kun opened the door to Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch.

“Young Master, I apologize for disturbing you, but the guest from the Heavenly White Tiger Clan would like to meet you. Due to her background, I shared with her the information you told me.” Xuan Kun said with an apologetic look on his face.

“She wants to meet me, huh?” Yuan didn’t immediately give an answer.

“She said she’s willing to share information on the Immortal Monarch if you agree to meet with her. Since she’s from an Immortal Clan, her information would undoubtedly be valuable.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before nodding his head, “Sure, I’ll go meet with her.”

“Thank you!” Xuan Kun bowed to him, feeling relieved.

Xi Meili placed the scroll in her hands down and asked through divine sense, “Should I come with you?”

“No, I can speak with her alone. She might also recognize you.” Yuan said.

“Alright.” Xi Meili nodded.

Yuan left the room while Xi Meili continued to read the scrolls.

Sometime later, Xuan Kun brought Yuan to Bai Ning.

The moment Bai Ning saw Yuan, she raised an eyebrow. She didn’t recognize Yuan but felt like they had met somewhere.

“Hello, I am Bai Ning of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan. How should I address you?” Knowing that he might be connected to the Immortal Monarch, Bai Ning spoke to him in a respectful tone.

“Yuan,” he said.

“I heard you wanted to speak with me. What is this about?” he then asked.

“I heard from Xuan Kun here that you were the one who told him the Immortal Monarch is alive. If you don’t mind me asking, how did you acquire this information? I will share with you everything I know about the Immortal Monarch.
