In case you don’t know, the Heavenly White Tiger God, our ancestor, was one of the Nine Divine Supremes that followed the Immortal Monarch, so we know more than most.”

“I don’t mind telling you, but can you tell me what you plan on doing with that information first?” Yuan said.

“What I plan on doing…?” Bai Ning raised an eyebrow, as she hadn’t thought about it yet.

“I-if the Immortal Monarch is truly alive, I want to meet him…” she responded in a low voice after a moment of silence.

“And what are you going to do once you meet him?” Yuan continued to ask.

“I will serve him just as the Nine Divine Supreme had! That is my dream!” she answered with a serious expression on her face.

“And what if he refuses?”

“That’s…” Bai Ning was left speechless.

“I haven’t thought that far, but I will not give up even if it’s the last thing I do!”

Yuan remained silent for a moment before speaking, “If you want to meet the Immortal Monarch that badly, I can let you meet him.”

“What?! Really?! You know where the Immortal Monarch is?!” Bai Ning’s eyes widened with shock.

“Yes, but you’ll have to do something for me.”

“I will do anything!” she answered without hesitation.

“We’ll see about that,” Yuan said with a mysterious smile on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1562 Vial Of Blood
chapters.body:
Chapter 1562 Vial Of Blood

Chapter 1562 Vial of Blood

1562 Vial of Blood

“So, what do you need me to do?” Bai Ning urged in an impatient manner.

Yuan calmly said, “You have a brother, right?”

“Yes… I have several younger brothers, in fact. Why are you asking?” Bai Ning raised her eyebrows with a confused look.

“I am looking for one of them. According to what I was told, he wields a pair of gauntlets.” Yuan said, pretending he didn’t know Bai Xutao.

“Gaunlet… I know three of them that wield them. Any more specifics?”

“I heard he came down to the lower heaven for some mission.”

“Oh, then you must be talking about Bai Xutao, our youngest. Why are you looking for him?”

“I met him not long ago and couldn’t finish our conversation because he had to leave suddenly. I’d like to speak with him and repay a little debt I owe him.” Yuan said with a calm smile on his face.

“Is that so…” Bai Ning’s face became a little nervous as she continued, “Unfortunately, Bai Xutao was heavily injured not long ago, so I had to send him back home to the upper heavens, so you might have to wait until he recovers.”

“Heavily injured in the lower heavens? What happened to him?” Yuan acted surprised to hear this information.

“He was viciously beaten by this masked individual.”

“What? Why would this individual do such a horrible thing?” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock.

“My brother claimed it was a random attack—that he has no idea why he was being attacked. However, Bai Xutao is a troublemaker, so I wouldn’t be surprised if he offended someone he shouldn’t have. This individual was insanely talented and powerful… there’s no way someone like that would attack Bai Xutao without reason.”

“This is also why I came to the Pavilion of Knowledge—to find this individual who attacked my younger brother.” Bai Ning clenched her fists in frustration.

In her head, she couldn’t care less about avenging Bai Xutao. The real reason she wanted to find this individual was to fight him again and to learn about his mysterious power.

“What will you do once you find this individual?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

“Well, I am going to find out the real reason he attacked Bai Xutao, but regardless of the reason, he’d nearly crippled someone from the Heavenly White Tiger Clan, so he will need to pay for his crimes. I will bring him back to my family so they can deal with him.” Bai Ning said.

“I see… I hope you find this individual and find justice for Bai Xutao.” Yuan said.

“As for meeting with the Immortal Monarch, he’s not in the condition to meet anyone right now, anyway. I’ll let you meet him in the future.”

“What?! Is he okay?! You make it sound like he’s injured or something!” Bai Ning’s face darkened, acting like a mother about her missing child. 𝖋𝖗𝖊𝖊𝖜𝔢𝔟𝖓𝖔𝔳𝔢𝔩.𝔠𝖔𝔪

“It’s complicated, but you’ll find out in the future. Anyway, can you share your information on the Immortal Monarch with me?”

After a moment of silence, Bai Ning nodded her head, and she retrieved a jade slip from her spatial ring before handing it to him.

“This is my personal logbook. I logged all of my information on the Immortal Monarch here. You can borrow it for now.”

Yuan accepted the jade slip and said, “Thank you. As a token of our new friendship and to further develop our trust in each other, I would like you to have this…”

Yuan retrieved an object from his spatial ring and offered it to her.

“This is…”

Bai Ning raised her eyebrows at the vial of blood before her with a puzzled face. Why was he giving her blood, and who does it belong to?

However, not wanting to be rude, Bai Ning accepted it without any question.

“T-thank you…?” she said in a stiff voice.

The vial was the length of her finger, and only a fourth of it contained blood.

“It was nice meeting you. Then I’ll go back upstairs now.” Yuan turned around and left before Bai Ning could question him about the blood.

Once Yuan left, Bai Ning opened the vial and took a sniff of the blood.

Her senses immediately went crazy from the sweet scent of the blood, and her body trembled uncontrollably from excitement.

“What a profound scent! Whose blood does it belong to?!” Even Xuan Kun could smell it from afar, and the scent made his mouth watery.

“I don’t know but… I really want to taste it for some reason.” Bai Ning swallowed nervously.

Usually, she’d never even think about tasting a stranger’s blood, but the sweet scent was too enticing and compelled her to do so.

“I’ll just taste a drop to see if I can identify it…” Bai Ning carefully retrieved a drop of blood from the vial with her Qi Manifestation and placed it in her mouth.

The instant the drop of blood touched her tongue, an overwhelming sensation surged through Bai Ning’s entire being, as if she had been touched by the divine itself, baptized in an ethereal purity that transcended all comprehension.

“Good heavens!” she cried out loud and fell to her knees, nearly dropping the vial of blood in the process.

“W-what the?! Lady Bai! Are you okay?! Did that bastard poison you?!” Xuan Wei exclaimed in a terrified voice after seeing her drastic reaction.

Bai Ning didn’t respond to him and merely stared toward the sky with a dazed look on her face.

“Lady Bai…?” Xuan Kun swallowed nervously.

“It’s… it’s so fucking delicious…” she muttered in a dumbfounded voice.

“Huh? Excuse me?”

“This otherworldly taste that’s unlike anything I’ve tasted before… this to-die-for taste… only one individual could make a divine beast fall to their knees with the delicious taste of their blood— the Immortal Monarch! This blood definitely belongs to the Immortal Monarch!!!” Bai Ning exclaimed as she came to a realization.

“T-the Immortal Monarch’s blood?!?! Impossible!” Xuan Wei cried out loud, his voice filled with disbelief.

chapters.title: Chapter 1563 Immortal Monarch’S Blood
chapters.body:
Chapter 1563 Immortal Monarch’S Blood

Chapter 1563 Immortal Monarch’s Blood

1563 Immortal Monarch’s Blood

Both Xuan Wei and Xuan Kun looked as though they just witnessed their ancestors’ ghost after hearing Bai Ning’s words.

“Lady Bai… I don’t mean to doubt you, and I apologize in advance for saying this, but could you be mistaken?” Xuan Kun said in a nervous voice.

“Mistaken? Me? Impossible! Although I have never tasted the Immortal Monarch’s blood before, I have read enough about it to recognize it if I ever tasted it! This blood is, without a shadow of a doubt, the Immortal Monarch’s blood, and I am willing to bet my life on it!” Bai Ning spoke with confidence. 𝖋𝖗𝖊𝖊𝔴𝖊𝖇𝔫𝖔𝖛𝖊𝖑.𝖈𝖔𝖒

“If you’re that certain…” Xuan Kun stared at the vial of blood and swallowed nervously.

He didn’t wholly believe Bai Ning, but it wasn’t as though he could make her use the Scroll of Validity, as that would basically tell her he didn’t trust her. However, if there’s even one percent that the blood genuinely belonged to the Immortal Monarch, he wanted it!

“Lady Bai… this may be imprudent of me, and allow me to apologize in advance again, but…”

“No.” Bai Ning responded before he could even finish his sentence.

She turned to glare at him and continued, “You were going to ask me to give you some of this blood, right? I refuse. I don’t care what you have to offer me. I won’t give you even a single drop.”

Xuan Kun began sweating profusely at her words, but he still remained hopeful, “I beg you, Lady Bai… You have about a dozen drops of blood inside that vial… Can’t you just spare a single drop for this old man? It would mean the world to me!”

Bai Ning sneered and said, “I’ll think about it if you can find me the individual who attacked Bai Xutao.”

Xuan Kun’s jaw dropped. After a moment of silence, he sighed out loud, “I’ll be blunt, Lady Bai. It’s almost impossible to find someone like that with so little information. The only thing we know about this individual is that he’s a genius who can fight multiple realms above his own cultivation and that he wields a golden spear that can penetrate spiritual energy.”

“And there’s a high probability that he’s from the dragon clan.” Bai Ning quickly added.

Xuan Kun sighed again, “I will ask my informants in the Third Heaven to see if they have any information. It might take a couple of days for a response.”

“That’s fine. I planned on staying here a little longer, anyway.” Bai Ning nodded as she looked at the upper floors of the tower.

Meanwhile, Yuan returned to Xi Meili’s side.

“How’d it go? Did she recognize you?” Xi Meili asked.

He shook his head, “No, but I got the feeling that she finds me familiar.”

“What did you talk about? It was over pretty fast, too.”

“It wasn’t much. She asked me how I knew about the Immortal Monarch being alive.”

“So, did you lie to her?”

“No, I didn’t lie. I didn’t even tell her anything.” He casually shrugged.

“Huh?” Xi Meili raised an eyebrow.

“She stopped caring about that when I told her that I could set up a meeting with the Immortal Monarch for her.”

“And you intend on keeping your word?”

“Of course, but she’ll need to do something for me first. I wasn’t able to kill Bai Xutao back then, so I’m giving her the chance to set up another meeting for me.”

Xi Meili’s jaw dropped at his words.

“And then what? You’ll reveal your true identity after killing Bai Xutao in front of her?”

“Pretty much.”

“That’s… pretty cruel. I wonder how she’ll react,” she muttered.

Yuan smiled and said, “I get the feeling that she’d do anything for the Immortal Monarch. I don’t doubt she’d even kill her own brother if the Immortal Monarch told her to.”

“Then you might as well do just that. At least that’ll save you some energy.”

“No, I won’t feel satisfied unless I squeeze Bai Xutao’s life away with my own hands for attacking you and Feng Feng.”

“Are you sure you want to do that? He’s a descendant of the Heavenly White Tiger God, one of the Nine Divine Supremes. I won’t blame you for forgiving him.”

Xi Meili wondered if he’d feel bad about killing one of his follower’s descendants.

“It’s not like he’s the Heavenly White Tiger God’s son—or even grandson. If the Heavenly White Tiger God were here, he’d probably kill Bai Xutao before I could even get to him.” Yuan shook his head.

“You make a fair point…” Xi Meili mumbled with a look of realization.

“Anyway, I’ll deal with it when we get there. For now, let’s check out the information that the woman gave me for meeting her.” Yuan retrieved the jade slip and began reading its contents.

Half of the information in the jade slip talked about the Heavenly White Tiger God’s achievements during his time as one of the Nine Divine Supremes. The other half was mostly scattered information about the Immortal Monarch, such as potential hobby, his height, his woman type, and how delicious his blood tasted.

“There’s not much in here that I don’t already know, but there is some useful information about the Heavenly White Tiger God and their Heavenly White Tiger Clan,” Yuan muttered after reading all the information, feeling as if he had just perused the diary of a love-stricken teenager. The text was filled with effusive praises for the Immortal Monarch, offering more adulation than substantial information.

“Most of the information left in this room are rumors rather than actual facts,” said Xi Meili.

Yuan nodded and said, “Then let’s read through them quickly and leave.”

A few hours later, a familiar voice resounded inside Yuan’s head, “My liege, I have found this immortal you’re looking for.”

“Oh? Where are they?”

“They are actually very close to your current location, hiding on a remote island in the middle of the Purple Sea.” Dong Ye said.

“Perfect.” Yuan smiled at this information.

chapters.title: Chapter 1564 Meeting The Escaped Immortal
chapters.body:
Chapter 1564 Meeting The Escaped Immortal

Chapter 1564 Meeting the Escaped Immortal

1564 Meeting the Escaped Immortal

After learning that the escaped immortal was close to his location, Yuan said to Xi Meili, “Let’s stop here. Something else came up.”

Worried that the immortal might leave before he could finish the scrolls in this place, Yuan decided to deal with the immortal first, then return to the Pavilion of Knowledge to finish the rest if possible.

“Huh? What came up?” Xi Meili lifted her head to look at him with a raised eyebrow.

Since Dong Ye spoke to Yuan through Divine Sense, she had no idea about their conversation.

Yuan gave her a quick brief of his situation, “There is a place called the Nine Heaven Eternal Grotto that imprisons some of the most dangerous immortals in the world. A few of them escaped recently, and one of them managed to enter the Fourth Heaven. That immortal happens to be close by, so I am going to kill them real quick.”

“What?! An immortal?!” Xi Meili was shocked speechless by such information.

“You make it sound like killing an immortal is a simple thing to do! They are so powerful that even a Divine Ancestor would not last a single strike from them! And they won’t die even if you destroy their body!” Xi Meili exclaimed.

“I doubt I will be able to kill an immortal in their perfect condition. This immortal is greatly weakened due to being in the Fourth Heaven, so I have a good chance of defeating them.”

“Even if their cultivation is weakened, they can still use powerful techniques and Immortal Qi, which mortals cannot resist!”

Yuan confidently smiled and said, “I know it’s dangerous, but I have to do this. You should stay here. I don’t want you to get caught up in our fight.”

“No! Let me fight with you!” Xi Meili quickly shook her head.

Yuan sighed, “That’s not possible, and you should know that. I won’t have the leisure to protect you during our fight, either.”

Xi Meili clenched her jaws tightly in frustration, but she couldn’t argue with Yuan. She knew that she would die long before she could even react if she fought with an immortal.

After a moment of silence, she sighed out loud, “In this entire universe, you’re probably the only mortal who would dare to fight an immortal. But that’s also what I like about you. Don’t die on me, Yuan.”

“I promise you that I’ll return.” Yuan nodded with a serious face.

He left the room shortly after while Xi Meili remained inside.

“Are you leaving already?” Yuan encountered Xuan Kun on his way out.

“I’ll be leaving for a little, but I’ll be back. Xi Meili will stay here.” Yuan said.

“Is that so… by the way… that vial of blood you gave to Lady Bai… do you have more? Even a single drop is enough. If so, I’d like to purchase it from you!”

Xuan Kun decided to try his luck with Yuan since it might be impossible to get Bai Ning to part with her supply.

Yuan shook his head and said, “Sorry, but that’s all I have.”

“Is that so…” Xuan Kun wore a dejected expression on his face, looking like he’d been rejected by the love of his life.

After leaving the Pavilion of Knowledge, Yuan asked Dong Ye for directions to the escaped immortal.

“If you fly southwest, you should arrive at a small island in three days. The escaped immortal is hiding there.” Dong Ye said.

“Alright.”

Without hesitation, Yuan started flying toward the southwest.

Two and a half days later, he could see a small island on the horizon.

This island had a small mountain on it, and it was surrounded by purple trees.

Yuan didn’t want to alert the immortal with his divine sense, so he restrained from using it on the island.

However, Dong Ye was a different story, as his divine sense would be too powerful for the immortal to sense.

“Where is he?” Yuan asked.

“Hidden in one of the mountain caves. They’ve hidden their presence and are trying to recover spiritual energy. If you want to fight them, now is the best time.” Dong Ye said.

Yuan nodded and took a moment to prepare himself.

After taking a deep breath, he retrieved Starry Abyss before releasing his aura. He purposefully avoided using Number One Under Heaven to see how much he could handle without her.

“Come out!” he roared.

The instant the immortal sensed Yuan’s presence and powerful aura, they immediately thought about escaping. However, they stopped when they realized that Yuan was alone and was a mere Spirit King. After a moment of pondering, the immortal emerged from their hiding spot to confront him.

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the immortal’s appearance, shrouded by a mysterious mist, looking like a cloud that could block divine sense.

‘If Divine Sense doesn’t work, let’s try Divine Gaze…’

Yuan used Divine Gaze, and to his surprise, he could see through the mist, but the immortal’s true appearance remained as a silhouette.

The figure inside the mist narrowed their eyes after sensing Yuan’s Divine Gaze.

‘A mere Spirit King using Divine Gaze only those in the Divine Realm can use? Who is this kid?’ the immortal wondered inwardly.

“Hey, why don’t you show me your real appearance?” Yuan suddenly spoke.

“And why would I do that?” The immortal spoke in a hoarse voice.

“I’m just curious. I want to see my opponent before fighting them.”

“You want to fight me…? A mere mortal? Do you even know who I am?” The immortal expected many things from Yuan, but a fight was not one of them. They were also confident that Yuan was not suppressing his cultivation and was a genuine Spirit King.

“Yes, I do. You’re an immortal who escaped the Nine Heaven Eternal Grotto, right?”

“And you still want to fight me despite knowing that…? Why are you trying to kill yourself?” The immortal couldn’t believe what they were hearing.

“Who said anything about wanting to die? You may be an immortal, but your cultivation is currently restricted to the Divine King realm.” Yuan smiled.

The immortal was speechless. They couldn’t fathom how Yuan could talk so confidently before someone several realms above his own strength.

“To think I’d be looked down upon by a mere mortal… hahaha!” The immortal suddenly laughed out loud.

“Very well… I shall teach you the difference between a mortal and an immortal today… at the cost of your insignificant life!” The immortal roared, his aura skyrocketing the next second.

chapters.title: Chapter 1565 Fighting The Escaped Immortal
chapters.body:
Chapter 1565 Fighting The Escaped Immortal

Chapter 1565 Fighting the Escaped Immortal

1565 Fighting the Escaped Immortal

When the Immortal released their cultivation, Yuan barely felt its pressure. However, they didn’t release all of it, and it only went up to Spirit Enlightenment.

The Immortal never intended to fight Yuan with their full power, thinking it would be an overkill. Moreover, they were still hiding from the pursuers from the upper heavens. If they released their full cultivation, the pursuers would definitely find them in a heartbeat.

‘There’s no need to expose my location just to kill a mere mortal! I’ll end this before he can even realize what had killed him!’ The Immortal laughed inwardly before they flew at Yuan with immense speed.

Seeing that the Immortal had no intention of fighting him with their full strength, Yuan smiled and said, “You’ll also regret underestimating me.”

Although the Immortal was going easy on him, Yuan didn’t have the leisure to go easy on the Immortal.

The Immortal, still with the appearance of a floating cloud, flew at Yuan.

Yuan immediately used Divine Gaze to see how the Immortal was going to attack him.

Sha!

As though the Immortal had turned into a porcupine, hundreds of needles made entirely of spiritual energy burst out of the cloud and flew toward Yuan.

<Ten Thousand Ghost Blades!>

Yuan cloned the Starry Abyss 10,000 times and used them to deflect the needles while attacking simultaneously.

“Cheap tricks!”

The Immortal roared and released some more of their aura, which was strong enough to block the Starry Abyss and, like an invisible wall, halted all of the attacks.

Yuan narrowed his eyes slightly at the sight of this.

‘Unlike Number One Under Heaven and Dragon’s Soul, the Starry Abyss is too weak to do anything. Even if I managed to hit him, it wouldn’t do any damage.’

Due to its lack of power, Yuan retrieved the Starry Abyss.

‘I definitely need to take some time strengthening the Starry Abyss after this…’ he thought to himself as he wielded Dragon’s Soul next.

“Two Soul Weapons?” The Immortal narrowed their eyes at Dragon’s Soul, surprised that a mortal could wield two Soul Weapons. They could also feel an unfathomable aura emitted by Dragon’s Soul.

Yuan used his movement technique to instantly close their distance, approaching the Immortal without hesitation.

Once he was close enough, he began thrusting Dragon’s Soul at the Immortal.

“!!!”

The Immortal was greatly shocked when the Dragon’s Soul touched their mist and destroyed it with ease.

‘Fuck! What the hell is that Soul Weapon?!’ The Immortal was left terrified after seeing their unique mist being destroyed when it was indestructible to weapons.

‘To think he’d have a weapon that could cut through my mist! What fucking bad luck!’ The Immortal cursed inwardly after realizing they’d met their worst enemy.

“Looks like this weapon is effective on you.” Yuan smiled after seeing how far the Immortal had backed up.

“So what? In the end, it’s still useless if you can’t hit my actual body.” The Immortal sneered.

“We’ll see about that.”

Yuan went on the offensive while the Immortal focused on avoiding his Dragon’s Soul, occasionally throwing powerful spiritual attacks at Yuan.

After just several exchanges, it was clear to Yuan that this Immortal was a cultivator who focused on spiritual techniques instead of using weapons like the majority of cultivators out there.

‘What the hell is up with this mortal human?! He’s just a mere Spirit King, yet he fights like he has countless years of combat experience!’ The Immortal was bewildered by how skillful Yuan was in combat.

None of his movements were wasted, and his decisions seemed instant, even reading several seconds ahead.

The Immortal had fought tens of thousands of Immortals even before they were sent to the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto, but they’d never fought someone half as experienced and skillful as Yuan.

‘My cultivation may be superior, but this bastard’s combat skills are unfathomably high! It’s like I am fighting someone in the God Ascension Realm!’ The Immortal began doubting whether Yuan was actually a Spirit King or actually a God Ascension Realm cultivator in disguise.

Yuan’s combat experience through the God of War helped him narrow the gap between him and the Immortal, but it was far from enough.

“Enough!” The Immortal suddenly roared, and their cultivation entered the Divine Realm, becoming a Divine Warrior.

The Immortal’s speed and overall prowess increased significantly after the boost. Yet, it still wasn’t enough to overwhelm Yuan.

‘Is this guy really human?! How is he fighting me while only having the cultivation of a Spirit King?! He should’ve died long ago!’ The Immortal was baffled by Yuan’s nonsensical existence.

There were plenty of unique and heaven-defying immortals inside the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto, but none of them were as ridiculous as Yuan, who could fight several realms above his own with ease.

Usually, one would be considered talented if they could fight someone one or two levels higher, a genius if they could battle someone at least three levels above, and only a true prodigy would be able to challenge an opponent an entire realm higher.

Yuan was currently fighting someone four realms above his own. Not only that, but he was also fighting someone one major realm above. The difference in prowess between Spirit King and Spirit Enlightenment was insignificant compared to Spirit Enlightenment and Divine Warrior.

If the former was akin to heaven and earth, the latter was akin to Earth and Nine Heavens.

Meanwhile, unbeknownst to Yuan or the Immortal, a figure was watching their fight from afar.

“So that’s the newcomer, huh? Magnificent! He truly deserves to be a General with his capabilities, much less a member of the Celestial Overlords!” Kelan smiled as he continued spectating their fight.

“However… to defeat that Immortal will require much more than that, Junior…”

A few moments later, the Immortal increased their cultivation by another level, entering Divine Master.

“The difference between Divine Warrior and Divine Master is greater than Spirit King and Divine Warrior. How will you deal with this now?” Kelan mumbled as he prepared to save Yuan at any moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 1566 Fighting The Escaped Immortal(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1566 Fighting The Escaped Immortal(2)

Chapter 1566 Fighting the Escaped Immortal(2)

1566 Fighting the Escaped Immortal(2)

“Divine Master, huh? Finally, some challenge.” Yuan smiled upon seeing the Immortal’s increased cultivation.

“That arrogance won’t last long!” The Immortal roared before they rushed at Yuan.

Just the pressure from the Immortal’s aura alone would kill an ordinary Spirit King. However, Yuan had the Golden Immortal Physique, as well as a Sword Body, so he barely felt it.

“Shrouded Illusion!”

The mist around the Immortal suddenly began spreading, quickly covering a hundred miles around them.

While inside the mist, Yuan’s Divine Sense and eyes were rendered useless. The only thing he could rely on was his Divine Gaze and instincts.

Suddenly, as though a massive hoard of monsters appeared, Yuan could hear loud noises approaching him from a distance. Soon, thousands of silhouettes appeared, crashing toward him like a wave.

Each individual silhouette had a distinct aura and presence, making them feel incredibly real, almost indistinguishable from reality.

Without hesitation, Yuan fiercely swung Dragon’s Soul, instantly destroying the hoard of silhouettes and scattering the mist like smoke.

However, the mist quickly restored itself, and another illusion appeared shortly after.

Moreover, the mist started transforming into a background until the mists were no longer visible, and Yuan quickly found himself in the middle of a bustling city.

“Chicken skewers here! Come get your chicken skewers!”

“Free newspapers! Free newspapers!”

“Mom, I am tired. Let’s go home already.”

The pedestrians spoke and acted like real humans. The smell of chicken skewers reached Yuan’s nose, and he could even feel the heat coming from the blazing sun in the sky. It was almost as if he’d been teleported to an actual city. However, Yuan’s expression remained nonchalant.

‘This is quite a powerful illusion… I wouldn’t blame anyone for falling to it, ‘ he thought.

If one had weak mental strength, one would quickly lose oneself in the illusion and fail to distinguish fake from reality.

Yuan closed his eyes and took a deep breath. A second later, when he opened them again, he could no longer see the people or the city, and the mist was visible once again.

Moreover, several spiritual attacks were launched in his direction.

Despite seeing the attacks and having plenty of time to avoid them, Yuan stood still and allowed the attacks to strike his body.

The impact from the attacks was strong enough to blow the mist field away. Yet, Yuan remained still, and his body was only slightly injured.

By the time the Immortal even realized Yuan had survived the attack, his injuries had already healed completely.

“Heavens! He took several Divine Master’s attacks with his bare body and took virtually no damage! What is his body made of?!” Kelan’s eyes widened with shock after witnessing this.

Despite being a Body Refiner himself, Kelan could not imagine the extent to which Yuan had tempered his physique to be able to achieve such a ridiculous feat.

Although the Immortal’s face wasn’t visible, Yuan could tell they were greatly shocked by his durability.

Not only did Yuan casually shrug off their powerful illusion, but he even ate several of their spiritual attacks with his bare body and came out unscathed.

The Immortal were beginning to wonder whether they could defeat Yuan without using their full strength. However, even if they managed to kill Yuan, they would have to deal with their pursuers afterward.

‘Damn it all! Why did I have to encounter such a fucking monster in the Fourth Heaven?! My luck is rotten to the core!’ The Immortal cried inwardly. They’d managed to escape from the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto to the Fourth Heaven without feeling half as cornered as they were currently, and it was all due to a single mortal.

‘Fuck it! Let’s just quickly kill him and hope my pursuers aren’t so close that I won’t be able to escape!’

The Immortal increased their cultivation once again, reaching all the way to the peak of Divine Grandmaster.

The aura being emitted by the Immortal now was at least four times stronger than before.

Sensing the tyrannical aura coming from the Immortal, Yuan knew that even his godly body would not be able to endure a single hit from the Immortal.

The difference between Spirit Master and Spirit Grandmaster was simply too massive for him to ignore.

‘I guess it’s finally time to fight seriously.’

For the first time during their fight, Yuan equipped his equipment, from the Stygian Vest to the Stygian Boots.

Indeed, Yuan had been empty-handed this entire time while fighting the Immortal.

The moment Yuan equipped his equipment, his aura changed drastically.

The Immortal frowned at this and, without hesitation, increased their cultivation once again, becoming a Divine Lord.

The Stygian Vest could deflect all attacks below Divine Lord, but that ability was rendered useless the moment the Immortal became a Divine Lord. However, Yuan was using it for its life-saving skill, so it was still useful.

Yuan and the Immortal began their clash shortly after. The two of them moved at the speed of light, and every exchange would fill the surface of the Purple Sea with large craters.

The immense spiritual energy emitted by their fight rippled far and wide, reaching even the Pavilion of Knowledge.

After sensing the powerful ripples, Bai Ning and the others within the Pavilion of Knowledge stopped whatever they were doing and became alert.

“What powerful spiritual energy! Two Divine Realm experts must be fighting each other nearby! I need to check this out!” Bai Ning looked at Xuan Wei and said, “I’ll be back.”

Since she had nothing to do while waiting for Xuan Kun, Bai Ning decided to entertain herself with this event.

“Allow me to come with you just in case…” Xuan Wei hastily said.

“Just in case what? You’re not even qualified to protect me when you’re so weak.” Bai Ning sneered at his attempt to be a knight, as his cultivation was nowhere near hers. If anything, she would be the one protecting him.

Xuan Wei’s face flushed with embarrassment after being reminded he was weaker than her. He lowered his head in silence, and Bai Ning left the place and made her way toward Yuan.

chapters.title: Chapter 1567 Fighting The Escaped Immortal(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1567 Fighting The Escaped Immortal(3)

Chapter 1567 Fighting the Escaped Immortal(3)

1567 Fighting the Escaped Immortal(3)

‘Yuan…” Xi Meili looked toward the direction of his battle with tightly clenched fists and a nervous face. She wanted to follow him so badly, but she knew she would only get in his way and convinced herself to stay in the room.

Meanwhile, Yuan and the Immortal continued to attack each other as though their lives depended on it.

“White Lance!”

The Immortal created over ten thousand spears out of spiritual energy in the sky before launching all of them at Yuan.

Seeing this, Yuan stopped moving and focused on blocking the attack. Using Dragon’s Soul, he destroyed as many of them as he could and purposefully allowed some of them to hit his body.

Even with his godly body and Stygian Vest, the spears were powerful enough to penetrate holes into his body easily. Still, due to his regeneration ability, these holes would instantly recover, and in seconds, it was as though they never existed.

The Stygian Vest, as a treasure, also had a similar function. Unless struck by an attack strong enough to obliterate it completely in one hit, it would always recover.

The Immortal slowly increased their cultivation from the first level of Divine Lord to the peak of Divine Lord.

After an entire day of trying to kill Yuan with no results, the Immortal finally had enough and roared, “Ahhhh! I’ve had enough of you! I don’t know what trick you’re using, but I will not let this go on any longer!”

He released all of his cultivation, causing it to shoot to the peak of Divine King instantly. The Immortal’s aura was so tyrannical that Yuan felt like someone was strangling him by the neck.

The difference between Spirit King and Divine King was simply too vast, even for someone like Yuan.

Without hesitation, Yuan tossed most of his cards onto the field. After releasing a deafening roar, Yuan transformed into his dragon form. However, he didn’t feel that was enough and equipped the Celestial Visage.

“A Celestial-grade treasure! How in heaven’s name did he acquire such a treasure in the Fourth Heaven?!” Kelan’s entire being trembled after seeing Yuan pull out a treasure far beyond their current realm.

However, what shocked him the most was Yuan’s transformation.

“Is he actually half human and half beast?” he wondered out loud.

“Just what the fuck are you?! How many more bullshit are you going to pull out of your ass?!” The Immortal exclaimed after seeing Yuan’s transformation.

The Immortal was certain that Yuan was only at the Spirit King level, as he would have advanced his cultivation base long ago if he were higher. However, the fact that he hadn’t meant that this was his true cultivation. Yet, this raised more questions. How could a Spirit King be on par with a Divine King?

The difference between their cultivation was so vast that no treasure or technique could bridge the gap—at least, that’s how it should’ve been.

“I am not done yet!” Yuan suddenly said.

<God of War’s Astral Arts!>

A massive avatar appeared, its frame big enough to completely obstruct the Immortal’s view of the Purple Sea.

“That’s ridiculous!” The Immortal cried out loud.

“That’s the God of War’s Astral Art! Did Celestial Overlord Xu teach him that technique?!” Kelan cried out loud.

Compared to his previous shocks, this revelation was the most profound, shaking him to his core.

However, Yuan still had more cards to play. After putting away Dragon’s Soul, he retrieved Number One Under Heaven, instantly filling the atmosphere with an otherwordly aura.

“A third Soul Weapon!?” The Immortal was on the verge of tears. They could’ve never even dreamed of encountering such a monster in the Fourth Heaven. Hell, not even the Supreme Heaven had a monster like Yuan.

“Let’s play to our heart’s content!” Yuan spoke with a wide grin on his face as he struck first.

<Nine Supreme Swords!>

The first strike descended upon the unprepared Immortal like a falling mountain.

However, as powerful as Yuan’s attack appeared, he was still fighting a Divine King, and the Immortal was able to block it without too much difficulty.

The second strike immediately followed.

“Don’t you dare look down on me, mortal! No matter how many tricks you have up your sleeve, it won’t help you defeat me!” The Immortal roared as they blocked the second strike, then the third and fourth shortly after.

The fifth and sixth strike was also blocked, but the Immortal struggled to do so.

The seventh strike was so powerful that the Immortal barely managed to block it.

Once the eighth strike descended, the Immortal didn’t dare to block it and opted to avoid it.

The eighth strike struck the Purple Sea, splitting it in half.

“What’s wrong? Why are you running from a mortal?” Yuan provoked the Immortal as he launched the ninth sword strike.

The pressure from the ninth sword strike was nothing to scoff at. Even Kelan, who was watching from afar, felt threatened by it.

Although enraged, the Immortal didn’t succumb to Yuan’s provocation. Instead, they employed a movement technique so swift that it appeared as if they had teleported.

The ninth sword strike cleaved the Purple Sea with such force that it not only split the water in half but also gouged a profound fissure into the seabed. Two powerful tsunamis were also created from the split water, reaching the clouds.

“Let’s see if you can avoid this!”

<Limitless Sword Domain!>

Thousands of swords appeared in the sky before flying toward the Immortal, who frantically tried to avoid them. However, the swords chased after them like homing missiles.

“Each sword is overflowing with Enhanced Sword Aura! What a terrifying ability!” Kelan felt a strong urge to fight Yuan after witnessing how fierce their battle was.

“I have severely underestimated our newcomer! At this rate, he might actually be able to defeat this Immortal!” Kelan almost couldn’t believe his own words.

The thought of a mortal killing an immortal was so outrageous that even insane people wouldn’t believe it. Yet, it was slowly happening before Kelan’s eyes.

chapters.title: Chapter 1568 The Escaped Immortal’S True Appearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 1568 The Escaped Immortal’S True Appearance

Chapter 1568 The Escaped Immortal’s True Appearance

1568 The Escaped Immortal’s True Appearance 𝐟𝗿ee𝘄𝗲𝗯𝐧o𝘃el.𝐜o𝚖

However, as much as Kelan wanted Yuan to defeat the Immortal, he still expected Yuan to lose the fight.

“Although you’ve been doing good so far, the Immortal still has one more trick up their sleeve. This trick is what differentiates and truly separates mortals and immortals.” Kelan muttered after seeing how much Yuan was dominating the Immortal.

‘This is outrageous! Why does such a mortal exist in a place like the Fourth Heaven?! If only I weren’t injured by the pursuers,I could’ve killed him in seconds instead of being ridiculed like this!’ The Immortal cursed inwardly.

They had been chased nonstop since escaping the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto and were injured in the process. If it weren’t for their injury, Yuan wouldn’t have been able to push the Immortal so far.

‘This mortal… should not exist! His existence is extremely dangerous! Even if I have to sacrifice my life in the process, I will get rid of him!’

Several hours later, the Immortal’s aura suddenly changed, causing their surroundings to freeze.

From spiritual energy to the Purple Sea, everything was still, as though time itself had frozen.

‘This pressure!’ Yuan halted his movements after sensing this familiar aura.

He excitedly smiled and said, “It’s about time you use Immortal Qi. I was wondering when you’d use it.”

“I hate to admit this, but to force an immortal like me to use Immortal Qi on you, a mortal, is an unprecedented feat that I must respect.”

As the immortal spoke, the mist around them began dissipating, revealing their true appearance for the first time.

Yuan’s eyes widened with surprise when he saw the Immortal, who was much smaller than he’d anticipated.

“A child?” Yuan muttered in a daze as his gaze stared at the little boy floating before him.

The Immortal’s true form was revealed to be that of a small boy, seemingly no older than ten years. Of course, the immortal wasn’t a real child but merely bore the appearance of one. Adding to the strangeness, a short white tail protruded from his back.

“As a show of respect, I shall fight you in my true form,” said the Immortal.

“You… you’re not human?” Yuan asked him with a strange frown.

“Do I look human to you? I am of the proud Misty Fox race.” The Immortal responded in a boastful tone.

“Haa…” Yuan sighed with a genuinely disappointed look on his face. He’d gone out of his way to fight this immortal because he thought they were human.

However, now that the Immortal is revealed to be a beast, it would no longer count toward his Unique Quest, even if he were to defeat the Immortal.

The Immortal misunderstood Yuan’s reason for disappointment and roared, “Are you looking down on me because of my appearance?! This is why I refuse to show myself!”

Despite saying such words, the Immortal had to reveal himself mainly due to his injury. Concealing himself required a significant amount of energy, and he wouldn’t be able to maintain it for much longer if he also had to use Immortal Qi on top of that in such a state.

In the end, the Immortal decided to throw away his defenses to focus solely on his attack power.

Yuan shook his head and said, “No, I am not looking down on you. I thought you were human, hence why I even bothered to fight you. Now that I know you’re a beast, I don’t really have a reason to continue fighting you.”

“What kind of bullshit is that?! You disturbed my recovery and forced me to reveal myself! We’re going to fight to death even if you don’t want to!” The Immortal shouted.

“I never planned on backing out of our fight, anyway.” Yuan smiled.

“There won’t be any fight! I am going to massacre you now!” The Immortal roared before activating a technique using Immortal Qi instead of normal Qi.

“Immortal White Lance!”

The same technique from before was now multiple times stronger, and since they were created from Immortal Qi, Yuan would not be able to destroy them even using Dragon’s Soul.

Furthermore, the Immortal Qi being exuded by the Immortal made it much more difficult for Yuan to move, and his entire body was screaming from pain. In fact, his muscles were being torn apart every passing second. He was essentially a sitting duck at this moment in time.

Seeing this, Kelan prepared to save Yuan.

However, despite his seeming perilous situation, Yuan did not panic.

“Die, you monster!” The Immortal gestured, causing hundreds of Immortal White Lances to rain upon Yuan.

Yet, Yuan refused to move, much less dodge the incoming attack.

“Has he given up on living?! Fool!”

Not daring to wait until it was too late, Kelan made his move, disappearing from his spot.

A split second later, Kelan appeared in front of Yuan, who was slightly surprised by this sudden intruder.

However, before Yuan could speak or take any action, Kelan released an overwhelming aura that far surpassed the Immortal and destroyed all of the Immortal’s attacks with a mere flick of his arm, treating them like they were mere candle flickers.

The Immortal’s entire body trembled in fear when he sensed Kelan’s profound presence.

Unlike the Immortal, who had traversed to the Fourth Heaven illegally, Kelan received approval, so his cultivation wasn’t as suppressed as the Immortal. However, the Immortal was sure he would not be this newcomer’s opponent even if they had similar cultivation.

Ignoring the Immortal, Kelan turned to look at Yuan with a proud demeanor around him.

“Why did you give up at such a crucial moment, Junior? Even if you are certain of your death, as a man, you should fight until your last breath. Who knows? Maybe a miracle could’ve happened.”

Kelan expected Yuan to thank him for saving his life. Yet, Yuan had a frown on his face.

He removed the Celestial Visage so Kelan could see his frown and said coldly, “Who said I gave up? And who the hell are you? Who permitted you to interrupt our fight?”

“Huh?” Kelan muttered in a surprised tone, his expression puzzled.

chapters.title: Chapter 1569 Distraction
chapters.body:
Chapter 1569 Distraction

Chapter 1569 Distraction

1569 Distraction

Kelan was rendered speechless by Yuan’s response, and after a moment of silence, he spoke, “Junior, it’s good to be confident in yourself, but you were clearly on the brink of death. If I hadn’t saved you just now, you’d have become a human skewer.”

“Also, if you hadn’t given up, why were you frozen just now? Any onlooker would’ve assumed you were giving up in that situation.” Kelan said.

Yuan shook his head and sighed, “While I appreciate your thoughtfulness, it was unnecessary and undermined my existence. Leave. I don’t need your assistance.”

“You…” Kelan’s expression quickly turned angry. He couldn’t believe he was being lectured by the very person he had just saved.

“Is that so! Then you can go ahead and die here, you stubborn and arrogant little bastard!” Kelan left the scene the very next moment.

However, Kelan didn’t wholly leave and only pretended to do so. Even if Yuan refused his goodwill and shooed him away, he still had a duty to protect Yuan, and it was not only his promise to Elder Bai that compelled him to do so.

Yuan’s talent was simply shocking and unlike anything he’d seen before, and that fact alone was worth his protection.

‘Regardless of his character, he’s a profound existence that will significantly benefit our Celestial Overlord in the future. However, he’ll need to survive until then, and with his suicidal nature, it’s not going to be easy keeping him alive.’ Kelan sighed inwardly, feeling like an older brother trying to keep his younger siblings out of trouble even though he was hated by them.

Meanwhile, the Immortal was left speechless by what had just transpired.

‘W-who the hell was that bastard just now?! I don’t recognize him, and since he left without doing anything to me, it’s safe to assume that he’s not with the pursuers!’ The Immortal swallowed nervously.

If Kelan had been with his pursuers, he would’ve never made it to the Fourth Heaven alive. That was just how vast the difference in their strength was. Immortals are prideful creatures, so for one to acknowledge their inferiority held great significance.

“I apologize for that.” Yuan suddenly said.

“What?” The Immortal stared at him with wide eyes.

“That man’s interruption. Although it was never my intention, he disturbed our battle. Go ahead and use that technique again. I can even wait for you to recover some of your energy.” Yuan explained.

Not just the Immortal, but even Kelan was utterly speechless.

After a moment of silence, the Immortal began trembling, his face flushed red, turning into a tomato.

“STOP MOCKING ME, YOU BASTARD! DO I REALLY APPEAR THAT PATHETIC TO YOU?!”

With an enraged bellow, his aura skyrocketed.

Meanwhile, several individuals far away from the Purple Sea sensed the distinct aura of the Immortal’s Qi.

“It’s the escapee!”

“Why are they releasing so much Immortal Qi?! Are they engaged in battle with another immortal?!”

“It must be the others! But why didn’t they notify me?! They know that Immortal isn’t someone they can handle alone!”

The immortals chasing after the escaped immortal were puzzled when they were not notified, and when they contacted the others, they were shocked to learn that none of them were actually fighting the escaped immortal.

“What? None of you are fighting the escapee?! Then what’s with this immense Immortal Qi?! They wouldn’t release so much Immortal Qi for no reason!”

“Maybe the escapee is trying to lure us into a trap.”

“Do you seriously believe that?”

“Whatever the escapee is attempting, we need to capture or kill them! Let’s hurry up before they escape our grasp again!”

These immortals immediately started soaring toward the Purple Sea.

At the same time, Bai Ning was just minutes away from reaching her destination.

Unlike Kelan, Yuan could sense her approaching presence from miles away. Not wanting to expose his identity so quickly, he wore the Celestial Visage again, hiding his face.

Just as Bai Ning arrived, the Immortal activated his technique again.

“Immortal White Lance!”

Thousands of white lances filled the sky, each exuding profound Immortal Qi.

Bai Ning was shocked by the scene, but when she noticed the masked man in the middle of the battlefield, her eyes fixed on him, and she was unable to look away.

“It’s him!” she exclaimed in a bewildered voice.

Although he was wearing a different mask, she recognized him as the individual who bullied Bai Xutao and used that weird technique on her.

Bai Ning wanted to approach Yuan, but he was in a position where she could not enter, as she risked getting blown away with him by the Immortal.

“DIE FOR ME, YOU HUMAN MORTAL!”

The Immortal released his technique.

Kelan gritted his teeth as he watched Yuan being bombarded with Immortal Qi. He wanted to believe in Yuan, but his instincts acted before he could even think.

However, he was immediately halted by an unfathomable presence that surpassed even Senior Bai.

“Who’s there?!” Kelan frantically looked around but couldn’t sense anyone.

‘To avoid my detection, they must be a God Ascension Realm expert! What the hell is going on right now?!’ he cried inwardly.

“Oh no! Junior!”

The distraction rendered Kelan unable to save Yuan, his mind already racing to formulate excuses for when he would have to report Yuan’s death to Senior Bai. Yet, Kelan’s face filled with disbelief as he witnessed the unfolding events. What he saw defied all expectations and left him momentarily paralyzed in astonishment.

The Immortal’s attacks landed with unerring precision, each strike tearing holes through Yuan’s body. Yet, to the Immortal’s astonishment, the grievous injuries Yuan sustained healed instantly, akin to a demon’s heaven-defying regenerative abilities. It was as if the very laws of nature bent to keep Yuan whole.

[Progress: 15%]

[Progress: 17%]

[Progress: 20%]

The merging of his physiques, which had been progressing at a snail’s pace, began to advance rapidly under the immense stress Yuan’s body was enduring.

“Just what the fuck are you?!?” the Immortal shrieked out loud, his face twisted in anger, but his eyes showed a hint of fear.

chapters.title: Chapter 1570 Progress
chapters.body:
Chapter 1570 Progress

Chapter 1570 Progress

“Why won’t you just die?! Die already, you fucking monster!” The Immortal relentlessly assaulted Yuan with Immortal Qi, but regardless of the countless wounds inflicted, Yuan would miraculously heal all injuries within half a second.

[Progress: 22%]

[Progress: 25%]

[Progress: 30%]

<Your understanding of Immortal Qi has significantly increased>

Not only was Yuan’s physique rapidly merging, but his comprehension of Immortal Qi was also deepening merely by being immersed in it.

Yuan even closed his eyes and started cultivating.

This bewildered everyone watching.

“What the hell is he? This is beyond the realm of talents and what humans can achieve…” Kelan swallowed nervously at the ridiculous scene before him.

“And his regenerating ability rivals that of a demon. Don’t tell me he has demonic blood flowing through his veins?”

The possibility that Yuan might be a demon filled Kelan with wariness and profound anxiety, knowing the immense implications it would have.

Meanwhile, Bai Ning had her jaw tightly clenched, her expression more irritated than shocked.

‘He can fight someone over eight realms above his own strength!’

Bai Ning was irritated that Yuan, the very person who had humiliated the Heavenly White Tiger Clan by defeating Bai Xutao, possessed far more talent than she did. Despite being a prodigy capable of battling opponents nearly two entire realms above her own cultivation and being hailed as one of the top prodigies of their era, she was akin to an ordinary individual before Yuan’s talents.

However, even with their differences, Bai Ning wanted to fight Yuan, and that feeling was boosted by her competitive nature.

[Progress: 85%]

[Progress: 88%]

[Progress: 93%]

As the Immortal increased the intensity of his attacks, Yuan’s merging progress advanced even faster.

“You will fall here even if it’s the last thing I do!” The Immortal bellowed as he exerted his maximum strength.

The pressure from his aura was so intense that it seemingly filled the entire Purple Sea, causing the surrounding islands to tremble violently.

The Immortal was exerting so much strength that he somehow broke through some of heaven’s restrictions on his cultivation, causing his cultivation to enter Divine Emperor.

The Immortal’s aura pierced the heavens.

“IMMORTAL MIST GATHERING!” The Immortal roared, creating a massive lance in the sky that rivaled the size of Yuan’s God of War’s Astral Arts.

“Let’s see if you can regenerate from a completely obliterated body!” The Immortal laughed maniacally before launching the massive lance at Yuan.

“Do you need my help?” Zi Xuan’s voice suddenly resounded.

“No, I’ll be fine.”

“Is that so? Then I’ll go back to sleep.” Zi Xuan calmly said.

The massive lance arrived in front of Yuan the following moment.

“HA!”

Yuan released a powerful roar as he brought forth every ounce of power within his body.

For the first time in a long time, Yuan was going beyond his limit.

<Your understanding of Immortal Qi has significantly increased>

A faint but noticeable amount of Immortal Qi exuded from Yuan’s body.

“Immortal Qi? Impossible!” Kelan cried out loud.

“No… it’s weak and incomplete, but it’s undeniably Immortal Qi! That arrogant bastard is already able to comprehend Immortal Qi?! He’s not even in the Divine Realm, much less the Immortal Realm!”

Most people start comprehending Immortal Qi at Immortal Ascension realm, and only when they fully comprehend Immortal Qi would one be able to enter the Immortal Realm as a Bronze Immortal.

However, Yuan, who was still a mortal with a Spirit King cultivation, was already showing signs of comprehending Immortal Qi. If the world knew such a genius existed, many powerhouses would try to recruit him or outright erase him if he rejected them.

In the next moment, the lance struck Yuan’s body. Yet, instead of piercing through him as usual, it froze in place, as if suspended in time, barely penetrating the surface like a needle hanging by a thread.

“What?!” The Immortal was shocked by what he was witnessing.

Despite Yuan’s Immortal Qi being incomplete, it was still powerful enough to barely hold back the Immortal’s Qi. [Progress: 97%]

[Progress: 98%]

[Progress: 99%]

“DIE ALREADY!!!” The Immortal bellowed as he detonated the lance, causing a massive explosion that shook the Fourth Heaven.

Profound Immortal Qi erupted like a shockwave, generating tsunamis that surged in every direction.

“Junior!” Kelan’s heart skipped a beat.

‘Damn it! I was too absorbed in their fight and failed to protect the newcomer! The Celestial Overlord and Senior Bai will skin me alive once they find out!’ Kelan cried inwardly, feeling a strong urge to bury himself in a hole and never emerge.

“Fuck! Don’t tell me he died just like that! I never even got the chance to have a rematch with him!” Bai Ning gritted her teeth in frustration.

“Hahaha! I did it! I finally killed that fucking cockroach!” The Immortal laughed out loud.

At the same time, several more individuals appeared at the scene. They were the Immortal’s pursuers, and they arrived shocked, as they’d witnessed the moments before Yuan was caught up in the explosion.

Meanwhile, after enduring the Immortal’s explosion head-on, Yuan’s body was obliterated, leaving behind only a few scattered cells. Yet, he did not receive the death notification.

[Progress: 100%]

<Congratulations, your Heaven Refining Physique has successfully merged with Golden Immortal Physique, transforming into Heaven Refining Immortal Physique>

<Congratulations on evolving your Physique! You have become the first Player to evolve your Physique!>

Yuan’s obliterated body immediately began to repair itself. As his form regenerated fully, his Dantian also underwent a profound transformation.

Follow current novℯls on ƒгeewёbnovel.com.

The invisible wall that had been blocking his cultivation advancement past Spirit King was finally lifted, and Yuan could feel his cultivation inching toward the next realm.

“I-impossible…” The Immortal muttered in a dazed voice, his expression filled with despair.

“N-not human… he cannot be human with that kind of regenerating ability! Don’t tell me he’s a demon?!” The pursuers quickly came to this conclusion.

“I’ll be damned…” Kelan’s entire body trembled in excitement.

“…” Bai Ning remained silent, but her bewildered expression spoke clearer than any words could.

chapters.title: Chapter 1571 Heaven Refining Immortal Physique
chapters.body:
Chapter 1571 Heaven Refining Immortal Physique

Chapter 1571 Heaven Refining Immortal Physique

<You have acquired the title ‘An Evolved Physique’>

<You have acquired the title ‘Physique Merger’>

Yuan inspected his body after acquiring the Heaven Refining Immortal Physique. His body was incomparably stronger than before, whether in strength or durability. If his body couldn’t handle the Immortal’s attacks previously, his current body would have no problem shrugging them off.

The difference was akin to comparing dried mud to steel. There were also several other changes that occurred within his body, but he had no idea what they could be for the time being.

“Don’t move, you demon!” A loud voice with a hint of terror suddenly resounded, snapping Yuan out of his daze.

He shifted his head to look at the person who’d just spoken—one of the pursuers.

Updat𝓮d fr𝙤m ƒгeeweɓn૦vel.com.

“What did you just call me?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“There’s no use hiding it! That ridiculous regenerating ability is unique to demons!” Yuan was speechless, but he didn’t blame this individual for mistaking him for a demon. After all, he would’ve probably done the same if he were in that man’s shoes, as his regenerating ability was acquired through Demon Cores, so the pursuers weren’t wrong.

“I understand your confusion, but rest assured, I am not a demon.” Yuan calmly said.

“Shut up! Do you think I’m stupid or something?!”

The pursuers outright ignored the Immortal, whom they had been chasing for months, to focus on Yuan, who was a much bigger threat to the Nine Heavens than the escapee.

“I don’t think you’re stupid… for now. If I were a demon, I would have horns and a crystal, wouldn’t I?” Yuan exposed his torso so show that he had no demon crystal on his body.

“You’re definitely taking me for a fool! High-ranking demons don’t have horns and crystals! They look no different than humans!” “So you’re suggesting that I am a Demon Emperor who looks no different than humans? How could a Demon Emperor be a mere Spirit King? And why would I bother to risk exposing myself to fight that immortal?” Yuan asked.

The pursuer couldn’t find a logical explanation immediately and momentarily became speechless.

“You’re obviously hiding your cultivation! There’s no way a Spirit King could emit Immortal Qi! I don’t know why you’re fighting with the escapee, but that doesn’t prove you’re not a demon!” One of the pursuers exclaimed.

“That’s right! You’re a demon hiding in human skin!” Another joined.

Seeing how the group of immortals prepared to kill him, Yuan sighed, “You guys must be quite weak if it requires four of you to chase after a single escapee. Come, I’ll play with you.” The group of immortals immediately exploded with anger after being provoked by Yuan.

“If we can’t capture him, kill him!”

“Kill me? Even though I’m a demon? You should probably get the Demon Sealing Clan instead.” Yuan laughed out loud.

The immortals’ faces flushed red before surrounding him in a formation.

“Golden Lotus Prison!”

The four of them worked together to quickly create a powerful formation around Yuan. Although they were only Tier 5 Array Masters, they were able to create a Tier 6 formation in the blink of an eye together.

Yuan suddenly found himself entrapped inside a golden lotus that slowly drained his spiritual energy.

When Kelan saw this, he thought about intervening, but he wanted to see how Yuan would deal with this situation first. As long as Yuan’s life wasn’t in danger, he decided to remain as a spectator.

However, with Yuan’s regenerating ability rivaling demons, Kelan was no longer worried that he might die.

The immortals, aware that their chances of killing Yuan were slim despite the disparity in their cultivation, resorted to using a formation specifically designed to capture entities as resilient as Yuan.

“Do you want me to destroy the formation for you?” Yu Ning, who had been silently watching this entire time, suddenly spoke.

“No, it’s fine. I want to test out my new physique.” “Okay…”

Yuan suddenly closed his eyes and took a deep breath.

The very next moment, the prison around Yuan suddenly began breaking down, almost as if it were slowly being devoured by an invisible force.

“W-what the fuck?!”

“The formation’s losing energy!”

“That’s impossible!”

“T-that demon bastard is absorbing the formation’s Immortal Qi!”

“What?! How is he doing that?! Techniques are sealed within the Golden Lotus Formation!”

“It must be one of his demon abilities!”

“H-hey! Escapee! Help us kill this demon, and we’ll give you freedom!” One of the immortals turned to look at the escapee.

“Are you serious? Even if I want to kill him, you saw for yourself that I am unable to do so.” The Misty Fox said.

“That’s only if you’re fighting alone! If the five of us work together, there’s still a chance!”

The Misty Fox couldn’t reject the possibility of freedom and agreed to help the immortals.

“The more the merrier.” Yuan smiled.

<Your understanding of Immortal Qi has significantly increased!>

After consuming Immortal Qi directly, Yuan’s understanding of Immortal Qi skyrocketed, causing the Immortal Qi he exuded to become more apparent and profound.

‘This is the perfect opportunity for me to understand Immortal Qi.’ Yuan found a reason to fight these immortals.

The five immortals, each restricted to the Divine King, began bombarding Yuan with techniques infused with Immortal Qi. frёewebηovel.cѳm

To Yuan, enduring these hits with his bare body felt like being struck with a sledgehammer. However, unlike before, it wasn’t enough to destroy his body and merely bruise it.

<Your understanding of Immortal Qi has increased!>

Moreover, whenever their attacks struck Yuan, his Heaven Refining Immortal Physique would automatically absorb some of the Immortal Qi, weakening the force of their blows and strengthening his body even further.

Suddenly, Yuan’s aura surged, and his Dantian expanded.

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have met the requirements for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached First Level Spirit Emperor!>

Amid the battle, Yuan had a breakthrough and finally escaped Spirit King, becoming a Spirit Emperor!

chapters.title: Chapter 1572 Spirit Emperor
chapters.body:
Chapter 1572 Spirit Emperor

Chapter 1572 Spirit Emperor

The moment Yuan’s cultivation entered Spirit Emperor, his aura surged so much that the immortals thought he’d skipped several levels in his breakthrough.

“This bastard had a breakthrough in the middle of a battle! What a fucking monster!”

“Wait… does that mean he was actually only a Spirit King…?”

The realization that Yuan, merely a Spirit King, possessed the prowess to fight them struck quickly. Their faces filled with terror as the full implications of his power dawned on them.

“W-we cannot let him live, or he’ll become an immense threat to humanity later!”

“Kill him! Use everything you’ve got!”

The immortals frantically attacked Yuan, viewing him as a looming calamity destined to destroy humanity in the future.

Meanwhile, seeing Yuan breakthrough snapped Bai Ning out of her daze, and she suddenly approached the battlefield.

“Stop!” she shouted, her voice filled with commanding authority.

“Who the hell are you?!”

“Are you this demon’s accomplice?!”

The immortals immediately became even more nervous. If Bai Ning turned out to be another demon, they’d have no chance of killing them.

However, Bai Ning retrieved a white and black medallion before throwing it at one of the immortals.

“That is my identity!”

“This is… the Heavenly White Tiger Clan’s medallion? And this name… you must be the Heavenly White Tiger Goddess!” “Are you here to help us subjugate this demon?! Thank the heavens!”

However, Bai Ning shook her head and said, “No, but I want to fight him, so get out of my way.”

“W-what? I understand that you want to fight him, but why must you do it alone? He’s a dangerous entity—a demon that terrorized our world and all living beings within!” “Do I really have to explain myself to you?” Bai Ning narrowed her eyes at them.

New n𝙤vel chapters are published on freewebnovel.cσ๓.

“…”

The immortals exchanged nervous glances with each other. Bai Ning’s status was simply too exalted for them to ignore, but it was not as though they could let her fight the demon alone.

If she died, they would be held responsible, hence their perplexed situation.

Seemingly aware of their predicament, Bai Ning said, “Don’t worry, even if I die, you won’t be responsible.”

Bai Ning no longer paid attention to the immortals and approached Yuan, who hovered there calmly.

Without an ounce of fear in her eyes, Bai Ning spoke, “Yo. Do you remember me?”

Yuan nodded, “You’re the one who interrupted my fight with Bai Xutao.”

“You call that a fight? You were straight bullying my brother.” “Do you know why I did what I did?” Yuan then asked.

“No, and I don’t care. The only thing that matters to me is you attacked someone from my family.”

“So you won’t care even if he slaughters an entire city of innocent people?” Yuan asked, his voice carrying a hint of coldness.

“…”

After a moment of silence, Bai Ning responded, “He will atone for whatever crime he’d committed, just like how you’ll be punished for attacking a member of the Heavenly White Tiger Clan.”

“Is that so? What if the Immortal Monarch were in my shoes? What would you do then?”

Bai Ning was taken aback by his sudden mention of the Immortal Monarch.

“How dare you utter his sacred name with your dirty mouth?! The Immortal Monarch would never be in your shoes in the first place!”

“Really? The world’s a big and mysterious place. What if I turn out to be the real Immortal Monarch?” Yuan teased.

“Blasphemy!” Bai Ning bellowed before suddenly transforming, her aura skyrocketing.

“I couldn’t kill you in the Third Heaven because of my restricted cultivation, but it’s a different story up here! Let’s see if you can repeat what you did last time!” Bai Ning’s aura surged all the way to the peak of Divine King, surpassing the other immortals’ aura.

“This lad is not bad…” Kelan nodded in approval after sensing Bai Ning’s powerful aura.

“No matter how high your cultivation is, you’ll never be able to defeat me.” Yuan casually shook his head.

“Shut up and fight me!” Bai Ning shouted as she started her assault.

Yuan didn’t move to purposefully test the strength of her first strike.

Sha!

Bai Ning used her bare hands—her claws—to strike. Imbued with powerful Immortal Qi, her claws were strong enough to cut through Yuan’s body. However, the damage was only superficial, and it didn’t take more than a second for it to recover fully. “Not bad, I suppose.” Yuan calmly commented.

“Shut up and fight me seriously!” Bai Ning shouted.

“Are you actually using Bai Xutao as an excuse to fight me? All you need to do is ask nicely, you know.”

Bai Ning responded with another powerful strike, its pressure powerful enough to cleave the Purple Sea.

Five red lines resembling sword cuts spread across Yuan’s chest but disappeared in the blink of an eye.

Despite Bai Ning’s relentless attack, Yuan remained still, seemingly unwilling to fight her.

“FIGHT ME, DAMN IT!” she roared, her aura surging even higher.

“Not until you admit that you don’t care about the punishment and are only using it as an excuse to fight me.” Yuan casually shrugged.

“Fuck you!” Bai Ning continued launching powerful strikes at Yuan. ƒгeewebnovёl.com

Several moments later, her most potent attack managed to slice his body in half, but it was pointless and just a waste of Immortal Qi if she couldn’t kill him.

“I’m waiting…” Yuan yawned, positioning himself as if he were lazily lying on a couch before a television.

Yuan’s unwillingness to fight and his blatant mockery infuriated Bai Ning.

“Fuck! Fuck! Fuck!”

Bai Ning stopped fighting for a moment and cursed out loud. “I don’t care about Bai Xutao or the punishment for attacking a member of our Heavenly White Tiger Clan! I just want to fight you!” she exclaimed with her face flushed red.

“That’s more like it. Don’t you feel better just from being honest with yourself?” Yuan chuckled.

“Fuck you! If you still refuse to fight me properly, I’ll make you regret being born!” she roared.

“I am a man of my word…” With Number One Under Heaven in his grasp, Yuan suddenly charged at Bai Ning.

Seeing this, Bai Ning prepared to confront him with a wide grin on her face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1573 Beyond Human Capabilities
chapters.body:
Chapter 1573 Beyond Human Capabilities

Chapter 1573 Beyond Human Capabilities

When Yuan swung Number One Under Heaven at her, Bai Ning prepared to confront it with her claws, which were sharper and more durable than even Mythic-grade treasures. However, moments before they clashed, her instincts kicked in, causing her to avoid the sword instead.

Sha!

A powerful sword light swung past Bai Ning’s claw and struck the Purple Sea behind her, splitting it in half.

Although the sword light didn’t touch her, its aura grazed her hand, and that was enough to cut through her skin.

Bai Ning swallowed nervously at this and cried inwardly, ‘If I hadn’t avoided his sword at the very last moment, my entire hand would’ve been cut off! I have never seen such a powerful Soul Weapon before!’ Seeing Bai Ning’s nervous expression, Yuan looked at Number One Under Heaven and showed a bittersweet smile, “You’re too powerful.”

After pondering for a moment, he put away the sword and retrieved Dragon’s Soul again.

“What is the meaning of this? Are you looking down on me?” Bai Ning’s brows deeply furrowed.

“I mean, it’s not like I am trying to kill you.” Yuan calmly said.

“Do you really think you have what it takes to kill me?! You’re thinking too highly of yourself just because you manage to fight with a few ordinary immortals! Today, I, Bai Ning, shall teach you what a prodigy is like!” Bai Ning roared before charging at Yuan, releasing a relentless assault on him.

Yuan parried all her attacks, but the impact from her strikes was far stronger than those of all the immortals combined, enough to push him back.

‘So it wasn’t just his sword that contributed to his prowess! This bastard must have a unique physique that rivals demons!’ Bai Ning gritted her teeth as she used all her power, which she rarely did.

Hours passed, but Bai Ning couldn’t see any opportunity to defeat Yuan. He was akin to an unsurmountable wall—one that could regenerate any damage in the blink of an eye.

‘Is he really a demon?!’ Bai Ning began doubting whether he was actually a demon.

Although she had never fought or seen a demon before, she’d heard plenty of rumors about them, especially how they terrorized the world with their god-like healing abilities—the same one Yuan was displaying at this moment.

“Are you really a demon?!” Bai Ning asked without stopping the fight.

“What do you think?” Yuan casually responded.

“I think your healing abilities are ridiculous!” “Thanks.” “I am not complimenting you! It’s so ridiculous that it’s not helping your case!”

Yuan chuckled and said, “People will believe what they want to believe regardless of the facts, even if it’s right in their eyes. There’s no need to try to convince these people, as I would only be wasting my breath.”

“You will regret this in the future!”

“Are you worried about me? If so, thanks.” Yuan chuckled.

“Shut up!”

Several more hours later, Bai Ning ran out of spiritual energy. Usually, she’d be able to fight for months, if not years, without exhausting her spiritual energy, but she had to use a tremendous amount of spiritual energy just to hurt Yuan.

Meanwhile, Yuan was still full of energy from absorbing her attacks, transforming it into his own energy.

“How… are you… still so full of energy?!” Bai Ning was in disbelief.

“I just am. Anyway, it’s been fun, but I have other things to do.”

“Where do you think you’re going?!” One of the immortals shouted at him.

Yuan fell silent and glanced at him through his mask.

After a moment of silence, Yuan suddenly withdrew Number One Under Heaven and charged at the immortal with his movement technique.

[Immortal Rendering Sword Strike!]

The immortal was caught off guard by Yuan’s surprise attack and failed to react in time before being sliced in half.

However, the immortal didn’t die and managed to survive with just his soul. Yet, Yuan swung his sword again, using nothing but his Enhanced Sword Aura.

“Nooo!”

The immortal cried out loud, his voice filled with fear.

Unfortunately, Yuan didn’t spare another thought before cutting down his soul, killing him entirely.

Ding!

<You have achieved an impossible feat by defeating a Cultivator that was at least nine realms above you!>

<You have acquired the title ‘Beyond Human Capabilities>

[Beyond Human Capabilities: When fighting a Cultivator, your base stats will be boosted by 100%]

[Completed]

With two of the most challenging tasks completed, Yuan felt like he was making progress for Heaven’s Supremacy.

“What the fuck have he done?!” Kelan was greatly shocked by Yuan’s actions.

In fact, nobody there expected Yuan to suddenly kill one of the immortals there.

“YOU SON OF A BITCH!” The other immortals quickly reacted to the death of their companion, their faces flushed with anger. Yet, none of them dared to attack Yuan.

“You will regret this, you damn demon!”

Instead of fighting him, the immortals turned around and ran away.

As for the Misty Fox, he also ran away. Kelan appeared shortly after with a deep frown on his face.

“You… why did you do that?” he asked.

Yuan looked at him and calmly responded, “Why not?”

“That man you killed belonged to the Nine Heavens Law and Order! Not only do they oversee the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto, but they’re also responsible for keeping order in the Nine Heavens! Their influence is second to the Celestial Emperor! And you just killed one of them!” “Is that so? I got unlucky, I guess.”

“…”

Kelan suddenly turned to look at Bai Ning and said, “Can you please leave us alone?”

“And who the hell are you to command me?” Bai Ning frowned.

Kelan’s body suddenly released an oppressive aura that filled the entire Purple Sea.

“Please leave… while I am still asking nicely.” “…” Bai Ning narrowed her eyes at him.

After a moment of silence, she turned to look at Yuan and spoke, “This isn’t over. I’ll defeat you the next time we meet!” freёwebnoѵel.com

Bai Ning left the scene shortly after, leaving Yuan and Kelan alone in the middle of the Purple Sea.

chapters.title: Chapter 1574 Nine Heavens Law And Order
chapters.body:
Chapter 1574 Nine Heavens Law And Order

Chapter 1574 Nine Heavens Law and Order

Once they were finally alone, Kelan raised his hand and showed his faction’s symbol, as long as his rank.

Yuan’s curiosity was piqued at the sight of Kelan’s symbol and rank. He raised an eyebrow and inquired, “What brings a high-ranking figure like you to the Fourth Heaven? I doubt it’s a simple task like catching an escaped immortal.”

Yuan pretended not to be part of the Celestial Overlord to see Kelan’s reaction.

“There’s no need to be on alert. My name is Kelan, and Senior Bai asked me to watch over you because he was worried that your fight with the immortal might go awry.”

“Oh, is that so? I apologize if I had offended you in any way before, Senior.” Yuan’s attitude suddenly changed, becoming more respectable.

This dumbfounded Kelan.

‘The way he treats his friends and foes are so vastly different. Even if I am someone slightly suspicious, he might not hesitate to cut me down.’ Kelan thought to himself.

“Anyway, even though nothing happened to you, and you even managed to slay an immortal—albeit the wrong one—I must apologize for failing to save your life multiple times due to how absorbed I was in your fight.”

“Your fight was truly mesmerizing and awe-inspiring. Unfortunately, you ended up killing someone you shouldn’t have, and you even made it seem like you’re a demon… or are you actually one?” Kelan inquired, seemingly unclear on his identity.

Yuan smiled and said, “Don’t worry, I am definitely human.”

Kelan sighed in relief and said, “Thank the heavens. You would’ve made things really difficult for us if you were a demon. With that said, you’ll probably still be hunted by the Nine Heavens Law and Order for killing their member, and rumors about you being a demon will spread.”

“Your healing abilities that rival a demon will concern many people, and they might treat you like a real demon, even if you’re a human. However, the Celestial Overlords will do everything they can to protect you.”

However, Yuan shook his head and said, “Don’t worry, I never intended to cause trouble for the Celestial Overlords. Even if they want to find me, they don’t know my real identity yet, so they’ll be chasing a phantom for a while.”

Visit freewebnoveℓ.com for the best novel reading exp𝒆rience.

“You’re unfathomably bold… Nobody could be as calm as you if they knew they were being hunted by the Nine Heavens Law and Order…” frёeωebɳovel.com

Yuan shrugged and said, “Anyway, since I am safe, does that mean you’ll return to the upper heavens now?”

“No, I’ll stick around a little longer. After all, it’s been so long since I visited the lower heavens. I also want to know more about you, Junior. And once you recover from your fight, let’s have a spar.”

Yuan nodded, “Alright.”

“Then I’ll see you later.” Kelan left the scene shortly after, returning to Senior Bai to notify him of their situation.

Yuan returned to the Pavilion of Knowledge.

“Yuan! You’re okay!” Xi Meili’s face immediately brightened after seeing his return.

“I told you I’d be fine.” Yuan smiled.

“How was it? Did you kill the immortal?” she asked.

“No, he got away, but I did kill an immortal.” “What…?” Xi Meili raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

“It’s a bit complicated, but this is what happened…”

Yuan recalled the events that had occurred during his battle.

“Heavens… what a mess…” Xi Meili’s eyes were wide open by the end.

Meanwhile, Kelan requested an urgent audience with Senior Bai.

When Senior Bai received this urgent request, his eyebrows rose.

Sometime later, he met up with Kelan, who was waiting for him inside his world.

“Senior.” Kelan respectfully bowed to him.

“What’s the matter? To request an urgent audience, did something happen to Yuan?” Senior Bai went straight to the point.

Kelan nodded with a serious expression and said, “A lot happened, so sit back and prepare yourself.”

Senior Bai immediately swallowed nervously.
