“I watched our Junior fight the escaped immortal. It was quite something. He exceeded my expectations in every metric and more. This is how it went…”

While Kelan recalled everything he had witnessed to Senior Bai, the same thing was happening elsewhere with the group of immortals that had confronted Yuan.

“It’s a demon, I tell you! We must contact the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto as soon as possible!”

“This demon was unlike anything I’ve seen or heard! He wielded a sword and spear and had the aura of a beast!”

“He was only a Spirit King, but he could use Enhanced Sword Aura and fight several Divine Kings simultaneously!”

The leader of Nine Heavens Law and Order listened to the information with a confused and bewildered look on his face.

“A Spirit King that can fight an immortal with their cultivation suppressed to Divine King? Has demon-like regenerating ability? What kind of fucking creature are you guys making up? Did you guys get hit in the head too hard when trying to capture the escapee?” The leader couldn’t believe what he was hearing. This entity sounded like something that wouldn’t exist even in exaggerated fiction.

“This monster is real!” The immortals insisted.

The leader sighed, “Alright, I’ll contact the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto. Tell me everything that happened.”

A few hours later, the leader of Nine Heavens Law and Order visited the Demon Sealing Faction’s headquarters and requested an audience with Qian Chu, the current leader of the Demon Sealing Faction.

“It’s been a while, Demon Sealer Qian.” Qian Chu casually nodded and said, “I was told you have information on a powerful demon?”

“Unfortunately. My faction members encountered this demon in the Fourth Heaven while chasing after one of the Nine Heavens Eternal Grotto escapees. With that said, I am not certain this information is trustworthy. You’ll understand what I mean in a bit.”

“A demon in the Fourth Heaven? This would be troublesome if true.” Qian Chu muttered.

“Alright, let’s hear it. Tell me everything you know about this demon.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1575 Unknown Demon
chapters.body:
Chapter 1575 Unknown Demon

Chapter 1575 Unknown Demon

The leader of Nine Heavens Law and Order began recalling what his subordinates told him with a somewhat stiff expression, almost as if he couldn’t believe what he was uttering. freewebnoveℓ.com

“This demon was only a Spirit King when they initially encountered it but had a breakthrough toward the end, reaching Spirit Emperor.”

“However, even before reaching Spirit Emperor, this demon could fight with the escapee, who was restricted to the Divine King realm, even overpower them to a certain extent.”

“Once this demon reached Spirit Emperor, it was able to fight five Divine Kings simultaneously, even killing one of our members.”

“This demon wielded two different weapons, a sword and a spear, being exceptionally proficient with the sword—to the point where he could use Enhanced Sword Aura. Moreover, both of these weapons were Soul Weapons.”

“This demon’s regenerating ability is also no joke, being able to recover within seconds after having their entire body annihilated.”

“Not only did they have a ridiculously fast regenerating speed, their body was also incredibly durable.”

“This demon wore a mask, but it had no visible horns or unique skin color, a common trait amongst demons. Furthermore, it had the aura of a beast.”

“…” Qian Chu listened to the information of this potential demon with raised eyebrows.

He’d studied and encountered more than anyone within the Demon Sealing Clan, yet he’d never encountered a demon that could wield Soul Weapons, much less two.

While demons are naturally powerful and could fight humans with higher cultivation than itself, not even a Demon Emperor could fight someone eight realms stronger, let alone five at once.

The most ridiculous claim about this demon was that it could use Enhanced Sword Aura. Demons specialized in blood techniques and their own bodies, and there had never been a record of a demon using Sword Aura, much less Enhanced Sword Aura.

If Qian Chu had to be honest, he would outright refute these claims. However, there was no reason for the Nine Heavens Law and Order to fabricate such information, especially since their leader had personally shown up to share it. The details were simply too elaborate to be a joke.

“So, what do you think? Ridiculous, right?” The Nine Heavens Law and Order leader said with an embarrassed face.

“However, I have verified the information myself. My members have no reason to lie about this, and they looked genuinely shaken by their encounter with this demon.”

Qian Chu quickly replied, “I would be lying if I said that I don’t doubt such information, but if a demon with this sort of capabilities were to truly exist, our world would definitely be plunged into another chaotic era—perhaps one that’s even more chaotic than the last one.”

The Nine Heavens Law and Order leader swallowed nervously after hearing such words, and he said, “Regardless of if this entity is a demon or a ghost, we must find it and ensure that such a thing won’t happen.”

Qian Chu nodded and said, “I will have all of my Supreme Demon Sealers and Master Demon Sealers look into this demon.”

“Thank you! I will also contact the Demon Sealing Grotto so we can work together. The sooner we deal with this threat, the better!”

Although the leader knew these two powers rarely collaborated and, more often than not, clashed with each other, they were not in a position to be picky. If this demon turned out to be the real deal, they might have another war with demons.

Qian Chu didn’t object to the leader’s idea to involve the Demon Sealing Grotto, knowing they would inevitably learn of the situation and intervene, regardless of the Demon Sealing Clan’s stance.

The Nine Heavens Law and Order leader didn’t linger and immediately went to the Demon Sealing Grotto.

Qian Chu also summoned all of the Supreme Demon Sealers and Master Demon Sealers to an urgent meeting, something that has not happened since he became the leader of the Demon Sealing Clan.

The demon sealers were taken aback by the sudden summoning. Filled with a mix of anticipation and a touch of dread, they swiftly assembled at the headquarters.

At the meeting, Qian Chu revealed the situation to the demon sealers, who responded with skepticism rather than shock or fear. “I know what you’re all thinking, but the leader of the Nine Heavens Law and Order personally delivered this information to me. Regardless if this entity is a demon or something else, it raises too many concerns and must be looked into.”

“Do we have a description of this demon?” Someone asked.

Qian Chu retrieved a large scroll and revealed a painting of Yuan on it.

“This was created by those who encountered the demon. It has all of the details they could recall. Study it and memorize it.”

While the Demon Sealing Clan was having their meeting, the Demon Sealing Grotto was also preparing to hunt this demon. After hearing how ridiculously capable this demon was, they decided to make hunting it their number one priority.

Over the next few days, hundreds of powerful demon sealers would be sent to the Fourth Heaven.

Meanwhile, Senior Bai had trouble standing once Kelan recalled everything to him.

“Dear heavens… are you sure you weren’t hallucinating? Yuan only became a cultivator a few years ago… How in heaven’s name is he already fighting immortals? Even if their cultivation was suppressed, their experience should’ve made an insurmountable difference. Or are you telling me that he’d managed to acquire what others need tens of thousands of years to learn in just several years? Do such terrifying talents truly exist in this world?”

“Trust me, I know how you feel, and I witnessed it first-hand. He’s the real deal—a real monster.” Kelan sighed.

He continued, “What should we do now? Although the Nine Heavens Law and Order aren’t aware of his identity now, they eventually will.”

Senior Bai pondered for many minutes with a serious expression on his face before speaking, “I’ll seek guidance from the Celestial Overlord. Don’t utter a word of this to anyone else before I return.”

Follow current novels on freewebnσvel.cѳm.

“Naturally.” Kelan nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1576 Leaving The Pavilion Of Knowledge
chapters.body:
Chapter 1576 Leaving The Pavilion Of Knowledge

Chapter 1576 Leaving the Pavilion of Knowledge

After he left, Senior Bai immediately sought an audience with Xu Jiaqi. Once they met, Senior Bai informed her of everything Yuan had done.

“He’s already capable of fighting Divine Kings despite being only a Spirit King? I mocked him for his slow cultivation speed not long ago…” Xu Jiaqi wore an astonished expression on her beautiful face, something that rarely happened.

“Moreover, they’re not ordinary Divine Kings, but real immortals with vast experience.” Senior Bai sighed.

He continued, “Anyway, his monstrous talent is one thing. Killing a member of the Nine Heavens Law and Order is another. I’m afraid they might give him—us trouble in the future.”

“About that… the Nine Heavens Law and Order have already acted.” Xu Jiaqi suddenly said.

“What?” Senior Bai’s eyes widened at this new information.

“The leader has already contacted the Demon Sealing Clan and Demon Sealing Grotto, and these two powerhouses have dispatched their experts,” Xu Jiaqi explained.

“Isn’t that really bad? What should we do?” Senior Bai asked.

Xu Jiaqi pondered for a moment before speaking, “We won’t do anything until we need to. Let’s just see where this goes.”

“Are you sure? What if it’s already too late by then?”

“Are you still underestimating Yuan after all this? He won’t go down so easily, and we’ll have more than enough time to react. Kelan will be with him, as well, no?”

Senior Bai nodded, “Kelan wishes to remain in the lower heavens a little longer.”

“Permission granted. Make it his mission to protect Yuan, too. However, don’t spoil him. Only act when absolutely necessary.” Xu Jiaqi said.

“I understand.”

“We also have a member within the Demon Sealing Clan. I’ll notify him of the situation.” Xu Jiaqi added.

Senior Bai nodded.

After talking a little longer, Senior Bai returned to Kelan to pass Xu Jiaqi’s decision to him.

Meanwhile, the Nine Heavens went into turmoil after news that the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto had dispatched most of their experts in some kind of expedition.

“Fuck, are we going to war with demons again?”

“No, they are looking for a single demon.”

“What? They dispatched so many people for a single demon? Did they find the Demon Emperor that escaped previously?”

“Apparently, this one is even more dangerous.”

“How long has it been since the Demon Sealing Clan’s been this active?”

“It’s been thousands of years… Unfortunately, their activity isn’t something to be celebrated but dreaded.”

Somewhere in the Demon Sealing Grand Library, a beautiful woman sat before a scroll with a ponderous face.

“I wonder how the founder’s doing… Hopefully, I’ll get to see him again soon.” Yan Hara muttered to herself as she stared at the scroll with the demon’s description.

As a Master Demon Sealer, she was also dispatched to the lower heavens.

Meanwhile, Yuan and Xi Meili spent several more hours at the Pavilion of Knowledge before leaving.

“Are you leaving already?” Xuan Kun asked them when he noticed them exiting the room.

“Yes, we’ve finished reading all of the materials.” Yuan nodded.

“I see… I hope it was helpful to you.”

“It was. I learned some new things about the Immortal Monarch and his companions—mostly about the Nine Divine Supremes.”

“If you ever need information on anything, don’t hesitate to visit our Pavilion of Knowledge. Here is our medallion. There are other ‘Pavilion of Knowledge’ in other places with different names, but they all serve the same purpose. Our Pavilion of Knowledge happens to be the first one to be founded.” Xuan Kun handed him a purple medallion with an image of a pavilion and the word ‘Knowledge’ engraved into it.

“Thank you.” Yuan accepted the medallion and tossed it into his spatial ring.

Xuan Kun shook his head and said, “No, I should be thanking you. Because of you, we know the Immortal Monarch is still alive out there somewhere. You have no idea how much this means to me—to many beast clans in the Nine Heavens.” “What does it mean?” Xi Meili asked out of curioisty.

“Well, for starters, there will be a massive movement to find the Immortal Monarch soon.” Xuan Kun revealed with a serious face.

Yuan raised an eyebrow and inquired, “And what will they do once they find him? The Immortal Monarch’s era ended long ago.”

“The Immortal Monarch’s era may have ended, but some—many still revere him. He is akin to their god—a being above humans and beasts. His return will have unfathomable significance that I cannot even begin to comprehend.” Xuan Kun spoke with a dazed look on his face.

“…” Yuan didn’t know how to react to that and remained silent.

As they made their way downstairs, their presence caught Bai Ning’s attention, who also appeared to be leaving.

“Where are you going after this?” Bai Ning asked him.

“I don’t know.” He shrugged.

“What about you?”

“I have to return to my family temporarily to report some things. Did you not notice the disturbance from earlier?” she asked with a raised eyebrow.

“I felt many powerful ripples, but I am not the type to stick my nose in other’s business.” Yuan replied with a smile.

“Good on you. It was a mess out there.” Bai Ning sighed.

“Oh? What happened?” he asked with a curious face.

“A fight between immortals and some crazy bastard—the same bastard that beat my brother.” Bai Ning clenched her fist as she recalled him.

“The masked individual? He was there? Did you avenge your brother?” Yuan asked with a worried face.

Bai Ning merely sighed at his question, her expression darkening with every passing moment.

“I see…” Yuan wore a look of understanding and didn’t inquire further.

“Well, I hope you’ll get to avenge your brother next time.”

She vigoriously nodded, “I won’t let him escape a third time!”

“Then I’ll see you later.”

After bowing to each other, Yuan left the Pavilion of Knowledge with Xi Meili, who tried hiding her face by looking away from Bai Ning.

Bai Ning stared at Xi Meili’s back as she walked to the exit.

‘She looks a little familiar… but from where?’ she wondered inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1577 A Hundred Million
chapters.body:
Chapter 1577 A Hundred Million

Chapter 1577 A Hundred Million

Once Yuan and Xi Meili left the Pavilion of Knowledge, they disappeared into the Purple Sea. Bai Ning left shortly after and returned to her clan in the upper heavens to update them on the situation.

“Where are we going now?” Xi Meili asked Yuan as they soared across the Purple Sea.

“A place that will allow me to kill a lot of magical beasts,” he calmly said.

“Why?” Xi Meili raised an eyebrow.

Although divine beasts and magical beasts are beasts, magical beasts are typically simpleminded and instinct-driven animals mutated through spiritual energy, so divine beasts don’t feel anything even if magical beasts are slaughtered. In fact, divine beasts often slaughter and consume magical beasts, which is akin to humans cultivating with spirit stones and monster cores.

“I need to kill a hundred million entities…” he sighed.

“A hundred million?!” Xi Meili’s jaw dropped at this astonishing number that would take years of constant grinding.

“I would love to ask why you’d need to kill so many, but I’m a little afraid to do so…” Xi Meili swallowed nervously.

Yuan chuckled and said, “You just asked. Anyway, it’s a quest—something I need to do to become stronger.”

“You’re already powerful enough to kill immortals. I cannot imagine how much more powerful you can become.” “No, I am not powerful enough to kill immortals. I only managed to kill that one because his cultivation was restricted. If I had to fight him at full power, he’d easily kill me.” Yuan shook his head at reality.

“Still, I don’t think anyone else out there can boast of defeating someone nine realms above their own strength.”

“With so many experts from the upper heavens showing up, I will need to become strong—much stronger if I want to protect you and everyone.” A glint of determination flickered within Yuan’s eyes.

“We could’ve asked Xuan Kun, but we had already left the Pavilion of Knowledge.” Xi Meili then said.

“It’s fine. I intend to return to the others first. We can figure out the rest afterward.” After returning to the Cultivators’ Haven, where Meixiu and the others are in seclusion, Yuan said to Xi Meili, “You can return to training if you want. I’ll be away for a few days.” “I’ve had enough training for now. I’ll take a look around the city while I wait for your return,” she said. ƒгeewebnovёl.com

Yuan nodded, “Stay safe.”

She chuckled, “Don’t worry, I doubt many Bai Xutao are out there.” Yuan logged off the next moment.

Once he returned to Earth, Yuan went to relax inside the bathroom by soaking in medicine. Even though the medicine no longer had any effect on his body, it still felt better than an ordinary hot bath.

After getting out of the bathroom, Yuan noticed a card placed on the desk beside the bed.

He picked it up and read its contents.

[Chairman Lee has contacted us on… The Great Battlefield has finished with its renovations and will resume Faction Wars soon. Our Faction War with Fiery Garden of Hell has been scheduled.]

“Two days ago, huh? Luckily, I finished my business in Cultivation Online earlier, or I would’ve missed the Faction War,” he mumbled to himself.

The Demon Sealing Faction and Fiery Garden of Hell was scheduled to begin in three days.

He went to the training grounds to see if anyone was there, but it was empty.

Everyone was busy with their training in Cultivation Online.

With nothing else to do, Yuan went to the balcony and started playing the zither to pass the time.

A few hours later, Meifeng knocked on the door.

“You can enter.”

Meifeng walked straight to the balcony and said, “You saw the note, right?”

“Yes.”

“Do you really intend to fight the Faction War alone?”

“Yes, but I’ll need you there to be the ruler,” he calmly nodded.

“Of course, I’ll be there.”

“I’ll make my way to the Eternal Lotuses now. I’ll see you at the Great Battlefield.”

R𝑒ad lat𝒆st chapt𝒆rs at free𝑤ebnovel.com Only.

Sometime later, Yuan disappeared into the clouds, speeding toward the Eternal Lotuses’ headquarters.

When he arrived, the guards at the entrance instantly recognized him and said, “Our leader is expecting your arrival, Player Yuan.” Yuan followed one of them inside and was led directly to Bai Lianhua’s room.

“Welcome, Yuan. I’ve been anticipating your match since Miss Meifeng told me about it two days ago.” Bai Lianhua greeted him with a bright and beautiful smile.

“If I was any slower with my business in Cultivation Online, I would’ve missed it and disappointed you.” Yuan smiled.

“But you didn’t. Anyway, Yu Rou is in her room.”

Yuan nodded.

“Ah, before you go to her, I’d like to thank you again for all your help. The resources you’ve given us greatly benefited every member of our faction, and our faction’s prowess skyrocketed in a short time.” “That was the purpose to begin with. Maybe you’ll be able to get your revenge on the Fiery Locust Swarm soon.”

Bai Lianhua’s eyebrows suddenly raised, and she said, “Unfortunately, that may no longer be possible, as the Fiery Locust Swarm had been disbanded after their rank was stripped and their leader and a majority of their members had been banned from participating in further Faction Wars for using drugs during their match with the Demon Sealing Faction.”

“Oh, right. I forgot they did something like that.” Yuan chuckled.

“Fiery Queen and her faction also faced ridicule since the Fiery Locust Swarn was under them.” Bai Lianhua added.

“So they must be extra pissed off right now. This is great. It’ll make our match even more entertaining.”

Yuan left to meet with Yu Rou shortly after.

The following day, Yuan traveled to the Great Battlefield with Yu Rou and Bai Lianhua.

When he arrived, Meifeng was already waiting for him—alongside the other members of the Demon Sealing Faction. This time, every member was present. However, none of them would be participating in the Faction War and were there as mere spectators.

chapters.title: Chapter 1578 Fighting The Fiery Garden Of Hell
chapters.body:
Chapter 1578 Fighting The Fiery Garden Of Hell

Chapter 1578 Fighting the Fiery Garden of Hell

“I was really hoping you didn’t show up so we could have some fun,” Wang Ming remarked as Yuan and his group arrived.

“Sorry, but I really need to do this alone,” Yuan said with an apologetic smile.

“It’s fine, but we have the next one!”

“Of course.”

As they made their way to the arena, their group drew the attention of everyone they passed, like a group of celebrities.

“Look! The Demon Sealing Faction is finally here!”

“It looks like their entire faction will be present for this Faction War!”

“That’s only natural. They’re against the Fiery Garden of Hell, after all. Even Player Yuan won’t be able to fight so many players alone.” “Who knows? With the new changes, anything’s possible.”

“No, it’ll definitely be much more difficult for Player Yuan if everyone can use deadly techniques.”

“I’m sure Player Yuan has some ridiculously powerful attacks that can wipe out thousands of players in the blink of an eye.”

“Even if that’s the case, I doubt he would be able to use those techniques too much before running out of Qi.”

The people speculated and debated on their Faction War, unaware that Yuan was planning to fight the Fiery Garden of Hell alone.

Meanwhile, the Fiery Garden of Hell was already gathering inside the new and improved arena.

The arena was expanded several times its original size, and hazardous areas, such as a river of real lava and deadly traps, were spread throughout it.

The arena itself could easily accommodate ten million players at once, so there was plenty of room for even the largest factions to go wild.

Due to the formation that prevents death and serious injuries, the technique evaluation was no longer required, making Faction Wars more accessible and exciting.

A record number of people showed up to spectate the Faction War between the Demon Sealing Faction and the Fiery Garden of Hell, not to mention the number of people watching from home and other places.

At this moment, more than half of the world tuned in to watch the Faction War—to see how the Demon Sealing Faction would deal with the Fiery Garden of Hell and its impressive number of players.

“I’ve been waiting for you, Yuan!” Chairman Lee’s face brightened like a lightbulb after seeing Yuan and the rest of the Demon Sealing Faction.

“Do I have to do anything before we can start the Faction War?” Yuan asked.

“No, we can start once it’s time.” Yuan nodded and said, “Then I’ll make my way to the arena.”

“Good luck, brother.” Yu Rou said to him.

The others followed, but Chairman Lee’s eyebrows raised when the Demon Sealing Faction also started saying it.

Seeing Chairman Lee’s puzzled face, Wang Ming said, “Only Yuan will be participating in the Faction War. We’re just here as spectators.”

“W-what?! You intend on fighting the Fiery Garden of Hell alone?! All 600,000 of them?!” Chairman Lee’s eyes widened with shock as he asked Yuan.

“Yes, I do.” Yuan calmly acknowledged.

“B-but… that’s…” Chairman Lee was at a loss for words. He couldn’t imagine how someone could fight so many people at once—not even someone like Yuan.

Yuan smiled and said, “There’s a reason I created the formation. Of course, I wanted to make Faction Wars safer for everyone, but it’s also so I can fight with fewer restrictions.”

“…” freewёbnoνel.com

Chairman Lee still couldn’t find any words.

Yuan continued walking to the exit, quickly disappearing from the waiting room. Meifeng followed him since she had to be the ruler.

“He’s serious?” Chairman Lee uttered in a dazed voice.

“He never jokes around in these sorts of situations.” Wang Ming chuckled.

Sometime later, Yuan appeared in the arena. When the spectators saw this, the entire stadium erupted with cheering.

However, the cheering quickly quieted once the spectators realized only two members from the Demon Sealing Faction were present.

“Huh? Where’s the rest of the members?” Someone asked out loud.

“Maybe they couldn’t show up, just like the last Faction War?”

Foll𝑜w current novels on fɾēewebnσveℓ.com.

“That’s impossible. I saw the entire Demon Sealing Faction at the airport. They’re definitely here.”

“So where the hell are they? The match’s about to begin in ten minutes.”

“L-look over there! The rest of the Demon Sealing Faction members are in the spectator’s area!” Someone suddenly shouted, his arm pointed at a specific location in the stadium meant for VIPs.

“No way… they’re here as spectators? Does this mean Player Yuan wants to fight the Fiery Garden of Hell alone?! That’s crazy!”

“Hahaha! His arrogance knows no bounds!”

“Are the Fiery Garden of Hell aware of this?”

“We’ll find out soon.”

Yuan, who showed up alone in the arena to face the Fiery Garden of Hell, shocked every spectator watching. Nobody knew what he was trying to achieve or what his thought process was, but many people were mad.

“Fuck! I can’t believe I woke up early for this shit!”

“I can’t believe I actually bet money on the Demon Sealing Faction’s victory! What was I thinking?!”

After realizing that Yuan planned to fight the Fiery Garden of Hell alone, many people became desperate. They gambled the rest of their money on the Fiery Garden of Hell’s victory, hoping to recoup some of their losses.

Meanwhile, Fiery Queen had someone scout the arena using a scouting technique. “What? Only Player Yuan is on the field, excluding the ruler?” Fiery Queen couldn’t believe what she was hearing and had the scouter do it again.

Yet, the scout reconfirmed that Yuan was alone.

“That bastard actually dares to face us alone? How much does he intend to look down on me?!” Fiery Queen gritted her teeth in anger instead of feeling relieved that he was alone.

Five minutes before the match, a familiar voice resounded through the broadcasting speakers.

“Hello, everyone! It’s me, Yan Xiaoxiao, and I will be the commentator for this Faction War!”

“Before we start, I would like to announce that the highly anticipated ‘Demon Sealing Faction Interview’ will be released after this Faction War is concluded, so stay tuned for that!”

chapters.title: Chapter 1579 A Display Of Overwhelming Prowess
chapters.body:
Chapter 1579 A Display Of Overwhelming Prowess

Chapter 1579 A Display of Overwhelming Prowess

“Now then, as you can see for yourself, it appears that Player Yuan intends to fight the Fiery Garden of Hell by himself! That’s one to 600,000 players! What kind of secret trick does he have up his sleeves to have so much confidence?!” Yan Xiaoxiao spoke with excitement. She was one of the very few people who weren’t angry at Yuan and even believed in him.

“Before we begin the match, allow me to give you all a brief explanation of the new rules.”

“Due to the generosity of a profound expert in formations, the Great Battlefield has gone through several renovations that will enhance our players’ experience and our entertainment!”

“With this formation, we will no longer have to worry about accidental deaths, allowing the players to truly go all out with their powerful techniques!”

“However! While players won’t die even if a sharp sword strikes their neck, they will still feel some of the pain, so it’s not completely risk-free!”

“Although players can no longer die, they will still carry a badge that represents their life, and if broken, they will be disqualified. A player will also be disqualified if they are knocked unconscious, which may happen if they suffer enough damage.”

“We will now briefly demonstrate the formation’s functions.” Two individuals appeared inside the arena and, before hundreds of millions of spectators, began hacking their sharp swords at each other’s bare bodies without any hesitation or holding back.

Many powerful blows later, not a single life-threatening injury could be seen on their body. Besides a few bruises, they looked otherwise completely fine.

“As you just witnessed, this formation is very reliable!” Yan Xiaoxiao commented as the two demonstrators left the arena.

The crowd cheered loudly, causing the entire stadium to tremble.

Sometime later, Yan Xiaoxiao received word that the Faction War would officially begin.

“Alright, you’ve all been waiting for long enough! The Faction War between Demon Sealing Faction— Player Yuan and Fiery Garden of Hell will begin now!”

A flare shot straight into the sky before exploding into a blue mist, signifying the start of the Faction War.

Once Fiery Queen saw this, she pointed toward the direction of Yuan’s castle and shouted in a commanding voice, “Teach that arrogant bastard what happens when he underestimates us, the Fiery Garden of Hell!”

However, before the 600,000 members of the Fiery Garden of Hell could go anywhere, they halted their advancement and stared at the massive figure in the distance with bewildered expressions.

Meanwhile, Yuan activated God of War’s Astral Arts the moment the match began.

“W-what the fuck is that?! It’s huge!”

“A giant! It’s a giant!”

The spectators watched with wide eyes and mouths as Yuan controlled the avatar, sweeping the arena with a golden sword.

Seeing this, the players on the field dropped their weapons and immediately began running away. Yet, none was able to out run the sword.

The moment the avatar struck the ground, the entire Great Battlefield shook violently, and a deep fissure appeared in the arena.

Before the Fiery Garden of Hell members could even leave their base, over 300,000 players were removed from the Faction War with a single strike.

In the blink of an eye, Yuan had annihilated half of the Fiery Garden of Hell’s players.

The spectators were speechless. Yan Xiaoxiao was speechless. There was not a single person watching who wasn’t shocked by what they had just witnessed.

Once she snapped out of her daze, Yan Xiaoxiao commented, her voice filled with excitement, “HEAVENS! WHAT A POWER PLAY! Player Yuan disqualified 301,283 players with a single strike the moment the match began! How will Fiery Queen and her faction respond to this?!”

After the sudden attack, Fiery Queen sat on her buttocks with a dazed look on her face as she stared at the golden giant that overlooked them from above like a god, feeling like an ant. “I-it’s not over! He must have consumed most, if not all, of his energy on that one attack!” Fiery Queen eventually recovered.

This content is taken from freeweɓnovel.cѳm.

The very next moment, not wanting to finish the Faction War too quickly, Yuan deactivated God of War’s Astral Arts.

Fiery Queen misunderstood this and became convinced that Yuan had exhausted all of his energy.

The remaining Fiery Garden of Hell players struggled to get back on their feet before advancing toward Yuan’s castle again.

However, before they could take more than ten steps, a beam of light suddenly appeared from the direction of their destination.

This beam of light contained an immense amount of spiritual energy, and everyone struck by it was instantly knocked unconscious. The same thing happened to those hit by the God of War’s Astral Arts.

Suddenly, another 50,000 Fiery Garden of Hell players were removed from the battlefield.

“M-my god! What on earth was that laser technique just now?! I have never seen—” Yan Xiaoxiao cried out loud.

Before Yan Xiaoxiao could even finish her sentence, Yuan launched another Heaven Splitting Sword Strike at the Fiery Garden of Hell, defeating another 32,000 players in the blink of an eye.

Several more Heaven Splitting Sword Strikes appeared shortly after, giving Fiery Garden of Hell no room to breathe as they were bombarded with powerful techniques from across the arena.

Less than a minute had passed since the match began, yet Fiery Garden of Hell’s impressive 600,000 player number had been reduced to just 100,000.

The spectators were unsure of what they were witnessing, but as Yuan’s overwhelming abilities became apparent, a palpable fear began to spread among them.

“If it weren’t for the formation, he would’ve killed half a million people just like that…”

“What kind of monstrous power is that? What if he suddenly decides to use it for evil? Who could stop him then?”

These kinds of questions began to arise in people’s minds. They feared that Yuan was too powerful, that he wielded unchecked and uncontrollable power that could spell the end of the world if he chose to end it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1580 A Display Of Overwhelming Prowess(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1580 A Display Of Overwhelming Prowess(2)

Chapter 1580 A Display of Overwhelming Prowess(2)

“Queen! What should we do?! We’re completely powerless right now and are no different than sitting ducks waiting to be slaughtered!” One of the Fiery Garden of Hell members suddenly cried out loud.

Fiery Queen didn’t immediately respond. She surveyed her surroundings with a dazed look, taking in the devastated scene. Over eighty percent of her forces had been defeated in less than a minute, but they still had 100,000 players remaining. If Yuan had exhausted all of his energy, they still had a good chance of defeating him.

“There’s no need to despair! We may have lost a lot of players, but we are still 100,000-strong! Player Yuan’s definitely exhausted his Qi this time! This is our chance to defeat him once and for all!” Fiery Queen shouted.

She pointed in Yuan’s direction with a whip in hand and continued, “Advance!”

The players quickly scrambled to their feet and started running toward Yuan’s castle for the third time with their eyes peeled open for incoming attacks.

“He’s stopped attacking! I think he’s really out of energy! We need to hurry and reach him before he recovers!”

New n𝙤vel chapters are published on freewebnovel.cσ๓.

“Move! Move your asses! We cannot allow him to recover even slightly!”

The Fiery Garden of Hell began advancing like madmen, almost as though they were racing each other to see who reached Yuan first. Even if they don’t have the capability to defeat Yuan, they could still hope to delay his recovery.

However, unbeknownst to them, Yuan still had plenty of spiritual energy. In fact, he’d been holding back the majority of his strength this entire time.

If he’d used his full strength—or even half of it, the players wouldn’t have survived it, even with the formation’s protection.

Seeing how desperately the Fiery Garden of Hell was trying to reach him, Yuan sat on the ground before completely lying down, looking like he was going to take a nap.

“What in god’s name?! Player Yuan appears to be taking a nap in the middle of the war! Did he run out of energy after using so many powerful techniques at once?!” Yan Xiaoxiao exclaimed.

“He really loves to show off, doesn’t he?” Wang Ming chuckled at this scene.

“He’s just bullying them at this point.” Shi Lang shook his head with a slight smile.

The arena was so massive that it took the fastest player in Fiery Garden of Hell nearly twenty minutes of straight sprinting to finally reach Yuan.

“That bastard’s asleep?!” The player almost couldn’t believe his eyes when he saw Yuan casually resting on his back, seemingly without a care in the world.

The player immediately thought about launching a sneak attack, but he quickly became doubtful whether Yuan was actually asleep or not.

‘What was I thinking? Unless he knocked himself out after exhausting himself, there’s no way he would actually be asleep. This is obviously a trap!’

However, even if it was a trap, the player couldn’t stand there and do nothing while Yuan continued to recover his energy. He had to do something.

The next moment, after taking a few deep breaths, the player made his move. Using his movement technique, he closed their distance almost instantly.

With a dagger in his grasp, the player aimed for the badge hanging by Yuan’s waist.

However, when the player reached Yuan, he saw that Yuan’s eyes were suddenly opened, and they were staring right into his eyes.

The player felt an incredible chill throughout his entire body, almost like he was suddenly swimming naked in ice water. Unfortunately, it was already too late to stop, and the adrenaline in his blood made him move even faster.

“Die for me, you monster!” The player roared as his dagger inched closer to Yuan’s life badge.

Right as the dagger was millimeters away from touching Yuan’s badge, the player suddenly found himself unable to move even the slightest, almost as if he was frozen in time.

Before the player could even react, Yuan casually smacked him in the face, sending him flying across the arena until he hit another player several minutes away.

“Where the hell did you come from?!” The player struck by the one who got hit cried out loud.

However, the player couldn’t respond, as he was knocked unconscious. Eventually, Fiery Queen and the rest of the players reached Yuan, who was no longer napping on the ground but hovering in the air instead.

“What took you so long? I fell asleep waiting for you.” Yuan said as his gaze stared at Fiery Queen.

“Shut up!” Fiery Queen bellowed with an enraged expression.

“Keep acting arrogant, but we all know that you’ve already exhausted your energy!”

“Oh, really? What gave it away?” Yuan smiled.

“Unless you have unlimited Qi, there’s no way you’re not exhausted by now!” “But I had plenty of time to rest.”

“Hah! Who are you trying to fool? You wouldn’t be able to recover completely even if we gave you an entire day!” Yuan laughed, “Fiery Queen, you don’t learn, do you? How many times do I have to teach you this lesson?”

“W-what lesson?” Fiery Queen frowned.

“That you’d never be able to defeat me!”

Yuan activated God of War’s Astral Art again.

The golden giant returned and even looked bigger than before.

Fiery Queen and the other players’ faces immediately darkened, and their hearts all dropped at the same time.

“Fiery Queen, let this be my last warning to you. If I have to deal with you again, it won’t be so peaceful.”

Yuan proceeded to bombard Fiery Queen and the remaining players with a barrage of attacks. Each time the golden giant swung its sword, the entire island would shake, and a new fissure would open up in the ground. After destroying almost the entire arena, Yuan deactivated the technique and descended before Fiery Queen, who he’d purposefully left alone.

Fiery Queen stared at him with a dumbfounded face. She opened her mouth, but no words came out.

In the end, Yuan destroyed her badge without uttering another word.

chapters.title: Chapter 1581 Controversies
chapters.body:
Chapter 1581 Controversies

Chapter 1581 Controversies

Fiery Queen stared at her destroyed badge with a dazed face. Although she sustained almost no physical damage during the Faction War, the damage dealt to her mentally was immense and profound.

This overwhelming sense of defeat and helplessness made her recall their previous confrontation and how he slaughtered all of the exiles. Tears began flowing from Fiery Queen’s eyes, but Yuan did not linger to watch her weep and was already walking away.

Despite the Demon Sealing Faction—Yuan’s overwhelming victory, nobody in the crowd cheered, and the place was so silent that a pin drop could be heard from miles away.

Eventually, Yan Xiaoxiao broke the silence by announcing the winner, “T-the winner of this Faction War is Demon Sealing Faction! Or should I say Player Yuan, who single-handedly defeated 600,000 players by himself!” Yan Xiaoxiao quickly appeared in the arena and sprinted to Yuan, fearing that he might leave before she could interview him.

“Congratulations on the Demon Sealing Faction’s second victory! With this momentous victory, the Demon Sealing Faction is now ranked 5th in the world! I’m sure the speed of your rise broke several records, but we can talk about that later. Right now, I want to know what you have to say!”

Yuan grabbed the microphone and spoke calmly, “I see a lot of disappointed people in the audience. Were you expecting something else? I’m sorry if I disappointed you, but I warned you the last time—never bet against me.” “Anyway, I’ve had enough fun of Faction Wars for now, so I will no longer be participating in them. With that said, if you wish to challenge the Demon Sealing Faction, I’m sure the other members will be more than willing to entertain you.”

Yuan returned the microphone to Yan Xiaoxiao before walking out of the arena.

The spectators remained silent, trying to comprehend what they had just witnessed. Given the absurdity of everything, many wondered if they had somehow logged into Cultivation Online without realizing it. Although the members of the Demon Sealing Faction tried to look calm, they were also inwardly shocked. They had never seen Yuan fight so fiercely, even during their training.

“I knew there was a wide gap between us, but I didn’t think it was this ridiculous…” Shi Lang muttered in a dazed voice. “Forget about the Faction War. He can probably conquer the world with those techniques.”

“I wouldn’t doubt it.” Wang Ming smiled.

After the Faction War concluded, topics on the subject began spreading online like wildfire. freewēbnoveℓ.com

[Even though the Faction War ended already, I still cannot believe it ended so fast.]

[I waited an entire month for this Faction War, and the Fiery Garden of Hell was defeated in less than half an hour—only because Player Yuan waited 20 minutes for them to reach him!]

Controversies on whether Yuan should be allowed to participate in future Faction Wars also began filling up the internet.

[Player Yuan is too powerful! He needs to be banned from future Faction Wars to keep things entertaining and fair!]

[Player Yuan is too much! He’s too arrogant! Fiery Queen did not deserve to be bullied like that!]

Most spectators who watched Yuan’s Faction War felt an indescribable sensation after the match. It was a feeling they were not familiar with. Gradually, they began to recognize that feeling as existential fear.

After witnessing someone who was beyond their understanding, they began to question the purpose of their own existence. However, unwilling to accept their inferiority, these people banded together to drag down the one who had made them feel that way.

[Player Yuan was definitely using drugs to enhance his strength!]

[Ban Player Yuan!]

[Remove the Demon Sealing Faction from the rankings!]

Countless calls for the Cultivators’ Association to ban Player Yuan and the Demon Sealing Faction flooded the internet. The sentiment only intensified over time, with more and more people echoing the same demand.

Chairman Lee, under pressure not only from the public but also from the Legacy Families and other powerful backgrounds, found himself in a difficult position.

The Legacy Families and Ten Great Families have always wanted to get rid of Yuan, but they have never had the opportunity to do so until now.

Soon, even government agencies from around the world got involved. Due to Yuan’s prowess, which they perceived as a threat, they had no choice but to intervene.

However, the governments had no interest in the Cultivators’ Association or the Faction Wars. They only had one thing in their sights—Yuan himself.

Follow curr𝒆nt nov𝒆ls on fɾeeweɓnѳveɭ.com.

Shortly after the Faction War had concluded, every single government agency in the world got together. They watched the Faction War between Yuan and Fiery Garden of Hell from beginning to end before they started their conversation.

They started the conversation by revealing all of their information on Yuan, from the day he joined the orphanage to the current day.

Although the government hasn’t acted until now, they have been gathering information on Yuan since the day they realized he was Player Yuan just in case of situations like this.

“This person clearly has enough power to threaten not just a country but the entire world. He’s too dangerous to be wandering around without any supervision.”

“Then what do you suggest? That we chain him down? Who here has the power to do that?”

“I do not know what has to be done or what can be done. I only know that something must be done before he gets out of control!” “He’s currently living within the Dragon Spiral Mountain, right? Perhaps we can get the Lord to deal with him.”

The room fell silent for a moment. The Lord’s identity as a divine beast was no secret among government agencies, but only those with the highest level of clearance knew about it.

“If it’s the Lord, he definitely has the power to control Yuan. No… he might be the only entity in this world who can do so.”

“But would he even help us?”

“The Lord is a reasonable entity. I’m sure he’ll understand our worries.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1582 Yuan’S Offer
chapters.body:
Chapter 1582 Yuan’S Offer

Chapter 1582 Yuan’s Offer

“Brother… is there anything you can do about this?”

Inside her room, Yu Rou shoved her phone at Yuan’s face, showing him all of the negative things being said about him with a concerned face.

After the Faction War, wanting to spend a little more time with his sister, Yuan left with Yu Rou and Bai Lianhua while the others returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Yuan glanced at the topic being made about him and shrugged, “What do you want me to do? Tell them to shut up?”

“I don’t know, but they’re demanding you to be banned from future Faction Wars! And it’s not just a few individuals!” Yu Rou said with a worried face.

“I kind of agree with them. I’m simply too powerful. Any Faction War with my participation will be a guaranteed victory for the Demon Sealing Faction by a landslide. I don’t intend to participate in more matches unless necessary, so this ban won’t really affect me that much.”

“But I enjoy watching you kicking everyone’s ass! Faction Wars will no longer be the same for me after seeing your performance today! They won’t be as entertaining!”

“Senior Sister, you should say something too!” Yu Rou turned to look at Bai Lianhua, who was leaning on the wall with a perplexed expression on her face.

“I don’t know what to say. I doubt the Cultivators’ Association would ban him because of this, but I have a feeling that the Ten Great Families will add to the pressure. There’s no chance in hell they’d let this opportunity to drag Yuan down slip away.”

Suddenly, Bai Lianhua received a call on her private cellphone. “Hello? Father?” It was her father, Bai Mengyao, calling.

“Yuan? Yeah, he’s with me. Why are you asking?”

“Huh? Seriously?”

Bai Lianhua’s face became filled with shock.

“Okay… I’ll tell him…”

After hanging up, Bai Lianhua turned to look at Yuan with a heavy gaze.

“What’s wrong?” Yuan asked.

“I was right. The Ten Great Families are working together to pressure the Cultivators’ Association to put a ban on you. However, that’s not what’s most concerning. Apparently, government agencies around the world are wary of your capabilities and have gathered to discuss your existence.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow at this news.

“The government? What are they worried about? That I might take over the world or something?” he spoke in a joking tone.

Yet, Bai Lianhua nodded and said, “Yes, precisely that. They are worried about the prowess you displayed today. As of this moment, they see you as an unpredictable disaster waiting to happen.”

“Even if you swear that you won’t take over the world, they won’t feel safe until they have you under their control.” “So what do you think they’ll do to me?” Yuan suddenly asked.

Despite being on the government’s watchlist, Yuan didn’t seem to be all that concerned. “Well, they’ll most likely try to put a leash on you—control you. However, they won’t approach you directly and will most likely…” Bai Lianhua paused midway and turned to look at Yu Rou with a worried frown on her face.

“They will most likely approach Yu Rou and those close to you first. Only once they have enough leverage over you will they finally approach you,” Bai Lianhua sighed.

“Is that so?” Yuan looked at Yu Rou and asked, “Want to spend some time with me in the Dragon Spiral Mountain? At least until everything calms down. Nobody will be able to bother you while you’re with me.”

“Of co—!” Yu Rou closed her mouth just as quickly as she opened it. She wanted to agree, but she had her own obligations at the Eternal Lotuses and couldn’t be selfish anymore.

She turned to look at Bai Lianhua, seemingly asking for permission.

Bai Lianhua smiled and said, “Go spend some time with your brother. You barely left this place since you joined our faction over a year ago. It’s about time you switch sceneries—not to mention the situation. This is also for your safety.”

“Thank you!” Yu Rou nodded.

Yuan looked at Bai Lianhua and said, “You know, the Dragon Spiral Mountain has enough room for the entire Eternal Lotuses to relocate. The spiritual energy there is also much denser than here, so you’ll all benefit from being there.”

Bai Lianhua was momentarily stunned by his suggestion. After snapping out of it, she said, “I know it’s incredibly expensive to live there—if you even get approved by its landlord. There’s no way they’d let so many people move there at once.”

Yuan smiled and said, “I happen to be very close with the landlord, so we should have no problem getting you guys settled there. Of course, this is only a suggestion—an offer. You don’t have to take it if you don’t want it.”

Unlike before, Bai Lianhua didn’t immediately reject his offer. Living inside the Dragon Spiral Mountain would not only provide them with security but also benefit their cultivation. However, the most important factor was that she would get to be neighbors with Yuan.

Seeing Bai Lianhua seriously pondering moving her headquarters to Dragon Spiral Mountain, Yuan gave her another push, “You also won’t need to worry about living expenses since that’ll already be taken care of.”

After a moment of silence, Bai Lianhua replied, “Can I give you an answer later? I need to speak with the others first. This decision is too big for me to decide alone.”

Yuan nodded, “Sure.”

The sourc𝗲 of this content is frёeωebɳovel.com.

“Then I will immediately speak with the others.” Bai Lianhua didn’t waste any time and gathered all the faction members before telling them about Yuan’s offer.

“We get to be neighbors with the Demon Sealing Faction and Player Yuan? I agree!”

“I heard that only the elite of elites are allowed to live there! I also agree with our relocation!”

The members were quick to give their opinions on the matter, and everyone agreed to move to the Dragon Spiral Mountain because they would only benefit from it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1583 Controlling Yuan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1583 Controlling Yuan

Chapter 1583 Controlling Yuan

“That was fast.” Yuan smiled when Bai Lianhua returned to the room much faster than he’d anticipated.

“Well… everyone’s eager to move…” Bai Lianhua replied with a bashful smile.

“Then it’s settled. You guys can make the move whenever. I’ll speak with the Lord once I return.”

Bai Lianhua nodded, “Okay. Thank you for giving us this opportunity.”

Yuan left with Yu Rou a few hours later.

As Yuan and Yu Rou made their trip back to the Dragon Spiral Mountain, government officials were already there to speak with the Lord.

However, before they could even reach the Lord, they had to deal with the manager first. Even as government officials, they couldn’t enter the Dragon Spiral Mountain without her approval.

“What is your reason for visiting?” Liya asked them with a nonchalant look on her face.

“We have some urgent matter that requires the Lord’s attention. This world’s balance is in jeopardy, and we believe the Lord is the only individual who can deal with it.”

Despite hearing this information, Liya remained uninterested and said, “I will need more information than that, or are you implying that I don’t qualify enough to hear it?”

The government officials exchanged glances with each other before giving a nod.

One spoke a second later, “We believe a certain individual has too much power and could be a potential time bomb strong enough to destroy this world if not controlled. He’s currently wandering the world unrestricted as we speak, and we’d like the Lord to do something about it.”

“Who is this individual?” Liya narrowed her eyes.

“He used to go by Yu Tian, but he’s known as Yuan right now, and he’s a resident at the Dragon Spiral Mountain.” Liya raised an eyebrow upon hearing this familiar name.

After realizing the situation, Liya couldn’t help but crack a chuckle.

The government officials raised their eyebrows at Liya’s reaction. Although they had met and spoken with her plenty of times, they had only seen her poker face, so it was an incredibly weird feeling to see her laugh, even if it was just slightly.

“Ahem.” Liya cleared her throat and spoke, acting like nothing had happened, “You need the Lord to control this Yuan individual, correct? I’ll lead you to the Lord right now.”

“Already?” The government officials couldn’t believe how fast they gained entrance. Typically, they would have to wait for Liya to contact the Lord before getting a response.

“Don’t tell me the Lord is already aware of the danger Yuan proposes to the world?” One of them speculated as they passed the gates, entering the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

“Maybe that’s why she laughed at us—because they are already working on it.”

Sometime later, these government officials reached the peak of the mountain.

“Wait here for a bit,” Liya said to them before disappearing for a few minutes.

While they waited, Liya went to notify the Lord of their visit.

“Government officials? What do they want?”

“That’s a surprise.” Liya smiled.

“…”

The Lord found Liya’s behavior to be unusual and immediately became suspicious. However, he didn’t question her any further and said, “Bring them to me.”

“Of course.”

Liya left to bring the visitors to him.

A few moments later, the government officials stood before the Lord’s cave with their heads lowered so much their chin was kissing their chest.

“What are you all waiting for? This is an urgent matter, right? Go ahead and tell him what you told me.” Liya spoke up when none of the government officials dared to speak without permission.

This chapter is updat𝓮d by freēwebnovel.com.

The officials felt something was off about Liya—almost like she was entertaining herself with their situation.

“W-we greet the Lord of the Dragon Spiral Mountain. Today, we have an urgent matter to discuss.”

“There is a particular individual we believe requires your attention. This individual possesses unfathomable power that threatens not only the balance of this world but also the world itself. He hasn’t done anything requiring immediate action, but we believe it’s only a matter of time, and at the speed he’s growing, we should move as soon as possible before he becomes truly uncontrollable.”

“This individual is a resident at the Dragon Spiral Mountain. He used to be called Yu Tian but is now widely known as Yuan, and we need you to put a collar on him…”

The moment Yuan’s name was uttered, the atmosphere in the Dragon Spiral Mountain changed, and a dreadful pressure appeared at the peak of the mountain, forcing all of the government officials to kneel on the ground.

“L-Lord?! What is the meaning of this?!” The government officials screamed in horror as the bones in their bodies began cracking.

The pressure disappeared a few seconds later, but the officials remained on the ground with their bodies still screaming from pain.

“I refuse.” The Lord uttered in a cold voice a moment later without explaining any further.

Despite their pain, the officials tried to change his mind.

“Please! You’re the only existence in this world who can control a monster like him!” “The world needs your help, Lord!”

“Why are you refusing to help us?! At least give us an explanation!”

“I will not repeat myself, nor do I owe you any explanation. Now get the hell out of my face before I lose my temper again!” The Lord roared, blowing them away with the force of his voice. “But—”

“The Lord has spoken. Unless you want to leave this mountain as a corpse, I suggest you leave now.” Liya stepped forward and interrupted.

The government officials gritted their teeth in frustration and began descending the mountain. Due to the pain in their body, they had to support each other as they slowly limped away.

“Why didn’t you try to stop them or convince them that Yuan isn’t dangerous?” Liya asked the Lord after the officials left.

“You think they’d listen to me? Humans are foolish and cowardly creatures, especially when their power and control are threatened.” The Lord sighed. “Anyway, this was bound to happen sooner or later. Let’s wait and see how he’ll deal with this.”

Liya silently acknowledged.

chapters.title: Chapter 1584 A Villain
chapters.body:
Chapter 1584 A Villain

Chapter 1584 A Villain

“Fuck! Why did the Lord refuse to help us?!”

“Could he be afraid of Yuan?”

“That’s impossible! The Lord has an unfathomable cultivation! He could easily kill someone like Yuan!”

“Whatever his reason is, we cannot leave without any results. Our supervisors will eat us alive if we return emptyhanded.”

“But what the hell are we supposed to do?”

The government officials stopped moving and decided to sit by the road to think. They were also in too much pain to walk and took this opportunity to heal. After some time, they considered returning to the peak for another attempt to convince the Lord, but no one actually dared, fearing that the Lord would kill them for such audacity.

“There’s nothing we can do here. I’m sure our supervisors will understand. Let’s get out of here before there’s a misunderstanding.”

“Yeah. It’s not like we can argue with the Lord.”

The officials got back on their feet and began descending the mountain again.

Sometime later, they noticed a handsome young man coming up the mountain from a distance.

Despite his youthful appearance, he had a profound aura and exuded the presence of an expert.

“T-that’s—!”

“Look over there!”

“It’s Yuan!” The government officials immediately halted their steps when they recognized the young man to be Yuan—the very person they were trying to put under control.

Yuan noticed these individuals long before they even saw him and had plenty of time to ponder how he should interact with them, if he even should.

Once they were only several feet apart from each other, Yuan stopped in front of them with a friendly smile on his face.

“Judging by your suits, you must be with the government. Did you already finish your discussion with the Lord? How did it go?” he asked.

“W-what are you talking about?” The officials’ eyes widened with surprise, almost as if they couldn’t believe what they were hearing.

Yuan pretended to look confused and said, “Hmm? I thought you were here to see whether you can get the Lord to deal with me or something along that line. Was I mistaken?”

“You!?”

The government officials’ faces darkened after hearing his words. However, they quickly realized the gravity of their situation.

If they respond incorrectly and offend Yuan, it might trigger what they feared could happen.

“I-I think there’s been a misunderstanding. We—our supervisors are not trying to ‘deal’ with you. We just want to make sure you don’t accidentally destroy a city or the world…”

“Wow, that’s quite the assumption there. How did you come up with such a conclusion? Is it because of my performance during the Faction War?”

“That’s…”

Before the officials could respond, Yuan continued, “Or is it because of the Ten Great Families? Are they telling you that I am dangerous and need to be dealt with?”

“Well, it doesn’t really matter. Tell your supervisors that I have no intention of being controlled, nor do I have any plans on overthrowing the government—as long as you know your place and don’t touch my friends and family.”

“In case they don’t want to listen, tell them that what they’d witnessed during the Faction War was only a small fraction of my full strength. If they want to see me prove it, they can go ahead and continue messing with me.”

The government officials trembled upon seeing Yuan’s cold smile. They couldn’t imagine Yuan possessing more power than what they’d already witnessed, but they didn’t doubt him.

Yuan suddenly walked forward until he was standing right in front of them.

“Are you afraid of me?” Yuan placed his right hand on the official’s shoulder and could immediately feel the man trembling in fear.

“There’s no reason to fear me… at least not yet. As long as the government doesn’t overstep its boundaries, I won’t do anything to you guys.”

Yuan gave the man’s shoulder a gentle pat before walking past them and disappearing into the formation.

All of the officials fell to their knees once they could no longer sense Yuan’s presence. Their bodies began sweating profusely, and their legs felt like tofu, almost as though they’d just escaped a near-death situation.

Visit fгee𝑤ebɳoveɭ.cøm for the best novel reading experi𝒆nce.

None of the officials spoke to each other, as they all had their own thoughts to figure out. Sometime later, they stood up and left the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

They would not utter a single word until they returned to their supervisors, informing them of the Lord’s response, as well as Yuan’s warning to them.

Meanwhile, Yuan met with the Lord, who had watched his conversation with the officials.

“You sounded like a villain back there. Are you sure that’s how you want to appear?” The Lord asked him.

Yuan shrugged, “They are the ones who branded me as one first. It wouldn’t have mattered how I acted since I would still be a villain in their eyes.”

“Anyway, I didn’t come here to talk about them. The Eternal Lotuses, who is in an alliance with my Demon Sealing Faction, would like to move their headquarters to the Dragon Spiral Mountain. My sister is there, and I would like to keep her close to me for now. Do you think that’s possible?”

“Of course, that’s possible. Even if we don’t have room, I’ll make sure there’s room. In fact, I have been thinking about clearing out the mountain so it’s exclusively used by you and those close to you.”

Yuan was surprised to hear this and quickly said, “There’s no need to do that for me. What am I going to do with an entire mountain, anyway? I am fine with what I currently have, so just keep things the way they are.”

“I understand.” The Lord nodded.

After chatting a little more, Yuan returned to his residence and contacted Bai Lianhua.

“Yes, I have received the Lord’s approval, so you can move here whenever you want.” “Then we’ll move there in a few days. I’ll see you then.”

“See you.”

Bai Lianhua immediately started the process after hanging up.

chapters.title: Chapter 1585 Releasing The Interview
chapters.body:
Chapter 1585 Releasing The Interview

Chapter 1585 Releasing the Interview

“Who the hell does that arrogant little brat think he is, threatening the government!?” Government agencies around the world were enraged when they were informed of the situation. They couldn’t believe that they were being threatened by someone who barely just reached adulthood. Yet, that wasn’t even the most irritating part.

The fact they couldn’t do anything about it was what truly infuriated them to the core.

The Lord refused to help them for some reason, and Yuan lived inside the Dragon Spiral Mountain, making things even more complicated than they already are.

If they wanted to deal with Yuan, they had to wait until he left the Dragon Spiral Mountain. Otherwise, they risk offending the Lord of the mountain by conducting their business within his sacred land.

The Lord possessed power and authority that far surpassed any other organization or individual in the world. The only saving grace for humanity was the Lord’s indifference toward humans as long as they didn’t mess with him.

Many decades ago, the government tried to subdue the Lord. However, they quickly found out how foolish their actions were when the Lord effortlessly killed everyone responsible without leaving the mountain.

Of course, the government tried nuclear weapons, but those were also rendered useless before they could even get close to the mountain.

After the old government was annihilated by the Lord, a new one formed and signed a peace treaty with the Lord.

The contract stipulated that they would not interfere with the Lord or any activities within Dragon Spiral Mountain, declaring the place an independent power. This meant that the laws governing the majority of the world were irrelevant within Dragon Spiral Mountain, and all business conducted there could not be judged by outside standards.

“I want the Dragon Spiral Mountain to on a 24-hour surviellance! Notify us immediately whenever Yuan leaves!”

The most uptodate nove𝙡s are published on frёewebnoѵel.ƈo๓.

“But what are we going to do once he leaves? Ambush him? We don’t have the power to do anything to him!”

“We can figure that out later! For now, I want everything on him—from what he eats to breakfast to when he shits it out! If we cannot deal with Yuan, we can always target those around him. Contact the Demon Sealing Faction members and try to recruit them to our side. Be cautious and discreet.”

“…”

The entire room went silent after such an impossible task was given.

It was already impossible enough to enter the Dragon Spiral Mountain, so how were they going to spy on Yuan to get information on him?

The entire room went silent after such an impossible task was given.

It was already impossible enough to enter the Dragon Spiral Mountain, so how were they going to spy on Yuan to get information on him?

“I guess we can try to convert someone that’s already close to him on our side, but that has its own risks. What if they feed us incorrect information? What if they expose us?”

“There’s no such thing as risk-free in our line of work. Everything has a risk. The only question is whether or not we’re willing to accept such risks.”

The room fell silent again.

Eventually, they agreed to try to convert someone from the Demon Sealing Faction.

Meanwhile, Yan Xiaoxiao just finished editing her interview with the Demon Sealing Faction and sent a copy to Meifeng to get it approved.

At the Demon Sealing Faction, Meifeng watched the interview with Yu Rou and Yuan together.

“It’s very well made and puts a good spotlight on the Demon Sealing Faction, which will definitely help us when everyone is trying to drag our name through the mud,” Meifeng commented after watching the interview.

“Do you think it’ll stop people from talking badly about my brother?” Yu Rou asked with a slight frown on her face.

“Unlikely, since most of the people speaking ill of Yuan are probably hired actors paid by the Ten Great Families and the government. This is very common practice for those who want to lower another’s reputation.”

“There’s no need to worry about me, Yu Rou. Whether my reputation is good or bad, it won’t change anything.” Yuan said to her.

“I know that, but it’s still frustrating to see people badmouth you every time I open up the internet…” she sighed.

Yuan suddenly placed a hand on her head and ruffled her hair. “Brother?!”

“Don’t waste your energy over something like that. It’s not worth it.” He looked at Meifeng and continued, “Anyway, you can let Yan Xiaoxiao know that she can release the interview whenever she wants.”

Meifeng nodded and immediately sent a message to Yan Xiaoxiao.

Not even a minute later, Yan Xiaoxiao posted the interview on her personal website and social profiles, instantly gathering tens of thousands of views.

In less than half an hour, the interview garnered over a hundred million views, becoming a sensation on the internet.

At first, there were many positive comments, praising the Demon Sealing Faction’s unique lifestyle and the members’ trust in Yuan.

However, once the Ten Great Families and the trolls heard about the interview, they bombarded it with the usual negative comments.

Yet, because these comments were made under Yan Xiaoxiao’s profile, her immense fanbase was immediately enraged, considering it an attack on Yan Xiaoxiao herself.

Thus, the trolls and Yan Xiaoxiao’s fanbase began a war with each other. Later that night, Yu Rou approached Yuan and said, “Let’s sleep together tonight!”

“I don’t mind, but…” Yuan showed his room to her, where another individual was already sleeping on his bed.

“Oh, right. You sleep together with Chu Liuxiang.”

Yu Rou proceeded to stand there in silence, seeming in deep thought.

“I have been wondering about this for a while now, but why isn’t Meixiu sleeping with you, too? Isn’t she also your lover? I don’t think it is fair to make her sleep in a separate room when Chu Liuxiang sleeps with you.”

“Well… it’s not like I am forcing her to be in a separate room. I also don’t want to force her to sleep with us, so I’m just waiting for her to say something.” Yuan said with a bittersweet smile.

“Is that so?” Yu Rou mumbled.

After another moment of silence, she said, “I changed my mind. I’ll go sleep with Meixiu tonight since it’s been a while.”

Yuan nodded, and Yu Rou knocked on Meixiu’s room the following moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 1586 Eternal Lotuses’ Relocation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1586 Eternal Lotuses’ Relocation

Chapter 1586 Eternal Lotuses’ Relocation

Once Meixiu answered the door, Yu Rou greeted her with a bright smile, “Meixiu, it’s been a while since we’ve really spoken with each other! Let’s sleep together tonight.”

Although she was a little surprised, Meixiu agreed with a slight head nod, “Sure. Come inside.”

“Goodnight, brother.” Yu Rou said as she strolled into the room.

“Goodnight.” Meixiu also said.

Th𝗲 most uptodate novels are published on ƒгeewёbnovel.com.

“Goodnight, you two.”

Once the door closed, Yuan returned to his room to sleep, as cultivating was pointless nowadays.

Due to the Celestial Emperor’s curse and his high cultivation base, his cultivation would go nowhere, even if he cultivated for years straight. Of course, he could always consume monster cores, but he had no reason to increase his cultivation on Earth any further. After all, he already had more than enough power to conquer the entire world if he so desired.

Meanwhile, inside Meixiu’s room, Yu Rou muttered as she sat on the bed, “Wow, this is a really comfortable bed.”

“Anyway, how have you been doing, Meixiu? Is my brother treating you right?”

Meixiu nodded with a smile, “I’ve been doing great, and Yuan’s treating me fine, too.” “Really? If that’s the case, why are you still sleeping alone while Chu Liuxiang is sleeping in the same room as him?” Yu Rou looked at her with doubtful eyes.

“I’m satisfied sleeping in my own room, and he does come sleep with me once in a while.”

Although she didn’t mention it, she’d actually heard Yu Rou’s conversation with Yuan outside her room earlier.

“That’s not good enough! We’ve been together since childhood, so you’re like a second sibling to me—an elder sister! I will not allow you to lose to someone else!”

“Lose…? But I am not losing… at least, that’s not how I see it.” Meixiu showed a stiff smile.

After all, she was the first one to be accepted by Yuan. Yu Rou sighed and said, “Although I wasn’t too surprised when I learned that you and my brother paired up since I knew it’d eventually happen, do you have any idea how shocked I was when I found out that he got a second lover?”

“Is it really that surprising?”

“Of course! I didn’t know he was capable of doing that! Anyway, you should start sleeping in the same room as my brother. I know you want to.” Yu Rou winked at her. ƒгeewebnovёl.com

“…”

After a moment of silence, Meixiu spoke in a low voice, “What about you?”

“Huh? What about me?” Yu Rou raised her eyebrows.

“Don’t you also love him?”

Yu Rou was momentarily stunned by such a question, but she quickly snapped out of it and chuckled, “Of course I do. He’s my one and only brother, after all.”

Meixiu gently shook her head.

“You know that’s not what I mean…”

Yu Rou’s body trembled slightly, clearly not expecting Meixiu to question her in such a manner.

“I know, but you should also know that we’re siblings.”

“But you’re not blood-related. That changes everything.”

Yu Rou shook her head and sighed, “While that’s true, it’s our perspective that truly matters. I love him very much, and I’d do anything for him, but I don’t know if I am ready—or will ever be ready—to treat him as anything other than my brother.”

“Is that so…”

Seeing the dejected look on Yu Rou’s face, Meixiu decided to stop talking about this subject.

After a moment of silence, Meixiu sat on the bed and said, “Tell me about what it’s like at the Eternal Lotuses.”

“Sure.”

After closing the light, the two of them lay underneath the blankets, and Yu Rou started recalling her experience at the Eternal Lotuses.

Once Yu Rou was finished, Meixiu spoke about her adventures with Yuan and her progress in Cultivation Online.

They ended up talking all night, only falling asleep once the sun began rising.

Over the next few days, in order to know those around Yuan better, Yu Rou would spend most of her time chatting with the members of the Demon Sealing Faction.

On the third day of Yu Rou’s stay at the Dragon Spiral Mountain, Bai Lianhua and the rest of the Eternal Lotuses were finally ready to move their headquarters.

Early in the morning the following day, Bai Lianhua and the entire Eternal Lotuses showed up at the bottom of the Dragon Spiral Mountain with several trucks full of items, from furniture to decorations.

Yuan went to greet them alone.

“So, how does this work?” Bai Lianhua asked.

“The manager is making her way here now. You can ask her in a bit.”

Liya showed up several minutes later and said, “There are several unoccupied properties. Since your circumstances are unique and we haven’t prepared a place for you, you can go ahead and pick the one you like best.”

“Once you have picked your property, you can start moving your stuff.”

Liya looked at Yuan before handing him a notebook and said, “Since you recommended them, they’re your responsibility. Everything you need to know is inside that notebook.”

Without further explanation, Liya turned around and disappeared.

“Are we really welcome here?” Bai Lianhua swallowed nervously after seeing Liya’s cold behavior. Yuan chuckled and said, “Don’t worry about her. She’s naturally grumpy like that. You’ll get used to it very quickly.”

“I can hear you!” Liya’s angry voice resounded in an echo.

Sometime later, Yuan brought Bai Lianhua and several high-ranking members inside the Dragon Spiral Mountain to look at the available spaces listed in the notebook Liya gave him.

Most of the property in the lower and middle areas of the mountain was already occupied, but there were still plenty of options available. In the lower area, two large mansions remained, while the middle area had three. As for the upper area, five homes were still unoccupied.

“The disparity in spiritual energy between the lower and middle areas is immense. I cannot fathom what it’d be like in the upper areas,” Bai Lianhua remarked as they entered the middle area.

“I never even thought such quality of spiritual energy was possible on Earth…” Another commented.

“This place is a cultivator’s heaven…”

chapters.title: Chapter 1587 Boot Camp
chapters.body:
Chapter 1587 Boot Camp

Chapter 1587 Boot Camp

A few hours have passed since Yuan started showing Bai Lianhua and the others the available properties within Dragon Spiral Mountain. All of the buildings were luxurious manors that were even bigger than Bai Lianhua’s family home.

While the upper area was more spacious, it was the higher spiritual energy quality that truly mattered. In the end, Bai Lianhua chose to live in the upper area while also staying as close as possible to the Demon Sealing Faction’s residence.

“Here is a list of our rules. I doubt you will cause any trouble for the Dragon Spiral Mountain, but it’s better to be safe than sorry.” Yuan handed him a list of rules that was placed inside the notebook.

Bai Lianhua accepted the list and spoke with a serious expression, “Don’t worry, I will make sure we don’t embarrass ourselves.”

Yuan smiled and said, “If you want, I can create a formation around your home that will prevent all commotion from leaving, so you won’t have to worry about being too noisy to your neighbors.”

Bai Lianhua nodded, “That would be great. Thank you.”

Sometime later, Bai Lianhua signed the contract and began sending the trucks up the mountain.

Once all of the items were laid out in front of the house, the members started moving them inside.

Yu Rou showed up to help with the move since she was also part of the faction.

Many hours later, once the moving was finished, the Eternal Lotuses began preparing for a massive feast to celebrate their new home, to which the entire Demon Sealing Faction was invited. “Before we get this feast started, I would like to give a special shoutout to the one who made this all possible—Yuan.” Bai Lianhua gestured for Yuan to go onto the stage, where she was standing.

The feast was held in the training ground, which was the most spacious. The stage they were standing on was the same one they used for sparring in their old location, something they decided to transport to their new home. Once Yuan was on the stage, Bai Lianhua continued, “In three, two, one—”

“Thank you, Yuan!” Everyone from Eternal Lotuses shouted.

“Do you have anything to say to us?” Bai Lianhua asked Yuan once the noises calmed down.

Yuan nodded and said, “Since we’re in an alliance, it’s only natural for us to help each other. Now, getting you all settled near us is only the first part. For the next part, I want to help you all grow stronger, and when I say stronger, I don’t mean give you powerful techniques.”

“I have already spoken to my faction about this, and we want to train you personally. Thus, we will have a 3-day boot camp session at the end of every month, where we will train you to become stronger than all of the other factions.”

“Of course, this boot camp is not mandatory and not for everyone, but for those of you who wish to stand above the rest, it’s your best chance of that.”

The Eternal Lotuses members exchanged glances with each other, their gaze filled with intrigue yet worry.

“Since the month is already ending and you just moved here, we won’t start until next month, but we will give you a one-day trial later so you know what to expect come next month.”

“With that said, let’s enjoy ourselves.”

The feast began shortly after, with everyone stuffing their mouth with food and drinks.

The feast continued into the night and even past midnight.

Once the feast ended and everyone cleaned up the scene, the members of Eternal Lotuses began cultivating in their new environment.

They knew the spiritual energy there was abundant and that their progress would be much faster than before, but when they finally experienced the difference firsthand, they were all profoundly shocked and elated.

“Hahaha! Although it’s not as good as Cultivation Online, this is still multiple times faster than before!”

The most uptodate nove𝙡s are published on frёewebnoѵel.ƈo๓.

“With this much spiritual energy, I should enter Spirit Master in no time!”
