“No wonder why people rush to ascend in Cultivation Online even if they’re severely under-leveled! This is worth the risk!”

None of the members of Eternal Lotuses were able to sleep that night, as if they were addicted to cultivating.

Two days later, Yuan and the rest of the Demon Sealing Faction showed up at the Eternal Lotuses’ residence with weapons in their grasp, looking like they wanted to start a war.

“If you wish to participate in the trial, go to this side. Otherwise, go to this side.” Bai Lianhua instructed her members.

In just a few moments, every member of Eternal Lotuses went to the participating side.

“Good.” Bai Lianhua nodded in approval at their response.

“Now, we’ll divide everyone into groups based on their cultivation base,” Wang Ming instructed as he stepped forward. “Spirit Warriors will be in one group, and Spirit Masters will be in another.”

A few minutes later, four different groups were formed from several thousand people. The first group contained about a thousand Spirit Apprentices, while the second group had around triple that amount of Spirit Warriors.

The third group had only several hundred Spirit Masters, and the last group had around fifty Spirit Grandmasters.

“Alright. Group one and group two will divide into smaller groups and follow me.” Wang Bingbing raised her hand, gesturing for them to follow her.

Hong Xiuquan, Chu Liuxiang, Meixiu, Wang Xiuying, and Xi Murong also followed her.

“I will take group three.” Shi Lang said.

“And group four is with me.” Wang Ming said.

“What about me? Are you going to train me?” Bai Lianhua asked Yuan while pointing at herself.

“No, she’ll be doing the honors.” Yuan gestured to Li Jinxi.

“Hello… You’re Li Jinxi, correct?” Bai Lianhua greeted her with a respectful bow.

“Yes.”

“I’ll be in your care.”

Li Jinxi silently nodded.

“By the way, in addition to the sound concealing formation, I also covered this place with a second formation that’s just like the one used at the Great Battlefield to prevent accidental death, so you don’t need to worry about dying.” Yuan suddenly said.

“I-is that so…?” Bai Lianhua responded with a stiff smile.

chapters.title: Chapter 1588 Boot Camp(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1588 Boot Camp(2)

Chapter 1588 Boot Camp(2)

This chapter is updat𝓮d by freēwebnovel.com.

“P-please! Spare me!”

“You call this training?! This is more like torture!”

The members of Eternal Lotuses quickly realized just how terrifying and outrageously difficult their boot camp was after only a few minutes. They were constantly being beaten and tossed around like ragdolls by the members of the Demon Sealing Faction.

“What’s wrong?! Are you giving up already?! We’ve just started!” Wang Bingbing shouted at them like a drill instructor in military boot camp.

“Did you think it was going to be easy?! If this is too much for you, give up! However, don’t complain if your peers leave you in the dust!”

While not every member of the Demon Sealing Faction screamed, not a single one of them was going easy on the members of Eternal Lotuses.

In less than ten minutes, over ten percent of the participants tapped out, refusing to continue the boot camp trial. It was simply too much for them to handle.

“If you think this is bad, my training is several times worse than what you’re experiencing now. Everything has a price, and this is the price for wanting to get strong fast.” Shi Lang said as he wasted everyone in group three.

Meanwhile, Bai Lianhua was also being tossed around by Li Jinxi.

‘It’s like I am fighting a relentless monster! She’s not giving me any room to breathe, much less attack!’ Bai Lianhua cried inwardly as she was tossed left and right by Li Jinxi’s heavy strikes despite blocking them.

Half an hour later, seventy percent of the participants gave up.

“What do you think? Want to give it a go?” Yuan looked at Yu Rou, who was watching the training with him.

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yu Rou nodded and said, “Yes, but I want you to train me, brother.”

“If you think I’ll go easy on you because you’re my beloved sister…” Yuan showed her a cold smile that sent shivers throughout her entire body.

“That’s not it… nor do I want you to go easy on me. I want you to train me because I want to be trained by the very best, even if it’ll be many times more painful.” Yu Rou spoke with clenched fists.

“As expected of my sister.” Yuan’s smile became warmer, and he continued, “Alright. I’ll train you, but don’t you dare complain to me later about it being too difficult.”

“Don’t look down on me, brother. I am no longer the pampered little princess I was before.” Yu Rou spoke with a serious expression.

“Follow me.” Yuan brought Yu Rou back to his residence since the training ground there was already fully occupied.

“Are you ready?” Yuan asked her once they were at the training ground.

“I am.” Yu Rou nodded.

“Then let’s get this party started.”

In the following minutes, Yu Rou experienced complete hopelessness as Yuan delivered a beatdown she would never be able to forget.

If it weren’t for the protection formation, she would’ve died over a hundred times every few minutes.

Of course, Yuan didn’t just silently beat her. Once Yu Rou started getting used to it, he began correcting her mistakes and giving her opportunities to counter him.

Time felt exceedingly slow for the members of Eternal Lotuses since they were constantly in pain. The trial boot camp only lasted three hours. Yet, by the end, everyone felt like they had been tortured for three days straight.

There were no visible injuries on the participants, but as if their bodies had memorized the pain, they would occasionally feel the sensation of phantom blows striking them even long after the training ended.

“Will this really help us get stronger…? I feel like we’re just being practice dummies…” One of them voiced her concerns.

“What would they gain from lying to us? Let’s just trust in the process.” “I am already exhausted from just three hours… I don’t know if I can endure three whole days of this torture, even if it’s just once a month.”

Sometime later, Bai Lianhua gathered all of her members.

Yuan had to carry Yu Rou back because she was so utterly drained that even the mere act of lifting a finger seemed insurmountable.

“So, what do you all think of the boot camp?” Yuan asked everyone there with a calm smile on his face.

“…”

After a moment of awkward silence, someone responded, “It was really difficult…”

With the floodgates opened, more and more of them started complaining.

“Difficult is an understatement!”

“Are you sure you’re not just torturing us for fun?!”

“How will this help us get stronger?”

After listening to all of the complaints, Yuan spoke, “Since I can only prove its effect with results, let’s do this. If you have endured three sessions of boot camp straight without very clear and satisfying results, I will give you an Ancient-grade technique or treasure as an apology.”

After hearing this, the audience’s faces immediately lit up with excitement. However, once they recalled their experience during the trial, their expressions quickly turned dreadful once more.

Seeing this, Bai Lianhua stepped forward and said, “Once again, participation is not mandatory. However, if you wish to rapidly advance and rise above the crowd, this will be the most effective method. Personally, I will be participating in the boot camp. In the last three hours, I have gained more experience than in the past year.”

“You may not see any improvements right now, but that doesn’t mean you haven’t improved at all.”

“Take your time to think this through. You have plenty of time until the next session,” Yuan told them.

After the gathering, the Demon Sealing Faction returned to their residence while Yuan went to the peak of the mountain to pick up Ji Ran, who had been restoring the Lord’s cultivation for the last few days.

“As always, thank you for the assistance.” The Lord thanked Ji Ran.

“I am only doing my duty.” Ji Ran responded.

Once he returned to Yuan’s Dantian, Ji Ran went into a deep slumber to recover.

“How are you doing in the Nine Heavens?” The Lord suddenly asked Yuan.

“Things are getting complicated, but I’m doing fine otherwise.” “Complicated? How?” The Lord inquired, his interest piqued.

chapters.title: Chapter 1589 Silhouettes (End Of Arc)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1589 Silhouettes (End Of Arc)

Chapter 1589 Silhouettes (End of Arc)

After hearing the Lord’s inquiry, Yuan said, “Well, some people think I am a demon because of my regenerative abilities, and I can’t really blame them, either.” “A demon, huh? That’s quite troublesome.” The Lord mumbled with a frown.

“It’s fine for now since they don’t know my identity, but it won’t stay like that forever.”

“Is that so… How about your progress finding the Dragon Spiral Mountain?” The Lord then asked.

Yuan’s eyes widened after being reminded of the Dragon Spiral Mountain—the one that existed within the Nine Heavens.

“I actually forgot about that…” Yuan said.

In order to travel to the Nine Heavens with his real body, he must find and activate the teleporter, which exists in a second Dragon Spiral Mountain somewhere between the Fourth Heaven and the Seventh Heaven.

“I might need to make a second trip to the Pavilion of Knowledge…” he muttered.

“Even if you find it, I would be surprised if the gate is still functional.” The Lord suddenly said.

“What makes you think that?”

“I would be hard-pressed to believe the Celestial Emperor hadn’t tampered with it since it might affect his plans. After all, we would be able to travel there with our real bodies, and I am not sure if that’s part of the Celestial Emperor’s plans.”

“That makes sense…” Yuan mumbled. “I guess we’ll find out once I go there.”

“By the way, why are there two Dragon Spiral Mountains, and what are their purposes? They were created before the Divine Heaven became the Nine Heavens, correct?”

“There are actually three Dragon Spiral Mountains in existence, and they were created to honor the Dragons of Origins, each representing a Dragon of Origin.”

“The one we’re on right now was heavily damaged during the divide and used to be much bigger and grander. The second one was completely destroyed after a certain incident long before the divide. The third one should be safe, but as I said, I wouldn’t be surprised if the Celestial Emperor did something with it.”

Updat𝓮d from frёewebnoѵēl.com.

“Oh, in case you’re interested, our Dragon Spiral Mountain is meant to honor the Dragon Ancestor. The Great One’s mountain was the one that got demolished. The last one is for Dragon Goddess Yeyou.”

“Anyway, other than being used to honor the Dragons of Origins, they were also used as a means of travel by the beast clans—mostly dragon clans.”

“The Divine Heaven was an unfathomably vast world before the divide, so these teleporters were important for travel back then.”

Yuan nodded and said, “Makes sense. The Nine Heavens is still massive even though it’s split into nine different realms. There are teleport formations in almost every city nowadays, too.”

Sometime later.

“Anyway, I will update you when I find the Dragon Spiral Mountain,” Yuan said to the Lord before returning home.

After dinner, as they walked back to their room, Chu Liuxiang suddenly stopped in front of their room and turned to look at Yuan, who was right behind her.

“Lulu?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“I heard what Yu Rou said a few days ago, you know?” she said.

“About it being unfair to Meixiu that only I get to share a room with you despite both of us being your partner.”

“That’s—”

Yuan tried to respond but was immediately stopped by Chu Liuxiang, “I already spoke with Meixiu about it, and I asked her if she wanted to move in with us. We have plenty of room in the bed, after all.”

“I agreed.” Meixiu’s voice resounded from behind.

Yuan turned around to see Meixiu approaching them with a resolute expression on her slightly rosy face.

“Is that so…” Yuan mumbled with a smile on his face.

“As for my old room, we can use it for… you know…”

“For when we want to do more than just sleep together.” Chu Liuxiang, who was much bolder, finished Meixiu’s sentence.

“Are you okay with that?” Meixiu asked Yuan.

He quickly nodded, “Of course. Why wouldn’t I?”

That night, Meixiu moved most of her belongings into Yuan’s room, and the three of them slept together. Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang decided to continue their training since they were close to a breakthrough.

The next day, they logged into Cultivation Online together.

Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang decided to continue their training since they were close to a breakthrough.

Yuan prepared to revisit the Pavilion of Knowledge to ask about the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

However, before he could go anywhere, a familiar figure appeared before him.

“You… how did you find me?” Yuan asked the tall and bulky figure in front of him.

“As a Commander, I have the location of every faction member below me at my palms,” Kelan calmly explained.

“Did you just come back from your world? I have been waiting here for a few days now.”

“Yes…” Yuan was a little speechless since this was the first time he was greeted in such a manner.

“Since you know I’m from Earth, it’s safe to assume you’re not cursed.” Kelan nodded, “I am from the upper heavens, after all.”

“Anyway, whatchu up to now?”

“Why does it sound like you’ll be following me?” Yuan asked with raised eyebrows.

“Because I will—for your protection. If you aren’t aware, the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto decided to send their top Demon Sealers to hunt you down after hearing about you.”

“They’re moving faster than I’d anticipated…” Yuan mumbled.

“That’s just how dangerous they deemed you…” Kelan sighed.

“Oh well. I’ll deal with it when the time comes. Anyway, do you happen to know a place called the Dragon Spiral Mountain?” Yuan suddenly asked, changing the subject.

Kelan raised an eyebrow and said, “How do you know that place? Aren’t you from the lower heavens?”

“So you do?”

“Of course. It’s a pretty famous place in the Seventh Heaven—sacred, even.”

“I see… so it’s in the Seventh Heaven… I guess I don’t have to worry about it for a while.”

“Why are you asking?” Kelan asked.

“Just curious. I learned about it from the Pavilion of Knowledge.” Yuan said.

“Anyway, you asked what I am going to do now, right? I am going to hunt some magical beasts. Do you know a good hunting spot?” he continued.

Kelan shrugged. “You’re asking the wrong guy. This is my first time in the Fourth Heaven.”

“I thought so. Then I’ll go ask around.”

Meanwhile, somewhere in the Nine Heavens, two silhouettes were chatting away while surrounded by a chaotic energy that would kill even Immortals.

“Did you hear? The Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto have sent the majority of their top Demon Sealers to the lower heavens! This is our chance to make a move!”

“Finally! We have been waiting for this moment for countless years! Once we summon the Ancient, the Demon Clan will rule over this world again!”

“Where’s Demon Emperor Carnage? I haven’t seen him for a while now.”

“I have no clue, but he was uttering some nonsense about the Divine Paragon before disappearing. He’ll show up once we start the summoning ritual.”

“Then let’s begin. I cannot wait to take my revenge on the Demon Sealing Clan.”

“Without the Divine Paragon, they’re just a bunch of nobodies.”

“Even if the Divine Paragon were still here, he’d be no match against the Ancient! Hahaha!”

“Hehehe…”

These two silhouettes soon vanished, their laughter echoing eerily until they were completely gone.

chapters.title: Chapter 1590 Endless Beast Den
chapters.body:
Chapter 1590 Endless Beast Den

Chapter 1590 Endless Beast Den

“A place with a lot of magical beasts, huh?”

An information broker pondered Yuan’s inquiry. After a brief pause, he said, “I know of the perfect place. It’s called the Endless Beast Den, and as the name suggests, an inexhaustible horde of magical beasts constantly emerges from its depths.”

He continued, “Nobody knows why this is happening, but it’s a very famous location for cultivators to train. The only downside is that the magical beasts there never drop cores. That said, there are always people there training all year round.”

“Due to its popularity, unless you belong to a powerful sect or have an influential background, you will have trouble hunting there without being confronted by others.”

“Where is this place located?” Yuan asked.

After receiving the location, Yuan paid the information broker several spirit stones for his services.

“Thank you for your business.” The information broker bowed to Yuan.

“To think the Fourth Heaven would also have a place like this…” Kelan muttered a moment later.

Yuan looked at him and asked, “Is there a place like this in the upper heavens?”

The source of this c𝐨ntent is freeweɓnovēl.coɱ.

“Yes, there are many. Some say the Heavens birthed them, while others claim they come from another world—one that is ruled by magical beasts. Unfortunately, all we know for certain is that no matter how many of them you kill, there will always be more.”

“Sounds like the perfect place for me…” Yuan smiled.

Before they made their way to the Endless Beast Den, Yuan went to pick up Xi Meili, who had been visiting every restaurant in the city. “Oh, you’re back already?” Xi Meili was in the middle of stuffing her mouth with food when Yuan appeared before her.

Yuan smiled and said, “Take your time.” While they waited for Xi Meili to finish her meal, Yuan explained the situation to her.

“I see… the Endless Beast Den, huh? This is my first time hearing about such a place, so this is going to be a new experience,” she said.

“By the way, who’s your new friend?” Xi Meili asked with her gaze on Kelan.

“I’m Kelan, and under the orders of my superiors, I am here to protect him.”

“He’s from the same faction as me, the Celestial Overlords,” Yuan said.

“You need protection?” Xi Meili looked at Yuan with raised eyebrows.

He was powerful enough to kill someone nine realms above his own cultivation. What could possibly threaten him in the Fourth Heaven?

“Because of your friend’s rash and reckless action, he’s now being hunted by the Demon Sealing Clan and the Demon Sealing Grotto, who thinks they’re hunting a powerful demon.”

“How powerful are they?” Xi Meili asked.

“They’re experts that specialize in demons, but they’re also some of the most powerful cultivators in the Nine Heavens since the ones coming after Yuan are top Demon Sealers. However, as long as I am here, they won’t be able to do a thing to Yuan.” Kelan patted his chest with a confident aura around his large body.

“…”

Yuan stared at Kelan with a pondering expression. After a moment of silence, he asked, “I have been wondering about this for a while now, but you’re unusually large. Are you related to giants or something?”

Kelan’s eyes widened with surprise after hearing Yuan’s question, and he immediately responded, “You… you know about giants?”

“Why do you look so surprised? Are they supposed to be a secret or something?” Yuan asked.

“No… but their race went extinct countless years ago, and barely anyone remembers them anymore, even in the upper heavens. You’re also the first one to ask me such a question. Everyone else just assumes my size is due to a unique physique.”

“So you’re a giant…?” “…”

Kelan didn’t immediately respond and appeared to be pondering whether he should tell the truth or not.

Eventually, he sighed, “I’m not a giant, but I do have giant blood flowing through my veins.”

“Huh? How does that work?”

“Just like how some humans inject themselves with the blood of a beast to gain unique powers, I was injected with giant blood,” Kelan said. freёwebnovel.com

One of the main reasons humans hunted dragons in the past was to harvest their powerful bloodlines to use for themselves. “Giants went extinct countless years ago, right? How did you acquire their blood?” Xi Meili asked, her eyes filled with interest, as this was her first time learning about the existence of giants.

“Though they may have gone extinct, remnants of their blood still exist, albeit in extreme rarity. In fact, you’re more likely to encounter dragon blood than giant blood in these times,” Kelan remarked.

“My family happened to have some in their possession, and it was used on me due to my high compatibility.”

“Anyway, enough about me. Tell me more about yourself and your world, Yuan.” Kelan looked at him.

“Sure, but I have one more question. The superior who told you to protect me… was it Senior Bai?”

Kelan nodded, “Yes, but it was also a request from Celestial Overlord Xu.”

“I see…”

Yuan proceeded to introduce himself from scratch.

Sometime later, the three of them left the restaurant and made their way to the Endless Beast Den.

After traveling for several days straight and using multiple teleporters, they finally arrived at the Howling Mountain Valley.

In this region, a dozen mountains pierced the clouds, and loud howling echoed from every direction.

“There’s a lot of magical beasts here, alright,” Kelan said after sweeping the place with his divine sense.

Yuan quickly located the Endless Beast Den with his divine sense and flew straight there.

The Endless Beast Den was a vast cave nestled within one of these mountains, from which magical beasts constantly emerged.

Right outside the Endless Beast Den lay a spacious, suspiciously empty expanse of land. It seemed as if someone had purposefully cleared the area to create a training ground.

Moreover, there were already several hundred people there, all of them engaged in fights with magical beasts.

chapters.title: Chapter 1591 Endless Beast Den(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1591 Endless Beast Den(2)

Chapter 1591 Endless Beast Den(2)

Visit fгee𝑤ebɳoveɭ.cøm for the best novel reading experi𝒆nce.

After arriving at the Endless Beast Den, Yuan silently observed the scenery from the sky. Despite the presence of hundreds of people, most wore similar robes, indicating they belonged to the same sect. The rest exuded a confident and arrogant aura, suggesting they hailed from influential backgrounds.

Most of these people were between the Spirit Lord and Spirit King, with only a few in the Spirit Emperor realm.

There was a single Spirit Sovereign at the scene, but he was not training and appeared to be overseeing the disciples to ensure their safety.

The Spirit Sovereign cultivator turned to look at Yuan once he noticed their presence.

‘First level Spirit Emperor at such a young age. Not to mention that tall man beside him. He must come from an influential background.’ The Spirit Sovereign immediately recognized Kelan as Yuan’s bodyguard due to his unfathomable aura, even though his cultivation was restricted to the peak of Spirit Enlightenment.

“The strongest beast here is only at the peak of Spirit King. What could you possibly gain from fighting them? At your current level of strength, it wouldn’t matter even if you killed a million of them.” Kelan said to Yuan.

Yuan retrieved the Starry Abyss and said, “I am going to strengthen my Soul Weapon.”

Of course, this wasn’t his only reason for being there.

Xi Meili then spoke, “I am also going down there. This will be good training for me.”

She was bored of training alone inside a confined space, and there are some things you can only learn through real experience.

“How long do you plan on staying here?” Kelan asked.

“Who knows? Until I am satisfied, I guess.”

Yuan and Xi Meili descended to the ground and started their hunting the next moment. As for Kelan, he decided to take a look around the Fourth Heaven.

His Divine Sense was powerful enough to cover the entire Fourth Heaven, so he could watch over Yuan no matter where he was. The only reason he decided to follow Yuan around was to chat, but now, with Yuan busy training, he decided to do some exploring. With that said, he made sure not to travel too far.

The area outside the Endless Beast Den was divided into three zones: the outer area, where there were the fewest beasts; the middle area, where the number of beasts was moderate; and the zone closest to the cave, which was filled to the brim with beasts.

Xi Meili went to the outer area to warm up. Meanwhile, Yuan headed straight to the zone closest to the cave. He landed right outside the entrance, where dozens of powerful beasts were gathered.

Yuan’s sudden appearance surprised the few who were training near him. They wanted to call him out, but before they could even open their mouths, Yuan slew all of the magical beasts around him, killing each of them with a single strike.

After snapping out of their daze, they called out to him, “Hey! Who do you think you are? We have this spot reserved for 30 days!”

Without looking at them, Yuan said, “Don’t worry, I’ll leave enough for you guys.”

“That’s not the point! We paid a fortune for this spot!”

Yuan finally turned to look at them and said, “Someone owns this place? Who did you pay?”

“The Three Grand Sects has joint ownership of this place. You’re not allowed to train here unless you’re a disciple of their sect or pay for a spot,” one of them explained.

Hearing this, Yuan looked around and asked, “Are we inside their sect right now?”

“No…”

“Then do they own this land?”

“I don’t think so…”

“So they’re making people pay to hunt wild magical beasts in a territory that they don’t even own. What kind of nonsense is that? Well, I am not going to play by their rules. If they have an issue, they can come to deal with me themselves.

Even as they conversed, magical beasts continued to emerge from the Endless Beast Den.

Yuan returned to killing magical beasts shortly after without giving the others any more attention. fɾeewebnoveℓ.co๓

The Spirit Sovereign overseeing the place frowned when he saw this. He’d initially thought Yuan already had permission to train there, but after hearing their conversation, that was clearly not the case.

And seeing that Kelan had disappeared somewhere, the Spirit Sovereign decided to approach Yuan.

“Young man, if you do not have permission to train here, I must ask you and your friend to leave.”

“And who are you?” Yuan asked without stopping his movement.

“I am Elder Hao of the Grand Spring Sect, one of the Three Grand Sects that holds authority in this area.”

“So what? Are you here to ask me for money?”

Elder Hao sneered and said, “I don’t like your attitude. We don’t need your money. Leave before things become difficult.”

“You’re the one making things difficult here. I am merely hunting magical beasts in the wild. Who are you—the Grand Spring Sect—to monopolize this? If anything, I should be getting paid for this. If you don’t want unauthorized people coming here, why don’t you build a sect around here or something?”

Yuan continued killing magical beasts without stopping as he spoke to Elder Hao, who was already fuming in anger by now.

“You must think you’re invincible because you have an influential background! Unfortunately for you, no matter how influential you are, the Three Grand Sects are still above you! I will give you three seconds to get out of here before I force you!”

Bang!

Yuan suddenly unleashed some of his aura, filling the entire Endless Beast Den with tyrannical pressure.

“You will force me? I’d like to see you try.” Yuan stopped hunting to glare at Elder Hao, who subconsciously started backing away from him.

Despite Yuan’s clearly lower cultivation base, Elder Hao did not dare to underestimate him, as the pressure being exuded by him was stronger than even their Sect Master.

chapters.title: Chapter 1592 Three Grand Sects
chapters.body:
Chapter 1592 Three Grand Sects

Chapter 1592 Three Grand Sects

“Are you trying to pick a fight with the Three Grand Sects? Do you have any idea what kind of trouble you’re getting yourself into?” Elder Hao growled in anger, realizing he had backed away from a junior.

Yuan casually shrugged, “No, not really, since I am not from around here.”

Elder Hao frowned at such words and asked, “Are you… from the upper heavens?”

By now, most of the sects in the lower heavens are aware of people from the upper heavens descending.

Since it was almost impossible for someone of Yuan’s caliber to be unrecognizable, there was a good chance he came from the upper heavens. “The opposite, actually. I came from the Lower Heaven.”

Elder Hao’s face immediately darkened.

“Since you managed to climb all the way to the Fourth Heaven, your abilities and talent are unquestionably up there. However, you’re simply too arrogant if you think you can offend the Three Grand Sects.”

“Since you’re new here, let me educate you. The Three Grand Sects are the strongest and most influential sects in the Fourth Heaven! We have been in this position for millions of years! We are unshakable!” “Thank you for the information, but that doesn’t change anything, unfortunately.” Yuan shrugged.

Elder Hao trembled with anger, enraged that a junior would look down on the Three Grand Sects to such an extent. The Three Grand Sects were highly respected and revered by everyone, and he had never encountered anyone who refused to show them any respect.

Yet, Elder Hao didn’t dare to attack Yuan.

‘I won’t be able to defeat him alone. I need backup.’

Thus, without saying another word, Elder Hao turned and disappeared into the distance. Seeing this, Yuan resumed his training. The others were left speechless by the results, but if Elder Hao couldn’t deal with him, they had no chance. Reluctantly, they returned to their own training.

Over the next several days, Yuan killed thousands of magical beasts. However, that was far from enough to complete his task. At this rate, he’d need to spend over a hundred years at the Endless Beast Den just to complete it.

‘I underestimated this task due to its simplicity… One hundred million… this might actually take the longest to complete…’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

He suddenly turned to look at the cave where all of the magical beasts were emerging from.

Ever since he showed up at the Endless Beast Den, he’d been getting a weird feeling coming from the cave, almost as if it was beckoning him.

However, he’d already examined the place with Divine Sense and Divine Gaze, yet he only found an empty cave. As for the magical beasts, they simply pop into existence like magic.

Suddenly, several powerful presences appeared, filling the entire Endless Beast Den and interrupting Yuan’s thoughts.

Yuan turned around to see four individuals staring at him from the sky. One of them was Elder Hao.

“You left to bring your friends?” Yuan smiled at them.

The group descended to the ground in the next moment, silently glaring at Yuan with profound frowns etched on their faces. These individuals were all in the early Spirit Enlightenment realm.

“You must be the one looking down on our Three Grand Sects. Identify yourself.” The middle-aged man standing at the very front said in a demanding tone.

Yuan tilted his head and said, “I don’t recall looking down on anyone, though. I do think it’s weird you guys are monetizing this place.”

“With an endless number of magical beasts flowing from this cave, if people stopped training here, you’d need to hire people to constantly clean up this place, or it will quickly fill the Fourth Heaven with wild beasts, so everyone training here is actually doing this world a service.”

“Regardless, I believe it should cost nothing to hunt wild magical beasts.”

“I don’t care what you believe. You will regret offending the Three Grand Sects.” The middle-aged man sneered.

Yuan raised an eyebrow and asked, “And how are you going to do that? By ganging up on me? I don’t think it will look good on the Three Grand Sects if people hear that you’d ganged up on a junior.”

“Who said we’re going to gang up on you? I alone am enough to deal with a brat like you,” the middle-aged man said as he released his cultivation.

“Which sect are you from?” Yuan asked.

“Grand Elder Du of the Grand Mystic Sect,” the middle-aged man introduced himself.

“I’m just a nobody from the Lower Heaven. I’ll let you make the first move.” Yuan spoke with a confident smile on his face.

“Arrogant little bastard…” Grand Elder Du’s face immediately flushed red in anger, and his aura surged exponentially.

The most uptodate nove𝙡s are published on frёewebnoѵel.ƈo๓.

“Profound Mystic Strike!” Grand Elder Du extended his palms, shooting a powerful blast of spiritual energy at Yuan.

Seeing this, Yuan placed his arms behind his back and confronted the blast with his naked body. ƒгeeweɓn૦vel.com

Bang!

The ground trembled violently from the impact, reducing the area around Yuan to rubble. Yet, amidst the destruction, Yuan stood unscathed.

“What?!” Grand Elder Du and the others were greatly shocked by the results.

Although Grand Elder Du had no intention to kill Yuan, he definitely wanted to teach Yuan a lesson that he’d never forget.

The attack was strong enough to break most bones in a Spirit Sovereign, yet Yuan came out unscathed while being a Spirit Emperor.

Elder Hao had warned them that Yuan was far more powerful than he appeared, but none of them could have anticipated the extent of his strength.

“I didn’t feel that, so I’ll give you another chance.” Yuan suddenly said.

Yuan’s provocation immediately infuriated Grand Elder Du, causing him to abandon any thought of going easy.

“You’ll regret this, brat!”

“Grand Mystic Typhoon!”

Grand Elder Du created a massive typhoon of spiritual energy around Yuan.

“This is your last chance to apologize because this will really kill you!” Grand Elder Du warned.

Despite Yuan’s arrogant behavior, he admired Yuan’s talents and didn’t want to destroy them in such a fashion.

chapters.title: Chapter 1593 Rift
chapters.body:
Chapter 1593 Rift

Chapter 1593 Rift

“Whoa! Run away! If you’re caught up in that, you’re dead!”

Grand Elder Du’s Grand Mystic Typhoon was strong enough to kill every entity there, and not wanting to get caught up in their mess, the people at the Endless Beast Den began running away.

“We’ve only been here a few days, and he’s already angered someone? He’s really talented in that regard, as well.” Xi Meili chuckled slightly before returning to her training.

She had no doubt that Yuan would be able to handle a few Spirit Sovereigns alone and was not worried for his safety in the slightest.

“Don’t force my hand, young man! This is your final warning!” Grand Elder Du warned Yuan for the final time.

“If you’re that worried for me, why even bother attacking me in the first place? Just get this over with so I can return to my training.” Yuan said, sounding a little bored.

“What a pity!” Grand Elder Du shook his head and increased the rate at which he gathered his spiritual energy.

A few moments later, he released all of that built-up energy, unleashing the technique.

The massive typhoon immediately collapsed on Yuan, consuming him and everything around him. The magical beasts swallowed by the technique were instantly torn to shreds, and their blood scattered everywhere, causing it to rain blood.

Grand Elder Du panted afterward, completely out of spiritual energy after using that one devastating technique.

“A-are you sure it was okay to kill him when we don’t even know his identity? A talent like that surely didn’t come from nowhere. What if he lied about coming from the Lower Heaven and actually came from the Upper Heavens?” one of the other individuals brought by Elder Hao asked in a slightly worried tone.

“I think it’s a little too late to ask those questions now, Grand Elder Lu,” another individual said.

Grand Elder Du nodded and said, “Grand Elder Tang is right. We’re way past that now. I didn’t want to kill him, but he left me no choice.”

“You make it sound like I had a sword against your neck or threatened your life.” A familiar voice suddenly resounded.

“W-what…?”

“T-that’s impossible!” Grand Elder Du exclaimed after hearing Yuan’s voice, shouting so loud that his saliva went everywhere.

Whoosh!

As if cut by a sword, the typhoon suddenly split perfectly in half.

Once the typhoon dispersed, Yuan could be seen standing in his original location without so much as a single blemish on his body.

“Y-you must have used a life-saving treasure! There’s no other explanation!” Grand Elder Du cried out loud, not daring to believe that Yuan had endured his most powerful technique with his bare body.

Without responding, Yuan suddenly took a step forward and disappeared into thin air.

When he reappeared, he was standing right in front of Grand Elder Du with a domineering aura around him.

Before Grand Elder Du could even respond, Yuan swung his arm, backhanding Grand Elder Du in the face and sending him flying.

“?!?!”

Seeing this, the other two and Elder Hao immediately went into a combative stance. Yuan glanced at them and said, “I will only attack those who attack me first, so if you wish for me to attack you, let me know. Otherwise, I’m going to resume my training.”

Grand Elder Lu and Grand Elder Tang exchanged glances with each other. They were all Grand Elders, second to the Sect Masters of the Three Grand Sects. They had never been disrespected in such a manner, much less threatened by a junior.

They were both Spirit Sovereigns, an entire realm above Yuan in terms of cultivation. Logically speaking, they should be able to deal with him without even breaking a sweat. Yet, neither of them felt like they could defeat him.

As for Elder Hao… if he thought he could defeat Yuan, he wouldn’t have requested help from three experts.

Follow current novℯls on ƒгeewёbnovel.com.

“If you’re not going to do anything, I will resume my training now. Don’t worry, I will make sure I don’t bother the others while I am training as long as they don’t bother me, and I will leave as soon as I am satisfied.”

After uttering such words, Yuan turned around and continued hunting magical beasts without a care in the world, leaving the Grand Elders speechless.

“Maybe we should just leave him alone… It’s not like he’s bothering anyone…” Grand Elder Lu mumbled.

“Huh? You want to let him go after he spat on our faces? If we don’t do anything, the Three Grand Sects will become a laughingstock once news begins spreading!” Grand Elder Tang looked at her with wide eyes.

Grand Elder Lu turned to look at Grand Elder Du, who was lying on the ground unconscious several miles away after receiving Yuan’s slap.

“Let’s attack him together! I bet he’s only acting tough!” Grand Elder Tang said with a serious frown on his face.

“But I only came here to spectate…” Grand Elder Lu sighed.

“Do you have any idea how much our images will hurt if we back down now?! Not only will we be forfeiting to a junior, but we even allowed him to disrespect the Three Grand Sects! I’d be surprised if we remain Grand Elders if we leave without doing anything!”

Grand Elder Lu clenched her teeth after hearing Grand Elder Tang’s persuasion, but she couldn’t refute his words.

“Alright… let’s attack him together…” she eventually agreed.

Grand Elder Tang nodded in acknowledgment.

The next moment, their aura began surging when they started gathering spiritual energy for their technique.

“…”

Yuan noticed what they were trying to do, but he simply ignored them and continued his training, acting as if they weren’t even there.

His indifference and blatant lack of respect infuriated the Grand Elders, causing their aura to skyrocket.

The Grand Elders unleashed their techniques simultaneously a moment later, and their combined techniques crashed into Yuan and the Endless Beast Den.

The impact violently shook the ground, causing a powerful earthquake.

Additionally, the mountain with the Endless Beast Den exploded.

“…”

The entire place became dead silent as everyone stared at the destruction with wide eyes. The only individual who wasn’t paying attention was Xi Meili, who was still focused on her target.

Suddenly, a powerful wind with a vacuum effect emerged, drawing in the dust and rubble.

Once all of the dust was gone, everyone could see that a rift had appeared where the Endless Beast Den once existed.

“What is that?” Grand Elder Lu asked.

“No idea… but that wasn’t there a moment ago…” Grand Elder Tang said. “Anyway, where did that brat go? Did we vaporize him?”

“Wait… there’s no more magical beasts appearing…” Elder Hao suddenly noticed this.

“What? Is it because we destroyed the mountain?” Grand Elder Lu immediately began panicking.

“That’s not possible! This isn’t the first time the Endless Beast Den has been blown up, and the beasts continued to appear despite that!” Grand Elder Tang quickly said.

“Yuan…?” Xi Meili suddenly halted her training when she noticed that Yuan’s presence had suddenly disappeared.

When she saw the rift, she immediately thought Yuan had entered it and quickly approached it. However, before she could get close, the rift closed, vanishing into thin air.

chapters.title: Chapter 1594 Yuan’S Disappearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 1594 Yuan’S Disappearance

Chapter 1594 Yuan’s Disappearance

After realizing that Yuan had completely disappeared, Xi Meili immediately transformed and approached Grand Elder Lu, who was stunned by her transformation.

“You bastards! What did you do to him?! Where did you take him?!”

Xi Meili grabbed the stunned Grand Elder Lu by the collar of her robes and roared, disregarding the difference in their cultivation.

“It’s a dragon!” The people there were incredibly excited to see someone from the Dragon Clan there, as dragons were incredibly rare within the Fourth Heaven.

“W-wait! We aren’t responsible for the rift!” Grand Elder Lu exclaimed, clearly nervous about Xi Meili’s identity.

“Bullshit! You were the ones who attacked him!” Xi Meili refused to believe that they were not responsible for Yuan’s disappearance.

“Please let her go. Although we attacked him, we didn’t create that rift.” Grand Elder Tang grabbed Xi Meili’s wrist and spoke with a deep frown.

“I swear on my soul that we didn’t purposefully create that rift! This is the first time I’ve seen or heard of anything like that, too!” Grand Elder Lu shouted.

However, Xi Meili refused to let her go.

Suddenly, another voice resounded, “Let her go. They’re not responsible.” Xi Meili turned around to see Kelan descending toward them.

“Che.” Xi Meili reluctantly released Grand Elder Lu, who quickly stepped back.

“Who are you?” Grand Elder Tang asked Kelan with a worried frown. Just by Kelan’s formidable aura alone, he could tell that he was not someone they could offend.

Kelan, who made a swift return after noticing Yuan’s presence had disappeared, revealed his Faction Symbol and said, “I am Kelan from the faction Celestial Overlords.” “The Celestial Overlords!?” The Grand Elders were greatly bewildered by this information.

Although the Celestial Overlords primarily operated within the Upper Heavens, their reputation and influence were immense enough to reach the Fourth Heavens.

‘What the hell is such a bigshot doing in the Fourth Heaven?! And he’s even a Commander!’ Grand Elder Tang cried inwardly.

As mighty and influential as the Three Grand Sects were, they were no different than ants before the Celestial Overlords. With just a single command, even someone at the rank of Commander like Kelan could wipe out the Three Grand Sects, and nobody would even question them. That was how powerful the Celestial Overlords were.

“This humble junior greets the Commander! Please forgive my earlier disrespect!” Grand Elder Tang quickly bowed, his tone laden with reverence. Grand Elder Lu immediately followed suit, mirroring his deference.

Seeing that Kelan hadn’t responded, Grand Elder Tang quickly continued, “Please let me explain—”

Kelan immediately interrupted, “You don’t need to explain anything. I have been watching from the beginning, so I understand the situation fully.”

“Do you know why Yuan disappeared?! If he was taken by the rift, where did it take him?!” Xi Meili urgently asked Kelan, her voice filled with desperation.

‘That young man has connections with the Celestial Ovelords?! We’re doomed!’ Elder Hao cried inwardly after connecting the dots.

Kelan looked at Xi Meili and responded, “I don’t know where he was taken, but I have some speculations.”

“Although nobody really knows where the magical beasts come from, people have speculated that they come from another world—one that is filled with endless magical beasts. As for why they’re being sent to the Nine Heavens, we don’t have the slightest clue.”

“The rift that took Yuan… I have heard about it before. They rarely appear when an ‘Endless Beast Den’ is destroyed. Many people have entered such a rift to explore the unknown and find out where the magical beasts are coming from, but nobody has returned so far, and people eventually stopped entering them.”

“What?! Nobody has managed to return?! Are you certain about that?! But Yuan—!” Xi Meili didn’t dare to finish her sentence.

Kelan understood what Xi Meili was trying to say and gritted his teeth in anger. He was tasked to protect Yuan and failed miserably so quickly.

“I’ll need to contact my superiors. Maybe they will know what to do…” Kelan eventually said.

“Y-your superiors…?” The Grand Elders swallowed nervously after hearing such words.

The only ones ranking above a Commander are Overlords and Celestial Overlords. Should an individual of such stature descend to the Fourth Heaven, it would undoubtedly provoke an immense commotion.

Foll𝑜w current novels on fɾēewebnσveℓ.com.

“What should I do…?” Xi Meili asked.

“Just stay here and wait for my return. Who knows. Maybe Yuan will return while I am away.” Kelan said before disappearing into the horizon.

“I-I must contact my Sect Leader!” Grand Elder Tang exclaimed, his entire being soaked in sweat.

“M-me, too!” Grand Elder Lu said.

Shortly after the two Grand Elders left the scene, Grand Elder Du woke up from his slumber.

Seeing this, Elder Hao immediately rushed to his side.

“W-what happened to me? Where am I…?” Grand Elder Du looked at his surroundings with a dazed face.

Grand Elder Du quickly came to his senses and jumped up, “Where’s that little bastard?!”

“Grand Elder! We have an emergency!” Elder Hao cried out loud once he reached Grand Elder Du’s side.

“Huh? Elder Hao? What’s the matter?”

“We need to return to the sect and speak with the Sect Leader!”

“Why? What happened?” “I don’t have the time to explain! We need to leave immediately!” Elder Hao didn’t say anything else and flew away.

Grand Elder Du looked around but couldn’t see either Yuan or the other two Grand Elders.

‘They must have dealt with that little bastard and left already.’

With that in mind, he also left the scene shortly after.

The people training at the Endless Beast Den had to stop their training because no more magical beasts spawned, not to mention their situation.

In the blink of an eye, the Endless Beast Den was devoid of people, falling completely silent for the first time in thousands of years.

Meanwhile, after being sucked in by the rift, Yuan found himself in an unfamiliar territory.

“Where…am I?” he muttered in a dazed voice as he surveyed his surroundings.

chapters.title: Chapter 1595 A Foreign World
chapters.body:
Chapter 1595 A Foreign World

Chapter 1595 A Foreign World

After quickly surveying his surroundings, Yuan found himself in the middle of a wilderness filled with oddly shaped, tall trees unlike any he had ever seen before. The sky was an ominous orange with a hint of red, and not a single cloud marred its expanse.

A unique and unmistakable stench of blood filled the air, reminiscent of the battlefield during his time as the Evil God. However, it was not the scent of human blood but that of beasts.

Beastly howls echoed from every direction. In fact, Yuan could sense tens of thousands of magical beasts in the area. Their strength varied greatly, ranging from the level of Spirit Apprentice to the Divine Realm.

“Brother Yuan… where are we?” Xiao Hua came out of his Dantian and asked, her face filled with curiosity and worry. “I have no clue…” Yuan shook his head.

Yu Ning’s voice resounded a moment later, “Young Master… I don’t know where we are, but this place is so massive that my Divine Sense cannot see all of it. Moreover, I could not find a single human being. This place is filled to the brim with magical beasts. Also…”

“…It appears that many powerful magical beasts are quickly approaching our direction, and they all seem frenzied, like starved beasts.” “…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan looked at Xiao Hua and said, “Xiao Hua, return to my Dantian for now.”

She nodded and immediately returned to his Dantian before asking, “What about you?”

“This is just my guess, but I think they are likely drawn by my scent—the scent of a human, a rarity in this place that seems to be filled with magical beasts,” Yuan speculated. fɾeeweɓnѳveɭ.com

“You’re right about that.”

An unfamiliar voice suddenly resounded behind Yuan, startling him.

‘What?!’

Like a startled cat, Yuan instantly leaped away and readied himself for combat. However, his eyebrows arched in surprise upon seeing the individual who had just spoken.

Standing right where he had just been was a little girl who appeared even younger than Xiao Hua. She had pitch-black hair that cascaded down her back like a waterfall, reaching the ground. Her eyes were clear and filled with intrigue. Yet, despite her human appearance, she exuded an aura that felt anything but human. Moreover, she was completely naked.

“W-when did she get there? No… how long has she been there? Even now, despite looking at her with my very eyes, I cannot sense her presence, almost as if I am staring at a ghost,” Yu Ning exclaimed in a shocked voice.

Yuan narrowed his eyes on the little girl. He also failed to sense her presence, even at this very moment. Fortunately, she didn’t appear to be a threat to them for the time being.

“Who are you?” Yuan decided to ask her.

“Are you sure you want to worry about me right now? You’ll be dead in a few moments once the others arrive,” she said.

Yuan gritted his teeth. The magical beasts approaching them were all in the Divine Realm. Yu Ning also warned him of magical beasts at the level of immortals that were too far for his Divine Sense to pick up.

“Since they’re here because of my scent…”

Yuan’s aura suddenly surged, and his body began to transform.

The little girl’s eyes widened with awe as Yuan entered his dragon form.

“You smell like a dragon now, but you had a vastly different scent just moments ago—one that I have never encountered before. Is this what humans are? I have only heard about your species but never met one,” she inquired, her eyes brimming with interest, almost like that of a child visiting the amusement park for the first time.

Hearing her question, Yuan became certain that she was not human.

“Sorry, but I need to leave this place for now…”

Not wanting to wait for the magical beasts to arrive, Yuan used his movement technique and swiftly left the scene.

“…”

The little girl silently stood there.

A few moments later, a red beast resembling a jaguar emerged. Initially, it didn’t notice the little girl, but when it finally laid eyes on her, a terrified expression crossed its face, and its body trembled with immense fear.

Without any hesitation, the jaguar turned around and ran away with its tail tucked between its legs.

This scene repeated several more times for the beasts that showed up at her location until the little girl disappeared into thin air like a ghost. Sometime later, after running for days without stopping, Yuan finally came to a halt.

“This should be far enough,” he mumbled, feeling relieved that none of the magical beasts chased after him.

He lifted his head to look at the sky, which had remained unchanged despite the passage of many days.

“Just where in the world are we…?” he sighed out loud.

“We’re currently at the Enchanted Forest.” A calm and familiar voice responded to Yuan’s sigh.

“What the—?!” Yuan jumped after being startled again.

His eyes widened with surprise after seeing the little girl had managed to follow him.

‘Yu Ning, can you sense her cultivation base…?’ he asked.

‘No… I cannot sense anything about her… It’s like she doesn’t even exist,’ she quickly replied.

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked the little girl, “Enchanted Forest… where is that in the Nine Heavens?”

New novel chapt𝒆rs are published on ƒгeewebnovёl.com.

The little girl slightly tilted her head and said, “Nine Heavens? I don’t know of that place.”

“W-we’re not even in the Nine Heavens…?” Yuan swallowed nervously after learning this information, though he was not entirely certain of its credibility. After all, it was possible that they were still in the Nine Heavens, but the little girl might not be aware of it.

He looked at the little girl and continued asking, “Then do you know what this world is called?”

“What a weird question,” the little girl said. “But I’ll entertain you. This world is called the Primal Expanse.” “Primal Expanse…?” Yuan repeated in a dazed voice.

‘Have you heard of this place? Yu Ning? Xiao Hua?’ he asked them.

‘No…’ they both replied simultaneously.

‘Well, I’ll be damned…’

chapters.title: Chapter 1596 Primal Expanse
chapters.body:
Chapter 1596 Primal Expanse

Chapter 1596 Primal Expanse

After taking a moment to digest the possibility that they had been transported to another world outside the Nine Heavens, Yuan turned to look at the mysterious naked little girl and asked, “Do you mind telling us a little about the Primal Expanse?”

Since the little girl had been answering his questions without any issues, Yuan figured it wouldn’t be an issue to continue asking her questions.

This time, however, she said, “I do mind. It feels like I am being used, and I don’t like it.”

“Sorry… that wasn’t my intention. What do you propose, then?” Yuan quickly apologized to her.

“I want you to answer some of my questions now,” she proposed.

He nodded, “That seems fair. What do you want to know?”

“Where did you come from? Your presence appeared out of thin air.”

“I came from a place called the Nine Heavens. I don’t know how I got here, but I was sucked inside a rift and arrived here before I realized it.”

The little girl took a moment to digest this information before asking the next question, “You’re a human, right? Does this Nine Heavens have a lot of humans?”

“Yes to both questions.”

“Where is the Nine Heavens?”

“Sorry, but I don’t think I can answer that question—not because I don’t want to. Since I don’t know where we currently are, there’s simply no way for me to pinpoint the Nine Heavens.”

The little girl tilted her head in a puzzled manner and said, “But I already told you. We’re at the Enchanted Forest within the Primal Expanse.”

“Well, you see…”

Yuan took a moment to think about how he’d go about explaining the situation to her.

“This is actually my first time hearing about a place called the Primal Expanse, and I am not sure if it’s a place somewhere within the Nine Heavens or outside—like another world. That’s why I wanted to know more about the Primal Expanse, so I can figure that out.”

“I see.” The little girl mumbled.

After a moment of silence, she spoke, “I have never heard of the Nine Heavens, but the Primal Expanse is a world of its own.”

“If you traverse beyond the sky and leave this world, you will only enter an empty, endless dark world we call the ‘Infinite Expanse’.”

Yuan then spoke, “If the Primal Expanse is its own world, it’s safe to assume that it’s separate from the Nine Heavens.”

He wondered if it was a situation similar to Earth and the Nine Heavens, where the Primal Expanse used to be part of Divine Heaven before the split happened.

“So… humans are a rarity in this place, huh?”

The little girl then said, “A rarity is an understatement. Humans essentially don’t exist in this world. According to legends, humans would occasionally appear in our world in the past, but it’s been millions of years since we’ve had a human visitor.”

“So only magical beasts exist within the Primal Expanse?” Yuan asked.

“Magical beasts? What is that?” The little girl asked.

Yuan raised an eyebrow and explained, “In the Nine Heavens, beasts with spiritual energy that lack intellect and only act upon instincts are called Magical Beasts. Then there are Divine Beasts. They are as intelligent as humans and can even transform into one—like you.”

The little girl fell silent again. After a moment of silence, she spoke, “In the Primal Expanse, those who cannot speak and only act upon instincts are called Primals. I am what you call a Predator.”

“…”

Yuan found their wording quite weird, but who was he to judge their world?

“Primal and Predators, right? I will remember that.”

Yuan fell silent afterward and wore a pondering expression on his face.

After connecting the dots, he was certain that the Primal Expanse and the Nine Heavens were connected in some way. After all, the very rift that transported him to the Primal Expanse appeared after the Endless Beast Den was destroyed.

‘It’s very likely the magical beasts that appear out of thin air came from the Primal Expanse. In that case, there must be a way to return to the Nine Heavens.’

He also wondered if Xi Meili had chased after him.

‘Either way, there’s no method to contact her while I am here. In that case…’

Yuan turned to look at the little girl and said, “I will be right back.”

He logged off Cultivation Online the next moment.

When he returned to Earth, both Chu Liuxiang and Meixiu were lying beside him.

He went to see if Meifeng was also in Cultivation Online.

When he couldn’t find Meifeng, he used Divine Sense and immediately located her inside her room with the helmet on.

The others were also inside Cultivation Online.

With no other options, Yuan took out a piece of paper and began writing on it.

Once he was finished, he placed the paper beside the bed and returned to Cultivation Online.

“Where did you go just now? Your presence completely disappeared. How did you do it?”

Yuan was bombarded with questions the moment he returned to Cultivation Online.

‘If she’s questioning it, it’s safe to assume the Celestial Emperor’s curse has no influence here, or her cultivation is simply that strong.’

“I’ll answer your questions, but can you put on some clothes first? Even if you aren’t human, it doesn’t feel right when I look at you.” After all, the little girl looked even younger than Xiao Hua. He hadn’t mentioned it until now because he wasn’t comfortable enough with her to make such demands, as it could offend her, who wasn’t human.

“Clothes? What is that?” she asked.

Yuan pointed at his own robes and said, “These are clothes—something you wear on your body to cover your skin.”

“Oh, those? I don’t have any. And why should I wear them? I don’t want to feel restricted,” she quickly rejected.

Yuan sighed inwardly, ‘I guess I’ll just have to deal with it for now until we part ways.’

Foll𝑜w current novels on fɾēewebnσveℓ.com.

He didn’t want to seem forceful and offend this little girl who was definitely much stronger than she appeared.

“By the way, if humans don’t exist and you’ve never encountered one, why are you in the form of one?” Yuan asked a question that had been on his mind for some time now.

“It’s a popular thing to do amongst Predators, but I only took this form shortly after your appearance since I figured it would be easier to have a conversation with you,” she said.

‘Is this why she somewhat resembles Xiao Hua…?’ Yuan wondered inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1597 Weird Spiritual Energy
chapters.body:
Chapter 1597 Weird Spiritual Energy

Chapter 1597 Weird Spiritual Energy

“By the way, my name’s Yuan. What should I call you?” Yuan said suddenly, recalling that he hadn’t introduced himself.

“You can call me Yingzi,” she said.

‘So they also have names here, huh?’ Yuan mused to himself.

Given that names are primarily a human custom, he assumed that entities in this world might not follow such practices.

However, after thinking about it for a moment, he realized it wasn’t that weird for them to have names since some of them also wear clothes.

‘They must have learned a thing or two from the humans who have traversed this world in the past.’

“Do you know what happened to the humans that came to this world before?” Yuan decided to ask.

“Most of them were killed and consumed,” Yingzi responded without an ounce of pity in her tone.

She continued, “As for those that survived, they were enslaved by Predators and studied.”

Yuan shuddered at this information.

“So there’s a possibility that some humans may still exist in this world?” he inquired.

Yingzi pondered for a moment before nodding, “It’s possible, but very unlikely. The last human to appear was several million years ago, and humans cannot live that long.”

“If they’re an Immortal, they should be able to survive that long, though,” Yuan said.

However, before Yingzi even responded, Xiao Hua’s voice resounded inside his head, “B-Brother Yuan! The spiritual energy in this place is very weird!”

“What?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

Yu Ning’s voice followed: “I can’t believe we haven’t noticed this until now. While the spiritual energy in this world may seem normal at a glance, it is actually incredibly difficult to absorb!”

“…”

Yuan also hadn’t noticed this change since their bizarre situation had completely occupied his mind. If Xiao Hua hadn’t mentioned it, he probably wouldn’t have noticed until much later.

However, when Yuan tried to absorb the spiritual energy around him to experiment, he didn’t notice any difference.

“Huh? Are you sure? I am absorbing it just fine, nor do I feel any difference when compared to the Nine Heavens.”

“Seriously…? But when I try to absorb it, I would only get about 1 percent of what I should… It’s like trying to breathe while being choked.” Yu Ning tried to describe her own experience.

“It’s the same for me.” Xiao Hua said.

Perhaps this was the real reason why Yuan hadn’t noticed anything. After all, he was absorbing the spiritual energy there just fine.

‘If the others were also having trouble absorbing the spiritual energy in this place, even Immortals would slowly perish from a lack of spiritual energy…’

Yuan quickly understood why Yingzi claimed that humans couldn’t live for so long. However, he couldn’t figure out why he was able to absorb the spiritual energy there without any issues.

“Hey.” Yingzi suddenly called out to him.

“Yes?”

“What does it mean to be human?” she asked, her eyes flickering with intrigue.

“Uhh… that’s a really difficult question to answer… If I were to ask you what it means to be a Predator, how would you respond?”

Yingzi pondered for a moment before responding, “I don’t know.”

Yuan then said, “The question of what it means to be human is deeply philosophical. I can’t define what it means to be human universally since everyone differs. Whether it be our culture, morals, ethics, emotions, and even biology, none of that really defines us as human. If anything, I’d say it’s our soul that makes us human.”

New n𝙤vel chapters are published on freeweɓnøvel.com.

“Your soul…? I don’t understand. I also have a soul, but I am not human,” said Yingzi.

“I guess it’d be more accurate to say the shape of our soul? I don’t know.” Yuan shrugged.

Yingzi fell silent again.

‘What’s she thinking now?’ Yuan wondered.

A few moments later, Yingzi suddenly wore a look of enlightenment on her face, and she spoke, “Then I’ll observe you until I understand what it means to be human.”

“You want to observe me…?” Yuan had a bad feeling about this.

‘Does this mean she’ll be following me as I explore this world?’

He wanted to politely refuse, but he had a feeling that she wouldn’t care about what he had to say and would still follow him regardless. Moreover, he risked offending her by refusing her company, which might get him killed.

‘Maybe this won’t be as bad as I am imagining. She’s been a great help thus far,’ he thought, trying to be positive about the situation.

If it weren’t for Yingzi, he probably would still be trying to figure out his location. freewebnoveℓ.com

He spoke a moment later, “If you want to understand what it’s like to be human, you can start by wearing clothes. All of the humans in my world wear clothes throughout the day.”

“…”

Yingzi frowned slightly at his suggestion, but it made sense.

“I don’t have any clothes,” she reminded him.

‘Xiao Hua, do you have extra clothes?’ he asked her since they had a similar build.

Xiao Hua came out of his Dantian for a split second to hand him a pair of robes before quickly returning to his body to avoid attracting the beasts there with her scent.

However, even though it was only for a split second, the Primals nearby had already noticed Xiao Hua’s scent and were rushing in their direction.

Seeing this, Yuan quickly handed the clothes to Yingzi and said, “Here, you can wear this. Come find me when you’re done.”

Without explaining further, Yuan turned around and left the scene.

“…”

Yingzi stared at the black robes in her grasp with a profound expression on her face.

After spending several minutes staring at it, she reluctantly started wearing it.

While Yingzi struggled to figure out how to wear the clothes, numerous Primals arrived at the scene, drawn by Xiao Hua’s lingering scent. However, their excitement quickly turned to terror as they noticed Yingzi’s presence, and they swiftly departed.

Once she had managed to dress herself, Yingzi looked in the direction Yuan had gone and began chasing after him.
